Business justification: Channel-level revenue recognition: each listing channel posts revenue to a specific GL account. E-commerce finance teams run channel P&L reports requiring listing→GL linkage for marketplace fee vs. di']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the seller or merchant account that owns this listing. Relevant for marketplace channels where multiple sellers can list the same SKU. For first-party (1P) listings, this references the company\'s own seller account.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master SKU in the product catalog that this listing represents.']
  age_restriction varchar [note: 'The minimum age required to purchase this product, if applicable. Common values: 18 (alcohol, tobacco), 21 (certain regulated products), 13 (COPPA compliance for digital products). Null indicates no age restriction.']
  average_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'The average customer rating for this listing on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00. Aggregated from customer reviews and used to calculate listing quality score, influence buy box eligibility, and impact conversion rate. Null if no reviews exist.']
  buy_box_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this listing is eligible to compete for the buy box on marketplace channels. The buy box is the prominent \'Add to Cart\' section on a product page. Eligibility is determined by factors such as price competitiveness, fulfillment method, seller performance metrics, and inventory availability.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'The sales channel where this product listing is published. Examples: WEB (website), MOBILE (mobile app), MARKETPLACE (third-party marketplace), B2B (business-to-business portal), BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), SOCIAL (social commerce platform).']
  channel_listing_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier assigned by the sales channel for this listing. For Amazon-style marketplaces, this would be the ASIN (Amazon Standard Identification Number). For other channels, this is the channel\'s native product identifier.']
  compare_at_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The original or suggested retail price shown for comparison purposes to highlight discounts. Used to calculate savings percentage displayed on PDP and Product Listing Page (PLP). Null if no comparison pricing is used.']
  condition_type varchar [note: 'The physical condition of the product being sold through this listing. New: brand new in original packaging; Refurbished: restored to working condition; Used: previously owned; Open Box: new but packaging opened; Damaged: functional but with cosmetic or minor defects.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this product listing record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) and GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the listing price. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CAD.']
  ean_code varchar [note: 'The 13-digit European Article Number barcode identifier for the product. Commonly used in international markets. Equivalent to UPC but with an additional digit for country code.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when this listing will be or was deactivated and removed from the channel. Null indicates no planned end date. Used for seasonal products, limited-time offers, or product lifecycle management.']
  featured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this listing is featured or promoted on the channel\'s homepage, category pages, or marketing campaigns. Featured listings receive higher visibility and priority in search and recommendation algorithms.']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'The fulfillment strategy for orders placed through this listing. FBM: Fulfilled By Merchant (seller ships); FBA: Fulfillment By Amazon-style (marketplace fulfillment center ships); 3PL: Third-Party Logistics provider; DROPSHIP: direct from manufacturer; BOPIS: Buy Online Pick Up In Store.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling, labeling, and shipping restrictions. Examples include batteries, flammable liquids, aerosols, and chemicals. Compliance with Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) and transportation regulations is required.']
  inventory_quantity varchar [note: 'The current available inventory quantity allocated to this listing on the channel. This may be a subset of total warehouse inventory based on channel allocation rules and safety stock policies. Used to determine Out of Stock (OOS) status and listing suppression.']
  last_sold_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent order placed for this listing. Used to identify slow-moving inventory, calculate Daily Sales Rate (DSR), and trigger automated repricing or suppression rules.']
  listing_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the product listing on the channel. Active: live and purchasable; Suppressed: hidden due to quality or compliance issues; Pending: awaiting approval or activation; Inactive: manually disabled; Suspended: temporarily blocked; Archived: permanently removed.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturer\'s unique part number for the product. Used for product identification, catalog matching, and compliance reporting. Distinct from SKU, UPC, and EAN.']
  maximum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'The maximum number of units a customer can purchase in a single order for this listing. Used to prevent overselling, manage high-demand products, or enforce promotional limits. Null indicates no maximum limit.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum number of units a customer must purchase in a single order for this listing. Commonly used in B2B channels or bulk purchase scenarios. Null or 1 for standard retail listings.']
  price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The current selling price for this product listing on the channel, in the channel\'s default currency. This is the price displayed to customers and may differ from the master catalog price due to channel-specific pricing strategies, promotions, or marketplace fees.']
  promotional_badge varchar [note: 'A marketing badge or label displayed on the listing to highlight special offers or features. Examples: \'Best Seller\', \'Limited Time Offer\', \'Free Shipping\', \'New Arrival\', \'Exclusive\'. Used to increase Click-Through Rate (CTR) and Conversion Rate (CVR).']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A calculated score (0-100) representing the overall quality of the listing based on factors such as content completeness, image quality, keyword optimization, customer reviews, conversion rate (CVR), and compliance with channel guidelines. Higher scores improve search ranking and buy box eligibility.']
  review_count varchar [note: 'The total number of customer reviews submitted for this listing. Higher review counts increase customer trust and improve search ranking. Used in conjunction with average rating for social proof.']
  search_keywords varchar [note: 'Channel-specific search keywords and tags used to optimize product discoverability in the channel\'s search and recommendation engine. Supports SEO (Search Engine Optimization) and SEM (Search Engine Marketing) strategies. Comma-separated or space-separated list.']
  shipping_dimensions varchar [note: 'The package dimensions in format \'Length x Width x Height\' with unit (e.g., \'30x20x10 cm\'). Used for shipping cost calculation, warehouse space planning, and carrier compatibility checks.']
  shipping_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The total weight of the product including packaging, measured in kilograms. Used for shipping cost calculation, carrier selection, and logistics planning by the Transportation Management System (TMS).']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when this listing was first activated and made available for purchase on the channel. Used for lifecycle tracking and performance analysis.']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the listing status is \'suppressed\'. Common reasons include: missing required attributes, policy violations, quality issues, pricing errors, inventory unavailability, restricted product category, or failed compliance checks (e.g., CPSC safety standards, PCI DSS for payment-related products).']
  total_units_sold varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of units sold through this listing since activation. Used for Best Seller Rank (BSR) calculation, inventory forecasting, and performance reporting.']
  upc_code varchar [note: 'The 12-digit Universal Product Code barcode identifier for the product. Required for many retail and marketplace channels. Used for inventory tracking, point-of-sale scanning, and catalog integration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this product listing record was last modified. Tracks changes to listing attributes such as price, status, inventory, or content. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and synchronization with downstream systems.']

  Note: 'Represents a marketplace or channel-specific product listing that maps a SKU to a specific sales channel (website, mobile app, marketplace, B2B portal, BOPIS). Captures SKU reference, channel code, channel-specific listing ID (e.g., ASIN for Amazon-style marketplace), listing status (active, suppressed, pending, inactive), listing title override, listing description override, listing price, buy box eligibility flag, listing quality score, suppression reason, and listing created/updated timestamps. Distinct from catalog_item as it models channel-specific presentation and eligibility.'
}

Table product.category_attribute_scope {
  category_attribute_scope_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the category_attribute_scope association']
  attribute_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the attribute definition that is scoped to this category']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the category to which this attribute definition is assigned']
  applicable_category_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product category codes or taxonomy nodes to which this attribute applies. Defines the scope of attribute applicability across the catalog. [Moved from attribute_definition: This denormalized STRING field stores a comma-separated list of category codes representing the M:N relationship. It must be removed from attribute_definition and replaced by proper FK records in this association table, enabling per-category overrides, efficient querying, and lifecycle management.]']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Category-specific sort order for rendering this attribute on PDP and PLP pages within this category context, overriding the global display_order on attribute_definition.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this category-attribute scope assignment is retired. Null for currently active assignments. Enables lifecycle management of individual scopes without affecting other category assignments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this category-attribute scope assignment becomes active and the attribute begins applying to products in this category.']
  is_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this attribute is mandatory for products in this specific category, overriding the global is_required flag on attribute_definition. Enables category-level completeness rules.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the schema governance contract between attribute_definition and category in the PIM system. It captures which attributes are assigned to which categories, along with per-scope overrides for requiredness, display ordering, and lifecycle dates. Each record defines one attribute\'s applicability to one category, enabling merchandising teams to manage catalog completeness rules at the category level. Replaces the denormalized applicable_category_scope STRING field on attribute_definition.. Existence Justification: In PIM systems, merchandising and catalog governance teams explicitly manage which attributes apply to which categories — this is a core operational function called \'attribute scope assignment\' or \'category attribute schema\'. An attribute_definition (e.g., \'Screen Size\') applies to multiple categories (e.g., Smartphones, Tablets, Monitors), and a category (e.g., Electronics > Smartphones) requires many attribute definitions (Screen Size, Battery Life, OS, RAM). This is not derivable from transactional data — it is a schema governance record that humans actively create, update, and retire.'
}

Table order.cart {
  cart_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cart (primary key).']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Abandoned cart recovery and cart-creation attribution require linking each cart to the originating campaign (email, paid ad). Enables campaign-level cart conversion rate reporting and marketing ROI an']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Cart channel attribution (email, social, paid search, organic) drives channel-level conversion funnel analysis. The existing plain-text \'channel\' column is a denormalized string; replacing it with cha']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: In B2B e-commerce, a specific contact (buyer) initiates and owns a cart. This FK enables contact-level purchase attribution, approval workflow routing, and B2B procurement reporting — standard B2B mar']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the cart.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.header. Business justification: When a cart is converted to a confirmed order, the resulting header record should be traceable from the originating cart. This FK enables cart-to-order conversion rate (CVR) analysis, abandoned cart r']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: CART PRICING CONTEXT: The cart calculates totals using a selected price list; persisting the list ID enables seamless order conversion.']
  shipping_customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: Cart currently stores shipping address as denormalized plain columns violating 3NF. Linking to customer_address enables address pre-fill from saved addresses, address validation during checkout, and c']
  wishlist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.wishlist. Business justification: Wishlist-to-cart conversion is a primary e-commerce funnel metric. Linking cart to the originating wishlist enables conversion rate reporting, abandoned cart recovery campaigns tied to wishlist intent']
  abandonment_email_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cart abandonment email has been sent.']
  abandonment_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason captured (if any) for why the cart was abandoned.']
  cart_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing cart identifier shown to customers and used in support.']
  cart_source varchar [note: 'System or integration source that created the cart.']
  cart_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cart.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Promotional coupon applied to the cart, if any.']
  coupon_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the coupon discount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cart was initially created (business event time).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used to create the cart.']
  discount_total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate discount amount from coupons, promotions, and cart-level discounts.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Projected delivery date based on shipping method and inventory.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cart is scheduled to expire if not converted.']
  gift_message varchar [note: 'Optional message entered by the shopper for the gift recipient.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client at cart creation.']
  is_gift boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cart is marked as a gift purchase.']
  item_count varchar [note: 'Count of unique product SKUs in the cart.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent interaction with the cart.']
  loyalty_points_applied varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty points redeemed against the cart.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cart record was first persisted in the data lake (audit).']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cart record (audit).']
  saved_for_later_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the cart is marked as saved for later purchase.']
  shipping_fee_estimated_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated shipping cost for the cart.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of line-item prices before discounts, taxes, and shipping.']
  tax_estimated_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated tax calculated for the cart based on jurisdiction.']
  total_estimated_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final estimated amount payable after discounts, tax, and shipping.']
  total_quantity varchar [note: 'Sum of quantities across all cart items.']
  total_volume_cm3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of all items, used for logistics planning.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all items in the cart, used for shipping calculations.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser or app user-agent string for the session.']
  version varchar [note: 'Optimistic concurrency version number for the cart record.']

  Note: 'Represents a customers active shopping cart prior to order submission. Captures session context, channel origin (B2C, B2B, marketplace, mobile app), cart creation and expiry timestamps, applied coupon codes, estimated totals, saved-for-later flag, and cart abandonment signals (last activity timestamp, abandonment email trigger status). Serves as the SSOT for pre-purchase intent and cart-to-order conversion tracking. This is the entry point of the order lifecycle — cart submission triggers order_header creation. Line-item details are held in the related cart_item product.'
}

Table order.cart_item {
  cart_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cart line item.']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bundle to which this line belongs.']
  cart_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shopping cart that contains this item.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: When a buyer adds a marketplace listing to cart, the specific listing (seller, price, condition, buy-box winner) must be captured for price-lock integrity, listing-level conversion analytics, and aban']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Price audit and abandoned cart analysis require knowing which specific price_list_item was presented to the customer. Enables detection of price changes between cart creation and checkout, and support']
  primary_cart_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Required for real‑time inventory, pricing and fulfillment; cart item must map to the definitive SKU record used in downstream order processing.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace seller offering the product.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Real-time cart availability check: when a customer adds an item, the system queries stock_position for ATP qty, backorder status, and lot availability. E-commerce experts expect cart_item to reference']
  added_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the item was added to the cart.']
  backorder_estimate_date date [note: 'Estimated date when the back‑ordered item will be available.']
  coupon_applied boolean [note: 'True if a coupon was applied to this line.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Code of the coupon applied, if any.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used during the add‑to‑cart action.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to this line item.']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Chosen shipping or pickup method for the item.']
  gift_message varchar [note: 'Optional message entered for the gift recipient.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client at the time of add‑to‑cart.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'True if the item is currently back‑ordered.']
  is_bundle boolean [note: 'True if the item is part of a product bundle.']
  is_gift boolean [note: 'True if the item is marked as a gift.']
  is_preorder boolean [note: 'True if the item is a preorder.']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'True if the item is part of a promotion.']
  is_tax_exempt boolean [note: 'True if the line is exempt from tax.']
  item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cart line.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to this cart line.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the item within the cart.']
  line_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final net amount for the line after discounts and tax.']
  preorder_release_date date [note: 'Release date when the preorder item becomes available.']
  price_after_discount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price after discount but before tax.']
  price_before_discount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Original list price of the line before any promotional discounts.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Code of the promotion applied to this line, if any.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the product requested in the cart.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Marketing source that drove the user to add the item.']
  shipping_estimate_date date [note: 'Projected date the item will be shipped to the customer.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the item was added to the cart.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax charged on this line item.']
  tax_exemption_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the reason for tax exemption.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price per single unit at the moment the item was added to the cart.']

  Note: 'Individual line-level SKU entries within a shopping cart. Tracks SKU identifier, quantity requested, unit price at time of add-to-cart, seller ID for marketplace items, product variant details, and item-level promotional flags. Supports cart-to-order conversion analysis and CVR optimization.'
}

Table order.header {
  header_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for header']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for B2B order processing: links each order to the corporate account used for billing and credit checks, enabling account‑level revenue and risk reporting.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: Required for Audience Segment ROI analysis: associates each order with the targeted audience segment, supporting segment‑level conversion and spend efficiency reports.']
  billing_address_order_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the address used for billing purposes.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Campaign‑Order Performance tracking: directly ties an order to the originating marketing campaign (e.g., email or ad) for conversion and revenue attribution.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Required for Order‑Channel Attribution Report: links each order to the marketing channel that drove it, enabling ROI calculations and channel performance dashboards.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Captures the B2B contact person responsible for the order, essential for order support, invoicing, and compliance audit trails.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: REQUIRED: Allocate order fulfillment costs to a cost center for expense tracking and budgeting.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_profile. Business justification: Guest checkout orders have no account_id but must still reference a customer profile for order history, GDPR data subject requests, and post-purchase communications. A direct header→customer_profile F']
  fulfillment_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or fulfillment center assigned to the order.']
  header_order_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the address where the order will be shipped.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: In multi-entity e-commerce operations, each order must be attributed to a legal entity for revenue recognition, intercompany accounting, VAT filing, and regulatory reporting. A finance domain expert w']
  loyalty_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.loyalty_enrollment. Business justification: Loyalty points accrual requires knowing the exact enrollment active at order placement — not just the program. This FK drives points-earn calculations, tier qualification spend tracking, and loyalty a']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Orders must be attributed to a specific marketplace for GMV reporting, marketplace-level SLA compliance, regulatory jurisdiction enforcement, and seller fee structures. order_source is a plain-text de']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.merchant_account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Reconciliation reports need the merchant account that processed each order; linking order_header to merchant_account enables accurate financial statements and audit trails.']
  payment_gateway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.gateway. Business justification: REQUIRED: Operational dashboards track which payment gateway handled each order for SLA monitoring and compliance; a FK from order_header to gateway provides this linkage.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: REQUIRED: Storing the tokenized payment method used for an order enables refunds, recurring billing, and fraud analysis; the FK links order_header to payment_method.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: ORDER PRICING AUDIT: Each order is priced using a specific price list; storing price_list_id enables price source traceability for compliance reports.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: REGIONAL PRICING: Orders are assigned to a price zone to apply region‑specific pricing rules; FK required for zone‑based revenue analysis.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: REQUIRED: Assign each order to a profit center for profit‑center performance reporting.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Needed for segment‑based order analytics and targeted marketing, allowing each order to be attributed to the customer segment at time of purchase.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order header record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency identifier for the order.']
  delivery_actual_date date [note: 'Date the order was actually delivered to the customer.']
  delivery_estimated_date date [note: 'Projected date for order delivery based on SLA.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to place the order.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total promotional discounts applied to the order.']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Risk score assigned by fraud detection engine.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal operational notes about the order, visible to staff only.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the client at order submission.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'True if any line items are on backorder at order creation.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order requires expedited handling.']
  is_gift boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is a gift purchase.']
  is_split_shipment boolean [note: 'True if the order will be fulfilled in multiple shipments.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes and discounts.']
  order_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by the customer or support staff.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable order identifier used in customer communications and external systems.']
  order_placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment the order was submitted by the customer.']
  order_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level used for fulfillment sequencing.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the order.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Logical classification of the order (e.g., standard purchase, gift, subscription).']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier returned by the payment gateway.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the payment.']
  promo_code varchar [note: 'Code of any promotion applied to the order.']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual fulfillment time in minutes measured for the order.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed fulfillment time in minutes for the SLA tier.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service‑level agreement tier promised for the order.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax applied to the order.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order is exempt from tax.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for tax exemption, if applicable.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the order before taxes, discounts, or fees.']
  total_volume_cm3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate volume of all items in the order, expressed in cubic centimeters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all items in the order, expressed in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the order header.']

  Note: 'The authoritative master record for a confirmed customer order within the OMS. Captures the full order header including order number, channel (B2C, B2B, marketplace, BOPIS, DTC), order placement timestamp, customer reference, billing address, total GMV, AOV contribution, tax amount, discount amount, currency, order status, SLA tier, and payment reference. This is the SSOT for order-level identity and lifecycle management across all channels.'
}

Table order.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order_order_line data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: When a bundle is purchased, the order_line must reference the bundle for bundle-level return policy enforcement, fulfillment splitting, revenue attribution, and discount validation. cart_item already ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Sponsored product and marketplace ad attribution requires campaign-level tracking at the line item — different SKUs in one order may be promoted by different campaigns. Enables product-level ROAS and ']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Carrier assignment per order line is required for carrier performance reporting and SLA compliance; e‑commerce ops track carrier_id for each line.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Each order line is fulfilled via a specific carrier service governing transit time, cost, and SLA. The existing shipping_method is a denormalized text field; a proper FK enables line-level shipping co']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Link enables compliance, merchandising and sales reporting by tying each order line to its master catalog item record.']
  commission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.commission. Business justification: Each order line must reference the exact commission record applied at transaction time for commission auditing, seller dispute resolution, and accurate payout calculation. Commission rates change over']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: REQUIRED: Track line‑level cost allocation to cost centers for detailed expense reporting.']
  dynamic_price_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.dynamic_price_rule. Business justification: DYNAMIC PRICING LOG: When a dynamic pricing rule adjusts a line, storing the rule ID supports performance reporting and rule‑impact analysis.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: REQUIRED: Directly map each order line to a GL account for precise accounting postings.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent order header.']
  markdown_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.markdown_schedule. Business justification: DISCOUNT AUDIT: A markdown schedule may be applied to a line; linking enables tracking of schedule source for discount compliance.']
  po_line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line_item. Business justification: Drop-ship and vendor-fulfilled order reconciliation requires matching each order line to its specific PO line item for three-way match (ordered qty vs PO line qty vs received qty), price variance anal']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: LINE PRICE SOURCE: Order line price is calculated from a concrete price‑list item; FK allows audit of which item supplied the unit price.']
  product_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_listing. Business justification: Order lines are fulfilled against a specific channel listing capturing price, condition, seller, and channel at purchase time. This link enables channel-level sales reporting, seller commission calcul']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: REQUIRED: Attribute each order line to a profit center for granular margin analysis.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Drop‑ship fulfillment creates a PO per marketplace order line; linking enables PO tracking, cost allocation, and delivery ETA reporting.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace seller offering the product.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Order line needs a stable reference to the SKU entity for shipping, tax calculation and returns; replaces the denormalized sku string.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: PICK PLAN: Mapping each order line to a specific stock position is required for the warehouse picking system to generate accurate pick lists and optimize routing.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Drop-ship fulfillment routing, supplier performance attribution per order line, and returns routing to the correct supplier all require direct supplier identification on the order line. E-commerce pla']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or fulfillment node assigned to this line.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the line was actually delivered to the customer.']
  asin varchar [note: 'Marketplace‑specific product identifier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  dimensions_cm varchar [note: 'Package dimensions formatted as LxWxH in centimeters.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Absolute discount applied to this line.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to this line.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the fulfillment process for this line.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the line is fulfilled.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product is classified as hazardous.']
  is_backordered boolean [note: 'True if the line is currently on backorder.']
  is_gift boolean [note: 'True if the line is marked as a gift for the recipient.']
  is_returnable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product can be returned under policy.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the order line.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the product.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'Date promised to the customer for delivery of this line.']
  quantity_fulfilled varchar [note: 'Number of units actually shipped to the customer.']
  quantity_ordered varchar [note: 'Number of units requested by the customer.']
  restricted_item_flag boolean [note: 'True if the product is subject to sales restrictions.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after delivery during which a return is allowed.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary tax charged for this line.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line is exempt from tax.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the tax authority for this line.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  total_gross decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount before tax and after discounts (quantity × unit_price – discount).']
  total_net decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after tax (gross total + tax_amount).']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the shipment.']
  unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price per unit before discounts and taxes.']
  upc varchar [note: 'Global barcode identifier for the product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the line record.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,3) [note: 'Physical weight of the line item in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Individual SKU-level line items within a confirmed order. Captures SKU/ASIN/UPC, product name, quantity ordered/fulfilled, unit selling price, line-level discount, line total, seller ID for marketplace orders, fulfillment node assignment, line status, promised delivery date, and line-level tax details (jurisdiction, rate, amount, exemption status). Supports GMV decomposition, line-level fulfillment tracking, returns attribution, and tax compliance reporting.'
}

Table order.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each status transition record.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the actor (user, system, seller, etc.) that performed the transition.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this status transition belongs.']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_support_case. Business justification: Order status changes triggered by service actions (RMA approvals, fraud holds, case-driven cancellations) require case linkage for audit trails, regulatory compliance reporting, and customer dispute r']
  actor_type varchar [note: 'Category of the entity that triggered the status change.']
  comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form text providing additional context for the status change.']
  is_system_generated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transition was automatically generated by the system (true) or manually entered (false).']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why the status changed (e.g., payment_failed, inventory_allocated).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this status history record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record (e.g., correction of comment).']
  sla_actual_seconds varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed time between the previous status and this transition, in seconds.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if the actual duration exceeded the SLA target, otherwise false.']
  sla_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Configured service‑level‑agreement target time for this status transition, expressed in seconds.']
  status_from varchar [note: 'The order status before the transition occurred.']
  status_to varchar [note: 'The order status after the transition occurred.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the status change was recorded.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of all order lifecycle state transitions for a sales order. Records each status change event (e.g., PLACED → CONFIRMED → ALLOCATED → SHIPPED → DELIVERED → CANCELLED), the timestamp of transition, the actor or system that triggered the change, and any associated reason codes. Enables SLA breach detection, order lifecycle analytics, and OMS orchestration tracing.'
}

Table order.order_address {
  order_address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the order address record.']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Provides audit link from the order shipping address to the customer\'s saved address record, required for address change compliance and delivery performance analysis.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the address purpose for the order (shipping, billing, or pickup).']
  carrier_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred shipping speed or carrier service level.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the address record was first created.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions provided by the recipient for delivery.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the recipient.']
  external_address_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by an external address verification or logistics provider.']
  geocode_accuracy varchar [note: 'Indicates the confidence level of the geocoding result.']
  is_residential boolean [note: 'True if the address is a residential location; false otherwise.']
  label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the address (e.g., Home, Office).']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the address in decimal degrees.']
  line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address.']
  line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the street address (optional).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the address in decimal degrees.']
  order_address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the recipient.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the address.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the person receiving the shipment.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the address.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction applicable to the address.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate for the address expressed as a percentage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the address record.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of address validation (valid, invalid, or partially valid).']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the address was last validated.']

  Note: 'Shipping and billing address records associated with a sales order. Stores address type (shipping/billing/pickup), recipient name, address lines, city, state/province, postal code, country, geolocation coordinates, address validation status, and residential vs. commercial flag. Supports last-mile delivery routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and fraud screening.'
}

Table order.order_payment {
  order_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the order payment allocation record.']
  authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.authorization. Business justification: Treasury and dispute-resolution processes require tracing each captured order payment back to the specific authorization record (auth code, AVS/CVV result, hold expiry). Finance and fraud teams use th']
  gateway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.gateway. Business justification: Split-payment scenarios route individual payment lines through different gateways with distinct fee structures and settlement cycles. Finance teams require per-payment-line gateway attribution for acc']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Every payment transaction must post to a GL account for period-end close, audit trail, and financial reporting. An e-commerce finance team expects each order_payment to be directly traceable to its GL']
  gmv_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.gmv_settlement. Business justification: Finance reconciliation requires knowing which GMV settlement period each order payment was included in. This FK enables payment-to-settlement traceability for audit, dispute resolution, and regulatory']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this payment allocation belongs.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: REQUIRED: Reconcile each payment to its accounting journal entry for audit and financial reporting.']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant (seller) receiving the payment.']
  method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.method. Business justification: Split-payment processing requires per-line payment method tracking for fraud scoring, daily/monthly limit enforcement, and method-level revenue reporting. payment_method on order_payment is a denormal']
  primary_payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment transaction from the payment gateway.']
  reconciliation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.reconciliation. Business justification: Period-close financial reporting requires tracing each order payment line to its reconciliation run to identify matched vs. unmatched lines and variance amounts. reconciliation_status on order_payment']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Required for marketplace split‑payment reconciliation; each payment line must be tied to the seller receiving funds, a standard financial report in e‑commerce.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.settlement. Business justification: Settlement reconciliation reporting requires linking each order payment line to the settlement batch it was included in. Finance teams track which orders settled in which batch and on which date for p']
  token_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.token. Business justification: PCI-DSS compliance audit trails require recording which payment token was used for each order payment line. Security and compliance teams use this link to verify token scope, expiry, and usage counts ']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount authorized for this payment allocation.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing audit events for this payment allocation.']
  authorization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was authorized.']
  capture_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was captured.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was initiated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code of the payment.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if settlement currency differs from transaction currency.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Processing fee charged by the payment processor.']
  fraud_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of fraud detection checks for this payment.']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Numeric fraud risk score assigned by the payment gateway.']
  is_gift_card boolean [note: 'True if part of the payment was made using a gift card.']
  is_refund boolean [note: 'Indicates if this record represents a refund transaction.']
  is_split_payment boolean [note: 'Indicates if the order payment is part of a split payment across multiple methods.']
  is_store_credit boolean [note: 'True if part of the payment was made using store credit.']
  line_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of payment units allocated (typically 1).']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of this payment allocation line within the payment transaction.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after taxes and fees.']
  note varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks about the payment.']
  payment_description varchar [note: 'Optional free-text description of the payment allocation.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment allocation.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External reference or token from the payment gateway for this payment transaction.']
  refunded_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount refunded for this payment allocation, if any.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the payment amount, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']

  Note: 'Order-level payment allocation records linking a sales order to one or more payment transactions. Captures payment method type, payment reference from the payment gateway, amount allocated to this order, currency, payment status (authorized, captured, failed, refunded), split-payment flags, and gift card or store credit redemption amounts. This is the order domains local reference to payment events — it does NOT own gateway integration, PCI-scoped card data, or settlement processing (those belong to the payment domain). Enables order-level payment reconciliation, refund eligibility checks, and revenue recognition without crossing domain boundaries.'
}

Table order.order_promotion {
  order_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order promotion record.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: Promotions are targeted to specific audience segments (VIP customers, lapsed buyers). Linking order_promotion to audience_segment enables segment-level promotion redemption analysis and validates targ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated the promotion, if applicable.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Promotions are distributed through specific channels (email, push notification, social). Channel-level promotion redemption reporting — which channel drives the most coupon usage and discount spend — ']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Promotional discounts must post to specific GL accounts (discount expense, promotional accrual). E-commerce finance teams require promotional spend to be GL-coded for P&L reporting and marketing ROI a']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this promotion is applied.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: The order_promotion product description explicitly states promotions are \'applied at the order or line level\'. The existing header_id FK covers order-level promotions, but line-level promotions (e.g.,']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program associated with the promotion, if applicable.']
  marketplace_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_promotion. Business justification: When a marketplace promotion (deal, coupon, sponsored discount) is applied to an order, the order_promotion record must reference the originating marketplace_promotion for ROI reporting, FTC disclosur']
  promotion_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotion_rule. Business justification: Compliance and audit processes require verifying the correct promotion_rule was applied to each order (correct discount value, stacking rules, max redemption limits). Without this FK, auditors cannot ']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Promotion redemption reconciliation and promotional spend financial reporting require order_promotion to reference the specific pricing.promotional_campaign that authorized the discount. Pricing analy']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace seller that funded or owns the promotion.']
  applied_by varchar [note: 'Mechanism that applied the promotion: system, customer service agent, or automated rule.']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the promotion was applied to the order.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'JSON string capturing audit events related to the promotion record.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance verification.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the promotion (e.g., GDPR, PCI).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion record was first created in the system.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied (currency defined by discount_currency).']
  discount_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which discount_amount is expressed.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage value of the discount when promotion_type is percentage.']
  eligibility_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation when eligibility_status is ineligible or pending.']
  eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Result of promotion eligibility validation for this order.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion expires and can no longer be applied.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion is currently active in the system.']
  is_combination_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if this promotion may be combined with other promotions beyond stacking_rule logic.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates if the promotion excludes all other promotions when applied.']
  is_refunded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion discount was later reversed or refunded.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the promotion within the order\'s promotion list.']
  max_uses_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum total number of times a single customer may use this promotion.']
  max_uses_per_order varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this promotion can be applied within a single order.']
  net_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Discount amount after tax adjustments, if applicable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional remarks or internal comments.']
  order_promotion_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the promotion\'s purpose and mechanics.']
  promotion_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the promotion for reporting.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Business code that uniquely identifies the promotion rule.']
  promotion_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the promotion – marketing campaign, loyalty program, seller‑funded, or system generated.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of promotion: percentage discount, fixed amount, buy‑one‑get‑one, or free shipping.']
  quantity_applied varchar [note: 'Number of discount units applied (e.g., number of items covered by a BOGO).']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount refunded for the promotion, if any.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time when the promotion refund was processed.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation or policy governing the promotion (e.g., GDPR, PCI DSS).']
  stacking_rule varchar [note: 'Rule that defines whether this promotion can be combined with other promotions.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion becomes valid for use.']
  tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discount amount is exempt from sales tax.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'System user or process that created the promotion record.']

  Note: 'Records of promotions, discount codes, and coupons applied at the order or line level. Captures promotion code, promotion type (percentage discount, fixed amount, BOGO, free shipping), discount amount applied, promotion source (marketing campaign, loyalty program, seller-funded), eligibility validation status, and stacking rules applied. Supports GMV-net-of-discount reporting and ROAS attribution.'
}

Table order.tax {
  tax_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each tax line applied to an order.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this tax line belongs.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Tax can be applied at the line level; adding order_line_id FK enables precise tax calculations per line and removes ambiguity of using only header_id.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Tax compliance requires attributing tax amounts to the responsible seller for jurisdictional reporting and settlement.']
  tax_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_record. Business justification: The order.tax table captures operational tax calculations; finance.tax_record captures the official tax filing record. Linking them enables tax reconciliation between calculated and filed amounts — a ']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated monetary tax amount (taxable_amount * tax_rate).']
  calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the tax engine performed the calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tax record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  engine_reference varchar [note: 'Opaque identifier returned by the tax calculation engine for audit and traceability.']
  exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tax was exempted for this order line (true = exempt, false = taxable).']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for tax exemption, if applicable.']
  jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction (state, province, country) that governs the tax calculation.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the tax line within the order for ordering purposes.']
  nexus_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where tax nexus exists.']
  nexus_state varchar [note: 'State or province code within the nexus country that creates tax liability.']
  rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0750 for 7.5%).']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the tax line.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Category of tax applied: sales tax, VAT, GST, customs duty, or other.']
  taxable_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the order line(s) that is subject to this tax.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tax record.']

  Note: 'Tax calculation and jurisdiction records applied to a sales order. Captures tax jurisdiction code, tax type (sales tax, VAT, GST, customs duty), taxable amount, tax rate applied, tax amount, tax exemption status, nexus state/country, and the tax engine calculation reference. Supports SOX-compliant tax reporting, GDPR-aligned data residency, and cross-border e-commerce compliance.'
}

  cancellation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cancellation event.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Cancellations must clear or reduce the corresponding AR balance — a core AR cash application and credit memo process. E-commerce AR teams need to trace which AR record is reversed by each cancellation']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Stop-ship/stop-pick workflow: when an order is cancelled, the corresponding fulfillment_order must be halted immediately. Operations need this direct link to trigger cancellation in the WMS/fulfillmen']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Cancellations trigger GL reversal postings (revenue reversal, refund accruals). Finance teams require cancellation records to reference the GL account for period-end accuracy and audit compliance. No ']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this cancellation applies.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Cancellations generate journal entries (revenue reversal, refund liability postings). Linking cancellation to its journal entry provides the audit trail required for SOX compliance and period-end clos']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: The cancellation product description explicitly states it records \'order or order-line cancellation events\'. The existing header_id FK covers order-level cancellations, but partial/line-level cancella']
  payment_refund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_refund. Business justification: The cancellation-to-refund lifecycle is a core e-commerce operational process: customer service and finance must trace each cancellation to the resulting refund record for SLA tracking, refund status ']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.rma. Business justification: Post-shipment cancellations convert to RMAs; linking cancellation to the resulting RMA enables end-to-end refund processing, inventory restocking coordination, and customer service case resolution. E-']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Seller cancellation rate (scorecard KPI) and commission reversal in gmv_settlement require linking each cancellation directly to the responsible seller. Seller ops teams use this for performance monit']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Order cancellation triggers inventory release back to available stock. The cancellation record must reference the specific stock_position to restore on_hand_qty and release reserved_qty. E-commerce in']
  actor varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the cancellation request.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cancellation request was approved.']
  cancellation_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable cancellation reference number used in communications and reporting.']
  cancellation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cancellation request.']
  cancellation_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the domain of the cancellation (e.g., order‑level, line‑item, shipment, payment).']
  cancelled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the cancelled items before any refunds.']
  cancelled_quantity varchar [note: 'Total quantity of items cancelled (relevant for partial cancellations).']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cancellation process reached its final state (refunded or closed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cancellation record was first created in the system.']
  is_partial boolean [note: 'True if the cancellation is partial; false if it is a full order cancellation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or internal notes about the cancellation.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing why the cancellation occurred.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the cancellation reason.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount to be refunded to the customer as a result of the cancellation.']
  refund_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the refund amount.']
  refund_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation qualifies for a refund according to policy.']
  refund_processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the refund was actually processed and posted.']
  rejected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cancellation request was rejected.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation why a cancellation request was denied.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cancellation was initially requested.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the cancellation applies to the entire order or only specific line items.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the cancellation request was received.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cancellation record.']

  Note: 'Records of order or order-line cancellation events initiated by customers, sellers, or the OMS. Captures cancellation request timestamp, reason code (customer-initiated, out-of-stock, fraud hold, seller cancellation, payment failure), status workflow (requested → approved/rejected → completed), refund eligibility, cancellation actor, and partial vs. full cancellation scope. Supports cancellation rate KPI tracking, GMV leakage analysis, and refund workflow triggering.'
}

Table order.delivery_confirmation {
  delivery_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for delivery_confirmation']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_shipment. Business justification: Delivery SLA reporting and carrier performance: delivery_confirmation records the final delivery event and must reference the specific fulfillment_shipment for SLA compliance measurement, carrier disp']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this delivery confirmation belongs.']
  actual_delivery_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Measured time from order dispatch to successful delivery, in seconds.']
  carrier_scan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code from the carrier\'s scan event confirming hand‑off.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery confirmation record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  delivery_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of delivery attempts made for this order.']
  delivery_exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why a delivery attempt failed or was exceptional (e.g., address_not_found, recipient_unavailable).']
  delivery_location_type varchar [note: 'Physical type of the delivery location where the package was left.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the delivery after this confirmation event.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the delivery was completed and confirmed.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the handheld device or scanner used to record the confirmation.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery point.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery point.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes entered by the driver or carrier about the delivery.']
  otp_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OTP provided by the recipient was successfully verified.']
  proof_of_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture proof of delivery (e.g., signature, photo, one‑time‑password).']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who received the package, captured for signature or verification.']
  recipient_signature_image_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored image of the recipient\'s signature, if captured.']
  scheduled_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for delivery as per the order SLA.']
  sla_delivery_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Service‑level‑agreement target delivery time expressed in seconds.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the delivery confirmation (e.g., WMS, TMS, OMS).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this delivery confirmation record.']

  Note: 'Final delivery confirmation records for fulfilled orders. Captures actual delivery timestamp, delivery location type (door, locker, neighbor, pickup point), proof-of-delivery reference (signature, photo, OTP), carrier scan event reference, delivery attempt number, and delivery exception codes (failed attempt, returned to sender). This is the SSOT for confirmed delivery events within the order domain, distinct from logistics shipment tracking.'
}

Table order.fulfillment_allocation {
  fulfillment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fulfillment_allocation']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Fulfillment allocation selects a specific carrier service (e.g., FedEx Ground, UPS 2-Day) based on zone, SLA, and cost. This FK enables SLA enforcement, shipping cost estimation, and carrier performan']
  center_id varchar [note: 'FK to fulfillment.center.center_id — Order allocation to warehouse tracing. Without this FK, the OMS cannot determine which fulfillment center was assigned to process an order line — breaking allocation-to-dispatch traceability.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fulfillment costs (shipping, handling, warehouse) must be allocated to cost centers for operational P&L and management accounting. The fulfillment_allocation has cost_estimate fields but no cost cente']
  delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery_zone. Business justification: Allocation decisions are zone-driven — delivery zone determines SLA, carrier eligibility, and surcharges. The existing shipping_zone is a denormalized text field; replacing it with a proper FK to deli']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Allocation-to-fulfillment traceability: when inventory is allocated for an order line, it results in a fulfillment_order. Operations teams need this link for allocation reconciliation, split-shipment ']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Allocation of inventory to an order line must reference the goods receipt that supplied the stock for accurate inventory and financial reconciliation.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order to which this allocation belongs.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: The fulfillment_allocation product description explicitly states it \'Records the assignment of order lines to specific fulfillment nodes\'. The existing header_id FK provides order-level context, but t']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Drop-ship fulfillment allocations (is_drop_ship=true) must reference the fulfilling seller directly for routing, SLA tracking, and seller performance measurement. Without this FK, drop-ship allocation']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Fulfillment allocation commits specific inventory (SKU+lot+location) to an order. Direct reference to stock_position enables reserved_qty management, lot traceability, and expiry-date-first picking. w']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, 3PL center, or seller location assigned to fulfill the line.']
  allocated_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units allocated to the fulfillment node.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or rule used to select the fulfillment node.']
  allocation_rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the allocation algorithm or rule set applied.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the allocation decision (automated system, manual entry, API call, batch process).']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the allocation decision.']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the allocation decision was made.']
  carrier_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred carrier or service level for the shipment.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of fulfilling the allocated quantity before taxes and shipping.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  dimensions_cm varchar [note: 'Length x Width x Height expressed in centimeters.']
  estimated_ship_date date [note: 'Projected date the allocated items will be shipped.']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Category of fulfillment node handling the allocation.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Specific instructions for packing or handling the item.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'True if the allocated quantity is on backorder.']
  is_drop_ship boolean [note: 'True if the fulfillment is performed directly by the supplier.']
  is_eligible_for_fast_ship boolean [note: 'True if the line qualifies for accelerated shipping options.']
  is_eligible_for_free_shipping boolean [note: 'True if the line meets criteria for free shipping.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'True if the allocation is marked for expedited processing.']
  is_fragile boolean [note: 'True if the item requires special handling due to fragility.']
  is_gift_wrap boolean [note: 'True if the allocation includes gift‑wrap service.']
  is_oversized boolean [note: 'True if the item dimensions exceed standard handling limits.']
  last_status_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent status change for the allocation.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential position of the line within the order.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments from the allocation engine or operator.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Marks the allocation as high priority for expedited handling.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation record.']
  shipping_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated shipping charge for the allocated quantity.']
  sla_actual_seconds varchar [note: 'Actual elapsed time from allocation to shipment.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if actual SLA exceeds the target.']
  sla_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Service‑level‑agreement target time for allocation fulfillment.']
  split_shipment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order line is split across multiple nodes.']
  tax_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated tax amount applicable to the allocation.']
  total_estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of cost, shipping, and tax estimates for the allocation.']
  volume_cubic_m decimal(8,3) [note: 'Physical volume of the allocated quantity.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical weight of the allocated quantity.']

  Note: 'Records the assignment of order lines to specific fulfillment nodes (warehouses, 3PL centers, FBA-style nodes, seller locations, dark stores). Captures the fulfillment node ID, allocation timestamp, allocation method (nearest node, lowest cost, seller-fulfilled, zone-skipping), allocated quantity, allocation status (pending, confirmed, picked, cancelled), split-shipment flag, and estimated ship date. This is the order domains handoff point to the fulfillment domain — it owns the allocation decision, not the pick-pack-ship execution.'
}

Table inventory.stock_position {
  stock_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the stock position record representing a SKU at a specific warehouse node or virtual location. Primary key of this entity.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Enables PO‑level stock traceability for recall, cost allocation and supplier performance dashboards.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketplace seller who owns this inventory. Null for first-party (1P) inventory owned by the platform operator.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU whose inventory position is being tracked. Links to the product catalog master record.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Provides supplier‑wise inventory health reporting and compliance checks, a core e‑commerce operational need.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: A stock position is physically stored at a specific bin/shelf location within a warehouse node. The existing bin_location_code (STRING) on stock_position is a denormalized reference to warehouse_locat']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical or virtual warehouse node (fulfillment center, dark store, 3PL facility, virtual location) where this stock position is held.']
  atp_qty varchar [note: 'Net units available for new order commitments, calculated as on_hand_qty minus reserved_qty minus quarantined_qty. The ATP quantity is the real-time sellable inventory figure exposed to the storefront and OMS for order promising.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stock position record was first created in the system, typically when the SKU was first received at this warehouse node or when the node was onboarded. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  cycle_count_variance_qty varchar [note: 'Difference between the system-recorded on-hand quantity and the physically counted quantity at the last cycle count (counted minus system). Positive = overage, negative = shrinkage. Used for inventory accuracy KPI reporting and shrinkage investigation.']
