Captures node code, node type, address, geographic coordinates, capacity (sqft and pallet positions), operating hours, supported fulfillment types (FBA-style, BOPIS, DTC), and active status. SSOT for warehouse network topology.'
}

Table payment.payment_transaction {
  payment_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment transaction.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Required for account‑level financial statements and reconciliation of each payment transaction to the customer\'s account.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Cash application process matches payment transactions to AR records to clear open receivables. Finance AR aging accuracy, cash application reporting, and DSO calculations require linking each payment ']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for Marketing ROI report linking each transaction to the driving campaign for revenue attribution.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Tax and regulatory reporting requires mapping each transaction to the catalog item to apply correct jurisdiction‑specific tax codes.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Channel-level revenue attribution and ROAS reporting: e-commerce teams report payment revenue by marketing channel (email, paid search, social). payment_transaction already has campaign_id but not cha']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating processing fees to the appropriate cost center in monthly expense reporting.']
  gateway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.gateway. Business justification: Every payment transaction is processed through a specific payment gateway. payment_transaction currently captures gateway_response_code and gateway_response_message as string fields but has no FK to t']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Supports direct posting of each payment transaction to a GL account for real‑time financial reconciliation.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with this payment.']
  marketplace_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_promotion. Business justification: Payment transactions are directly discounted by marketplace promotions at checkout. Finance teams need this link for promotion ROI analysis, promotional discount liability accounting, and reconciling ']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant receiving the payment.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables attribution of net revenue from each transaction to the correct profit center for performance dashboards.']
  reconciliation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.reconciliation. Business justification: Transactions are part of a reconciliation run; linking via reconciliation_id enables auditability and variance analysis.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Seller‑level revenue and commission reporting needs each transaction linked to the seller who fulfilled the order.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.settlement. Business justification: Each payment transaction is settled in a settlement batch; adding settlement_id links transaction to its settlement for traceability and reporting.']
  token_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.token. Business justification: Payment transactions processed with tokenized card data reference a specific PCI DSS token in the token registry. payment_transaction currently has card_last4, card_brand, card_expiry_month, card_expi']
  tokenized_payment_method_id varchar [note: 'PCI‑DSS compliant token representing the stored payment method.']
  wallet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.wallet. Business justification: Payment transactions can be funded from a customer digital wallet. payment_transaction currently has no FK to the wallet table, despite wallet being a first-class payment instrument in the domain. Add']
  amount_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Processing fee charged by the payment gateway.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount authorized before fees or taxes.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount settled to the merchant after fees and taxes.']
  amount_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the transaction.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Code returned by the issuing bank authorizing the transaction.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'Brand of the payment card when card‑based method is used.']
  card_expiry_month varchar [note: 'Expiration month of the payment card.']
  card_expiry_year varchar [note: 'Expiration year of the payment card.']
  card_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the card number for display and reconciliation.']
  chargeback_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction resulted in a chargeback.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of compliance regimes applicable to the transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the transaction.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to capture the payment.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert from original currency to settlement currency, if applicable.']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned by fraud detection engine (0‑100).']
  gateway_response_code varchar [note: 'Standardized response code from the payment gateway.']
  gateway_response_message varchar [note: 'Human‑readable message accompanying the gateway response code.']
  identifier varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the payment gateway for this transaction.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the transaction was initiated.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'True if the transaction is part of a recurring payment schedule.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s industry.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Digital or physical channel through which the payment was captured.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to fund the transaction.']
  payment_transaction_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or memo for the transaction.']
  payment_transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transaction.']
  recurring_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of the recurring payment.']
  recurring_end_date date [note: 'Date when the recurring payment schedule terminates.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount refunded to the customer, if any.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the refund was processed.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification derived from fraud score and other signals.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which funds were settled to the merchant account.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the settlement process.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction event occurred.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the transaction record.']

  Note: 'Core authoritative record of every monetary transaction processed on the platform, including the immutable event log of all state transitions (authorized, captured, settled, voided, failed) and gateway webhook callbacks. Captures payment amount, currency, exchange rate applied, processing status, gateway response codes, authorization codes, timestamps, retry history, and event-driven lifecycle audit trail. Each transaction references a payment method, may have one or more authorizations, and flows through to settlement. Supports PCI DSS audit requirements and PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) compliance evidence. SSOT for all payment transaction records, their lifecycle events, and state transition history.'
}

Table payment.method {
  method_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for method']
  billing_customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: Address verification and 3NF normalization: payment method billing address is denormalized across five columns. Linking to customer_address enables AVS (Address Verification Service) checks, address c']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns this payment method.']
  card_network varchar [note: 'Card scheme or network for card‑based methods.']
  cardholder_email varchar [note: 'Email address associated with the cardholder.']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the cardholder as it appears on the card.']
  cardholder_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number associated with the cardholder.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance verification.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance posture of the payment method with regulatory standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment method record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the payment method.']
  daily_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative amount allowed per day.']
  expiry_month varchar [note: 'Expiration month of the card (1‑12).']
  expiry_year varchar [note: 'Expiration year of the card (four‑digit).']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score (0‑100) indicating fraud likelihood.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this method is the customer\'s default payment instrument.']
  is_international_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the payment method supports cross‑border transactions.']
  is_one_time_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the payment method can be used for one‑off transactions.']
  is_recurring_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if the payment method can be used for recurring billing.']
  issuing_bank varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution that issued the card or account.']
  last_four varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the card number (masked).']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment method was last used in a transaction.']
  method_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text description of the payment method.']
  method_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment method.']
  method_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment instrument.']
  monthly_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative amount allowed per calendar month.']
  payment_method_name varchar [note: 'User‑defined nickname for the payment method.']
  preference_rank varchar [note: 'User‑defined ranking indicating preferred payment method order.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification for the payment method.']
  sca_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment method is enrolled for SCA (e.g., 3‑DS).']
  token_reference varchar [note: 'PCI‑DSS token that represents the underlying card or bank account.']
  tokenization_provider varchar [note: 'Vendor or service that performed tokenization of the payment instrument.']
  transaction_limit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allowed per individual transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment method record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Result of the verification process for the payment method.']

  Note: 'Master record of all payment instruments registered by customers on the platform, including credit/debit cards (PCI DSS-tokenized), digital wallets (Apple Pay, Google Pay, PayPal), bank accounts (ACH/SEPA), buy-now-pay-later instruments, gift cards, and marketplace credits. Stores tokenized card data reference, billing address, expiry, card network (Visa/MC/Amex/Discover), issuing bank, method lifecycle status (active/expired/suspended/deleted), customer preference ranking, and SCA enrollment status. SSOT for payment instrument definitions referenced by transactions, authorizations, and tokens.'
}

Table payment.authorization {
  authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payment authorization record.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who initiated the payment.']
  gateway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.gateway. Business justification: Every authorization request is routed through a specific payment gateway. authorization currently has no FK to the gateway master table, despite capturing gateway_response_code and gateway_response_me']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Authorizations are initiated during marketplace transaction checkout. Risk and payments teams need this link for authorization rate reporting by marketplace segment, 3DS compliance tracking per transa']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant account receiving the payment.']
  method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.method. Business justification: Every authorization attempt is made against a specific payment method (credit card, debit card, etc.). authorization currently stores payment_method as a string and tokenized_card_number as a string, ']
  token_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.token. Business justification: authorization stores tokenized_card_number as a raw string, which is a denormalized reference to the PCI DSS token registry. Adding token_id as a proper FK to the token table normalizes this relations']
  auth_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount requested for authorization, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  auth_code varchar [note: 'Issuer‑provided code confirming successful authorization.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the authorization (e.g., authorized, declined, voided).']
  avs_result varchar [note: 'Result of the AVS check comparing billing address to card records.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the billing address.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'Brand of the payment card (e.g., Visa, MasterCard, Amex).']
  card_expiry_month varchar [note: 'Expiration month of the payment card (1‑12).']
  card_expiry_year varchar [note: 'Expiration year of the payment card (four‑digit).']
  challenge_status varchar [note: 'Status of any 3‑DS challenge presented to the cardholder.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework applicable to this authorization (e.g., PCI DSS, PSD2, GDPR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the authorization record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the authorization amount.']
  cvv_result varchar [note: 'Result of the CVV check for the card.']
  decline_reason varchar [note: 'Issuer‑provided textual reason for a declined authorization.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the authorization request was sent to the issuing bank.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the transaction amount to the settlement currency, if applicable.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the payment gateway or processor for this authorization.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction was flagged as potentially fraudulent.']
  hold_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time when the authorization hold is scheduled to expire if not captured.']
  is_captured boolean [note: 'True if the authorized amount has been captured.']
  is_partial_capture_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the authorized amount can be captured in parts.']
  is_refunded boolean [note: 'True if the captured amount has been fully refunded.']
  is_test_transaction boolean [note: 'True if the authorization was generated in a test or sandbox environment.']
  is_voided boolean [note: 'True if the authorization was voided before capture.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code classifying the merchant\'s business type.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency code of the amount originally authorized before any conversion.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Digital channel through which the payment was initiated.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used for the payment (e.g., credit card, digital wallet).']
  processing_latency_ms varchar [note: 'Round‑trip latency of the authorization request measured in milliseconds.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk assessment (0‑100) generated by fraud detection engine.']
  settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final settled amount after capture, fees, and adjustments.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the authorized amount was settled to the merchant.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional amount added to the authorization for surcharges (e.g., service fees).']
  three_ds_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the 3‑DS/SCA authentication flow.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the authorization record.']

  Note: 'Captures the real-time authorization request and response lifecycle for each payment attempt against the issuing bank via the payment gateway. Records authorization code, AVS/CVV result, issuer response, decline reason, authorization amount, hold expiry, 3DS/SCA authentication result and challenge status, and gateway latency. An authorization may be voided, partially captured, retried, or expire without settlement — distinct from the final captured transaction. Supports PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication compliance tracking.'
}

Table payment.settlement {
  settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the settlement record.']
  finance_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_bank_account. Business justification: Settlement funds are deposited into specific bank accounts. Treasury and cash management processes require knowing which bank account received each settlement batch. Bank reconciliation and cash flow ']
  gateway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.gateway. Business justification: Settlement batches are submitted through specific payment gateways to acquiring banks. settlement currently has acquiring_bank_code but no FK to the gateway master. Adding gateway_id links each settle']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Settlement batches must be posted to specific GL accounts for cash receipt accounting and month-end close. Finance teams run settlement-to-GL reconciliation reports. A finance expert would consider th']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Settlements must be attributed to the correct legal entity for statutory reporting, intercompany reconciliation, and regulatory compliance. Multi-entity e-commerce platforms process settlements under ']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant (seller) account receiving the settlement funds.']
  reconciliation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.reconciliation. Business justification: Settlement batches are reconciled as part of a reconciliation run. settlement currently stores reconciliation_status and reconciliation_timestamp as denormalized fields, but has no FK to the reconcili']
  acquiring_bank_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the acquiring bank that processes the settlement.']
  assessment_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Regulatory or network assessment fee applied to the settlement.']
  audit_user varchar [note: 'User or service account that performed the last update on the settlement record.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier linking settlements that belong to the same processing batch.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was first created in the system.']
  cross_border_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional fee applied for cross‑currency or cross‑border transactions.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the settlement currency.']
  currency_conversion_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee for converting the transaction amount into the settlement currency.']
  cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency with which settlements are generated for the merchant.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which fee amounts are expressed.']
  fee_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all individual fees associated with the settlement.']
  gateway_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee retained by the payment gateway provider for service usage.']
  interchange_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the card scheme for processing the transaction.']
  is_manual_settlement boolean [note: 'True if the settlement was created manually (e.g., adjustment or correction).']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount transferred to the merchant after all fees are deducted.']
  processing_fee_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Rate applied to calculate the processing fee (e.g., 0.0025 = 0.25%).']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the settlement was executed.']
  settlement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the settlement.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to transfer funds to the merchant (e.g., ACH, wire, card payout).']
  settlement_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable settlement reference number used in finance and reporting.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the settlement.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment (including time zone) when the settlement was posted to the acquiring bank.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the record represents a batch settlement or a single‑transaction settlement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream payment gateway or processing platform that generated the settlement record.']
  status_detail varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text information describing the current status (e.g., reason for failure).']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the settlement before fee deductions, expressed in the settlement currency.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual payment transactions included in this settlement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement record.']

  Note: 'Records settlement batches and individual settlement entries submitted to acquiring banks and payment networks for fund transfer, including all associated processing fees at transaction level. Settlement attributes: settlement date, amount, currency, acquiring bank, merchant account, status (pending/settled/failed). Fee attributes: fee type (interchange, assessment, gateway fee, cross-border fee, currency conversion fee), fee amount, fee rate, fee currency. SSOT for settlement records and their associated processing fees. Supports daily reconciliation, financial close, cost-of-payment analysis, and fee reconciliation against gateway invoices.'
}

Table payment.payment_refund {
  payment_refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the refund transaction.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Refunds must be posted to the originating customer account for balance updates, audit trails, and regulatory reporting.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Refunds reduce or close open AR balances. Cash application and AR reconciliation processes require linking refunds to the AR records they offset. AR aging accuracy and customer account statements depe']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Needed to attribute refunds to the originating campaign, enabling accurate net revenue and ROAS calculations.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Channel-level net revenue and refund rate reporting: marketing teams track refund rates by acquisition channel to assess channel quality and true ROI. payment_refund has campaign_id but not channel_id']
  coupon_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.coupon_redemption. Business justification: Coupon redemption reversal on refund: when a refund is processed for an order where a coupon was redeemed, the specific coupon_redemption record must be located and its status reversed/voided. Direct ']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Allows refund audit linking each refund to the exact fulfillment order it reverses, required for refund‑to‑fulfillment reporting.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Refunds require GL postings to reverse revenue and record cash outflows. Finance month-end close, refund expense reporting, and revenue reversal entries all require knowing which GL account each refun']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Each refund triggers a journal entry reversing the original revenue and cash postings. SOX controls and finance audit trails require tracing every refund to its corresponding journal entry for complet']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Shipping cost refunds (lost/damaged shipments) require direct reference to the logistics shipment for carrier claim filing and refund reconciliation. E-commerce ops teams must trace which shipment tri']
  method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.method. Business justification: Refunds are returned to a specific payment method (typically the original method used for the transaction). payment_refund currently stores refund_method and payment_method as string descriptors but h']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Refunds must be linked to the original payment transaction for traceability and reconciliation; this FK enables joining refund details to the transaction record.']
  promotion_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotion_rule. Business justification: Promotional refund calculation: when a promotion rule (BOGO, tiered discount) was applied to an order, the refund processor must reference the specific rule mechanics (discount_value, rule_type, stack']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Regulatory and financial audits track refunds of promotional discounts, requiring a link to the originating campaign.']
  proof_of_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.proof_of_delivery. Business justification: Refund eligibility verification: e-commerce refund processing checks POD status before approving refunds to prevent refund fraud on confirmed-delivered items. No existing link connects payment_refund ']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the RMA that triggered this refund, if applicable.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Refunds must be attributed to the originating seller for correct financial adjustments and seller performance metrics.']
  service_refund_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_refund. Business justification: Links financial refund record to service‑side refund process for reconciliation and end‑to‑end refund tracking.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.settlement. Business justification: Refunds are settled through settlement batches submitted to acquiring banks. payment_refund currently stores settlement_status and settlement_date as denormalized fields but has no FK to the settlemen']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Refund processing must reference the exact SKU being refunded for inventory adjustment and accurate financial reconciliation.']
  tax_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_record. Business justification: Refunds require tax credit notes and VAT/GST adjustment entries. Tax compliance reporting (VAT returns, GST filings) requires linking refunds to their tax records to correctly reverse tax liabilities ']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount requested for refund before any fees or adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the refund record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the refund amount.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Processing fee charged by the payment gateway for the refund.']
  fraud_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the fraud detection check for this refund.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the refund was initially requested.']
  is_fraud boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund is associated with a suspected fraud case.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount that will be returned to the customer after fees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional information about the refund.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the original payment was made.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Original payment instrument used for the transaction being refunded.']
  payment_refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the refund request.']
  processing_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the payment processor for handling the refund.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the refund was issued.']
  reference varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the refund for tracking and reconciliation.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method used to return funds to the customer.']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the refund is for the full transaction amount or a partial amount.']
  request_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the refund request (e.g., customer service agent, self‑service portal, automated rule).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the refund record.']

  Note: 'Tracks all refund transactions initiated against original payment transactions, including full and partial refunds. Records refund amount, refund reason code, originating transaction reference, refund method (original payment method or store credit), processing status, and gateway refund confirmation. Supports RMA-driven refund workflows and customer service-initiated refunds.'
}

Table payment.chargeback {
  chargeback_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the chargeback record.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Chargebacks reverse previously recognized AR balances. AR dispute management and write-off processes require linking chargebacks to the AR record being reversed. AR aging reports and dispute resolutio']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'External dispute reference number assigned by the cardholder\'s issuing bank.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_shipment. Business justification: Chargeback evidence package assembly requires direct access to shipment records (tracking number, delivery status, carrier data) to contest \'item not received\' disputes. No existing path connects char']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Chargeback losses and fees must be posted to GL accounts (chargeback expense, fee expense accounts). Finance P&L reporting on chargeback costs and provisioning for chargeback reserves requires linking']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with the disputed transaction.']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant whose account is affected by the chargeback.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original payment transaction that is being disputed.']
  proof_of_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.proof_of_delivery. Business justification: Chargeback dispute resolution for \'item not received\' claims requires POD as primary regulatory evidence submitted to card networks. No existing path connects chargeback to proof_of_delivery. Card sch']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Chargebacks in marketplace e-commerce are filed against specific seller transactions. Chargeback management, seller liability recovery, and order_defect_rate performance scoring all require direct sel']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_support_case. Business justification: Chargeback dispute resolution in e-commerce requires a linked service support case for evidence gathering, customer communication, and SLA tracking. Operations and compliance teams need to correlate c']
  tax_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_record. Business justification: Chargebacks require VAT/GST credit note issuance and tax reversal entries. Tax compliance for disputed transactions requires linking chargebacks to their associated tax records for accurate VAT return']
  amount_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee assessed by the card scheme for processing the chargeback.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after fees that the merchant is liable for.']
  amount_original decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross amount originally charged to the cardholder.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes from auditors or investigators regarding the chargeback.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'Brand of the card used (e.g., Visa, MasterCard, Amex).']
  card_last4 varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the card number (PCI‑DSS compliant token).']
  card_type varchar [note: 'Type of card (e.g., credit, debit, prepaid).']
  chargeback_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the chargeback.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification status for the chargeback.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the chargeback record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the disputed transaction.']
  dispute_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the dispute reason.']
  dispute_subcategory varchar [note: 'More granular sub‑type within the dispute category.']
  dispute_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute was initially filed by the cardholder.']
  dispute_type varchar [note: 'Indicates who initiated the dispute: cardholder, merchant, or issuing bank.']
  evidence_submission_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the merchant submitted evidence.']
  evidence_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'True if the merchant has submitted required evidence for the dispute.']
  fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional fee levied by the card scheme for processing the chargeback.']
  fraud_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score assigned by the fraud detection engine.']
  is_fraudulent_flag boolean [note: 'True if the dispute is determined to be fraud.']
  liability_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount the liable party must pay after the dispute is resolved.']
  liability_party varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for the net chargeback amount.']
  merchant_category_code varchar [note: 'Four‑digit code that classifies the merchant\'s business type.']
  merchant_location_city varchar [note: 'City where the merchant\'s primary location resides.']
  merchant_location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the merchant\'s primary location.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final result of the dispute after adjudication.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Digital channel through which the payment was made.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used for the original transaction.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the reason for the dispute (e.g., Visa R01, MC 4837).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the dispute reason.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to any regulatory filing or compliance case linked to the chargeback.']
  response_deadline timestamp [note: 'Latest timestamp by which the merchant must respond to the dispute.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the chargeback amount is settled with the merchant.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the chargeback record.']

  Note: 'Manages the full lifecycle of payment disputes and chargebacks initiated by cardholders through their issuing bank. Tracks chargeback reason code (per Visa/MC/Amex dispute categories), dispute amount, dispute date, response deadline, evidence submission status, chargeback outcome (won/lost/accepted), and associated financial liability. Critical for fraud management and financial reconciliation.'
}

Table payment.fraud_case {
  fraud_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each fraud case.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst assigned to the case.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fraud losses are allocated to cost centers for internal management reporting and budget variance analysis. Operations and finance teams track fraud costs by business unit to measure cost center perfor']
  coupon_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.coupon. Business justification: Coupon fraud investigation: coupon cloning, fake coupon codes, and mass redemption fraud are major e-commerce fraud types. A fraud investigator must identify which specific coupon was involved to asse']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: Fraud investigation workflow: fraud analysts must verify the shipping/billing address associated with a suspicious transaction. fraud_case tracks geo_country_code and ip_address but lacks a direct add']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_shipment. Business justification: Fraud investigation shipment audit: triangulation fraud and friendly fraud detection require verifying whether goods shipped to the claimed address. No direct path from fraud_case to fulfillment_shipm']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Fraud losses and recovery amounts must be posted to GL accounts (fraud loss expense, fraud recovery income). Finance fraud provisioning, loss reporting, and P&L impact analysis require linking fraud c']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with the payment.']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Fraud cases originate from specific marketplace transactions. Trust & safety and fraud operations teams need this direct link for seller liability assignment, marketplace fraud rate reporting, and tra']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant/seller linked to the transaction.']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Promotional fraud analysis: promotional abuse (fake accounts exploiting campaigns, discount stacking) is a primary e-commerce fraud vector. Linking fraud_case to promotional_campaign enables campaign-']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Marketplace fraud cases can originate from seller-side fraud (counterfeit listings, payment fraud). Fraud investigation teams must link cases to seller profiles for seller suspension workflows, KYC re']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_support_case. Business justification: Fraud investigations in e-commerce generate parallel customer service cases (unauthorized charge complaints, account compromise). Fraud operations teams must correlate fraud_case records with service_']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable case number used in investigations and reporting.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fraud case.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fraud case record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fraud amount.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the fraud detection signal.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fraud signal was generated.']
  device_fingerprint varchar [note: 'Hashed identifier of the device used in the transaction.']
  fraud_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with the suspected fraudulent transaction.']
  fraud_type varchar [note: 'Category of fraudulent activity detected.']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the transaction origin.']
  investigation_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the investigation was concluded.']
  investigation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the investigation began.']
  investigator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the analyst handling the investigation.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the transaction originated.']
  is_high_risk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case is flagged as high risk for priority handling.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes entered by investigators.']
  processing_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the payment processor for handling the transaction.']
  recovery_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount recovered from the fraudulent transaction.']
  resolution_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the fraud case after investigation.']
  rule_action varchar [note: 'Automated action taken when the rule fires.']
  rule_activation_status varchar [note: 'Current activation state of the fraud rule.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the fraud detection rule.']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of the rule; higher values indicate more critical rules.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the underlying payment transaction occurred.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the fraud case record.']

  Note: 'Operational record of confirmed or suspected fraudulent payment activity requiring investigation and action, plus the authoritative registry of fraud detection rules and velocity checks that drive automated prevention. Case attributes: fraud type (CNP, account takeover, friendly fraud, synthetic identity), detection source, fraud amount, case status, investigator assignment, resolution outcome, recovery amount. Rule attributes: rule logic (e.g., max transaction amount per hour, BIN country mismatch, device fingerprint anomaly), rule priority, action (block/review/allow), activation status, performance thresholds. SSOT for both fraud investigation cases and the fraud prevention ruleset. Supports real-time fraud blocking, post-transaction investigation, rule tuning, and regulatory SAR (Suspicious Activity Report) filing.'
}

Table payment.gateway {
  gateway_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for gateway']
  api_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the gateway\'s API that the platform integrates with.']
  compliance_gdpr boolean [note: 'True if the gateway adheres to GDPR data‑privacy obligations.']
  compliance_iso27001 boolean [note: 'True if the gateway is certified under ISO 27001 information‑security standards.']
  compliance_pci_dss boolean [note: 'True if the gateway meets PCI DSS requirements for card data handling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the gateway record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the gateway configuration becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the gateway configuration expires or is retired; null if open‑ended.']
  endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Base URL used for API calls to the payment gateway.']
  failover_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority for automatic failover; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  fraud_detection_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the gateway includes built‑in fraud detection services.']
  gateway_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the gateway\'s purpose, capabilities, or special notes.']
  gateway_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the payment gateway (e.g., Stripe, Adyen).']
  gateway_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the gateway configuration.']
  gateway_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the gateway (e.g., payment, bank, alternative, crypto).']
  is_3d_secure_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether 3‑D Secure authentication is available for this gateway.']
  is_multi_currency_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gateway can process transactions in multiple currencies without separate configurations.']
  is_tokenization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gateway supports PCI‑DSS tokenization of card data.']
  last_tested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the gateway configuration was last health‑checked.']
  max_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper monetary limit per transaction that the gateway will accept.']
  min_transaction_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Lower monetary threshold per transaction for the gateway.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes or comments from administrators.']
  processing_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the primary region where the gateway processes transactions.']
  provider_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to reference the gateway provider.']
  provider_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the gateway service provider.']
  routing_logic varchar [note: 'Textual description of the routing and fallback logic used when multiple gateways are configured.']
  settlement_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for settlement payouts.']
  settlement_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after a transaction before funds are settled to the merchant.']
  sla_response_time_ms varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed response time in milliseconds as defined in the service‑level agreement.']
  sla_uptime_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Targeted uptime percentage for the gateway service (e.g., 99.95).']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated ISO 4217 currency codes that the gateway accepts.']
  supported_payment_methods varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of payment methods (e.g., credit_card, debit_card, paypal, apple_pay) the gateway can process.']
  supports_chargebacks boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gateway provides chargeback handling capabilities.']
  supports_partial_refunds boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gateway allows partial refunds.']
  supports_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gateway can process recurring subscription payments.']
  supports_refunds boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gateway allows full refunds.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent connectivity/health test.']
  transaction_fee_fixed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary fee (in the settlement currency) applied per transaction.']
  transaction_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variable fee percentage applied to each transaction processed through the gateway.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the gateway record.']

  Note: 'Master reference for all payment gateway integrations and acquiring bank connections configured on the platform. Records gateway provider (Stripe, Adyen, Braintree, etc.), supported payment methods, supported currencies, processing region, gateway endpoint configuration, failover priority, and SLA parameters. Enables multi-gateway routing and fallback logic for transaction processing.'
}

  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Merchant account processing fees, chargeback fees, and settlement amounts are posted to specific GL accounts. Finance reporting on payment processing costs and fee analysis requires linking merchant a']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Merchant accounts are registered under specific legal entities for regulatory compliance, PCI-DSS scope, and tax reporting. Finance and compliance teams must know which legal entity owns each merchant']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Merchant accounts are registered and operated within a specific marketplace context in multi-marketplace platforms. Compliance, settlement routing, and fee configuration differ by marketplace. Finance']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Type of merchant account.']
  acquiring_bank_code varchar [note: 'Internal identifier for the acquiring bank.']
  acquiring_bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bank that acquires transactions for the merchant.']

  chargeback_fee_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Fee rate applied to chargeback transactions.']

  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the merchant account is terminated or expires (nullable).']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems to reference this merchant account.']
  fraud_detection_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if fraud detection is active for this account.']
  is_multi_currency boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account supports multiple currencies.']
  is_test_account boolean [note: 'Indicates if this is a test or sandbox merchant account.']
  last_settlement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent settlement for this account.']

  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the merchant was onboarded to the platform.']
  payment_network varchar [note: 'Payment card network associated with the merchant account.']
  pci_compliance_status varchar [note: 'PCI DSS compliance status.']
  processing_currency varchar [note: 'Currency in which transactions are processed.']
  processing_fee_fixed decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed fee per transaction.']
  processing_fee_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage fee applied to each transaction.']
  regulatory_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the account is subject to special regulatory reporting.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Internal risk assessment score for the merchant account.']
  settlement_bank_account varchar [note: 'Bank account number where settlements are deposited.']

  settlement_bank_swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code of the settlement bank.']

  settlement_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of settlement for the merchant account.']
  supported_currencies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO currency codes supported.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the merchant account was terminated.']
  tokenization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates if card data tokenization is used.']
  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method of tokenization applied.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the merchant account record.']

  Note: 'Master record of merchant accounts established with acquiring banks and payment processors for fund collection. Tracks merchant account number, acquiring bank, payment network agreements, processing currency, settlement currency, merchant category code (MCC), account status, and associated processing fees. Supports multi-merchant-account routing for marketplace and DTC channels.'
}

Table payment.reconciliation {
  reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each reconciliation run.']
  finance_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_bank_account. Business justification: Payment reconciliation matches gateway settlement records against bank account statements. The bank account is the primary reference for reconciliation; treasury and finance teams need this link to ru']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Payment reconciliation matches internal transaction records against GL account balances to identify variances. Finance close process and external audit require reconciliation records to reference the ']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity (e.g., corporate subsidiary) for which the reconciliation is performed.']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.merchant_account. Business justification: Reconciliation runs are performed for specific merchant accounts, comparing internal transaction records against external bank/gateway statements. reconciliation currently has no FK to merchant_accoun']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the monetary values.']
  exception_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the primary reason for any reconciliation exception.']
  matched_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of line items where internal transaction matched the external statement.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the financial period covered by this reconciliation.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the financial period covered by this reconciliation.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier for the reconciliation run, often used in reporting and audit trails.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reconciliation run.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the reconciliation process was executed.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reconciliation run frequency or trigger.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the external statement (e.g., payment_gateway, bank).']
  total_external_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of external bank/gateway statement records for the period.']
  total_internal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary value of internal transaction records for the period.']
  total_line_count varchar [note: 'Total number of line items processed in the reconciliation run.']
  unmatched_line_count varchar [note: 'Number of line items that did not find a matching counterpart.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between total internal and external amounts (internal - external).']

  Note: 'Tracks the daily and periodic reconciliation process between internal transaction records and external bank/gateway statements, at both run-level summary and individual line-item detail. Run attributes: reconciliation period, matched/unmatched counts, variance totals, status (open/reconciled/exception). Line-item attributes: internal transaction reference, external statement reference, match status, variance amount, exception reason code. SSOT for all reconciliation runs and their constituent line items. Supports financial close, SOX-compliant payment reconciliation, and drill-down investigation of discrepancies.'
}

Table payment.wallet {
  wallet_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the digital wallet record.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns this wallet.']
  gateway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.gateway. Business justification: Digital wallet transactions are processed through a payment gateway for top-ups, withdrawals, and transfers. wallet currently has no FK to the gateway master table. Adding gateway_id links each wallet']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Wallet balances represent a customer liability on the balance sheet and must be tracked against a GL account (deferred revenue or customer deposit liability). Finance balance sheet reporting and e-mon']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Wallets are issued under specific legal entities for e-money regulatory compliance and financial reporting. Multi-jurisdiction e-commerce platforms must attribute wallet liabilities to the correct leg']
  balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current available balance in the wallet, expressed in the wallet\'s currency.']
  compliance_token varchar [note: 'PCI‑DSS‑compliant token representing the underlying payment instrument used for funding; stored instead of raw card data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the wallet record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the wallet (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the wallet becomes inactive or expires, if applicable.']
  is_default_wallet boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this wallet is the default payment source for the owner customer.']
  last_topup_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent top‑up (funding) transaction.']
  last_transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent balance‑affecting transaction recorded for the wallet.']
  last_withdrawal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent withdrawal or redemption transaction.']
  pending_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance amount that is pending settlement (e.g., authorizations awaiting capture).']
  total_credits decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative sum of all credit‑type movements (top‑ups, refunds, promotions) to the wallet.']
  total_debits decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative sum of all debit‑type movements (purchases, withdrawals, redemptions) from the wallet.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of ledger entries recorded for this wallet.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the wallet record.']
  wallet_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the wallet (e.g., "My Store Credit").']
  wallet_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable unique wallet number assigned to the account.']
  wallet_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the wallet.']
  wallet_type varchar [note: 'Category of wallet indicating its purpose and rules (store credit, loyalty points, prepaid balance, gift, promotional).']

  Note: 'Master record of customer digital wallet accounts on the platform with complete transactional ledger of all balance movements. Wallet attributes: balance, currency, type (store credit, loyalty points, prepaid balance), funding sources, status. Transaction attributes: movement type (top-up, refund credit, promotional credit, loyalty redemption, purchase payment, withdrawal, expiry), amount, balance before/after, reference order or promotion, timestamp. SSOT for wallet accounts and their full transaction history. Provides complete audit trail for wallet balance integrity. Distinct from third-party digital wallets (Apple Pay, PayPal) which are captured as payment_method records.'
}

Table payment.token {
  token_id varchar [pk, note: 'Globally unique identifier for the token record.']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchant that owns or uses the token.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the underlying payment method (e.g., credit card, bank account) associated with the token.']
  audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit log entry that records token lifecycle events.']
  compliance_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance verification.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI‑DSS compliance status of the token.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the token record was first inserted.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for transactions using this token.']
  encryption_key_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cryptographic key used to encrypt the token.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the token becomes invalid.']
  format varchar [note: 'Technical format of the token (e.g., alphanumeric, numeric).']
  hash_algorithm varchar [note: 'Hash algorithm applied to the token for integrity verification.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the token is used for testing purposes only.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the token was created.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent transaction that used this token.']
  length varchar [note: 'Number of characters in the token.']
  method varchar [note: 'Process used to generate the token.']
  provider varchar [note: 'Entity that issued or generated the token.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Defines whether the token can be used once or multiple times.']
  secure_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'PCI‑compliant token that replaces the original PAN for transaction processing.']
  token_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the token.']
  token_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the token is a network‑issued token or a platform‑generated token.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the token record.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times the token has been used in successful transactions.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the token format or schema.']

  Note: 'PCI DSS-compliant tokenization registry mapping sensitive payment card data (PAN) to platform-generated tokens used in transaction processing. Stores token value, token type (network token vs. platform token), associated payment method reference, tokenization provider (Visa Token Service, Mastercard MDES, internal vault), token status, and expiry. Ensures raw card data never persists in application systems.'
}

Table seller.seller_profile {
  seller_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the seller profile record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for internal cost allocation reports that map each seller\'s expenses to a finance cost center for profitability analysis.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Maps sellers to the legal entity under which they operate, needed for tax filings, regulatory reporting, and jurisdictional compliance.']
  merchant_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.merchant_account. Business justification: REQUIRED: Mapping sellers to their merchant accounts enables accurate payout processing, settlement reconciliation, and regulatory reporting.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Each seller profile is assigned a default price list governing their product pricing on the platform.   profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables revenue and margin reporting by assigning each seller to a profit center, a standard practice for financial performance dashboards.']
  seller_tier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.tier. Business justification: Seller tier classification is defined in the tier reference table; replacing the string tier attribute with a foreign key enforces referential integrity and supports tier‑based analytics.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: In hybrid e-commerce platforms (marketplace + wholesale), a seller is often also a registered procurement supplier. This FK enables unified vendor health reporting, drop-ship eligibility checks, and K']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the seller\'s marketplace account.']
  business_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the seller based on size and structure.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of each sale that the marketplace retains as commission from this seller.']
  compliance_flags varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of compliance indicators (e.g., policy_violations, sanctions).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the seller profile record was first created in the system.']
  gmv_attribution decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total GMV generated by the seller that is attributed to the marketplace.']
  incorporation_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the seller is legally incorporated.']
  kyc_completed_date date [note: 'Date when KYC verification was successfully completed.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of Know‑Your‑Customer / Know‑Your‑Business verification.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the seller entity as registered.']
  notification_preferences varchar [note: 'Serialized preferences indicating which notification channels the seller opts into (e.g., email, SMS, in‑app).']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the seller onboarding workflow.']
  performance_score_bsr varchar [note: 'Numeric score derived from the seller\'s Best Seller Rank performance.']
  performance_score_dsr varchar [note: 'Numeric score reflecting the seller\'s daily sales rate performance.']
  platform_join_date date [note: 'Date the seller first joined the marketplace platform.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Language the seller prefers for communications and UI.']
  primary_category varchar [note: 'Top‑level product category the seller primarily operates in.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary point‑of‑contact for the seller.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued registration identifier for the seller (e.g., company registration, business license).']
  seller_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Overall rating score (0‑5) based on buyer feedback and performance metrics.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identifier applicable to the seller (VAT, EIN, GST, etc.).']
  trading_name varchar [note: 'Public-facing name used by the seller on the marketplace.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the seller profile record.']

  Note: 'Master record for every third-party merchant and marketplace seller entity on the platform. Stores legal business name, trading name, registration number, business type (individual/SMB/enterprise), incorporation country, tax identification numbers (VAT/EIN/GST), primary and secondary contact details (owner name, email, phone, role, notification preferences), primary category, current seller tier reference, onboarding status, account status (active/suspended/terminated), platform join date, and preferred communication language. This is the SSOT for seller identity and contact information — all other seller domain entities reference this record. Sourced from the Seller Management Portal.'
}

  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the supporting document submitted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: passport|business_license|utility_bill|tax_certificate|insurance_certificate|bank_statement|product_certification — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_upload_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the document was uploaded to the system.']
  document_uri varchar [note: 'S3 or blob storage reference where the document is stored.']

  officer_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the compliance officer reviewing the verification.']
  provider varchar [note: 'External service or internal team that performed the verification.']

  workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the verification workflow.']

  Note: 'Tracks Know Your Customer / Know Your Business (KYC/KYB) identity verification workflows and serves as the authoritative document repository for all seller-submitted compliance materials. Captures verification type (identity/business/bank/tax), verification provider, submission date, workflow status (pending/in_review/approved/rejected/expired), rejection reason codes, risk classification, and compliance officer notes. Stores associated documents: document type (passport/business_license/utility_bill/tax_certificate/insurance_certificate/bank_statement/product_certification), document name, file reference (S3/blob URI), upload date, expiry date, issuing authority, document verification status (unverified/verified/expired/rejected), and whether document is seller-submitted or platform-issued. Each seller may have multiple verification records with multiple associated documents across lifecycle events. Supports regulatory obligations under FTC, GDPR, AML/CFT, CPSC, and EU DAC7 frameworks. Single point of response for regulatory document requests and KYC audit inquiries.'
}

Supports GMV-based tiered commission models and category-specific rate overrides. Referenced by payment settlement and GMV attribution processes.'

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the scorecard record.']

  Note: 'Periodic performance scorecard record for each seller capturing key operational and commercial KPIs used for tier management and enforcement decisions. Stores scorecard period (daily/weekly/monthly), DSR (Daily Sales Rate), BSR (Best Seller Rank) within category, order defect rate (ODR), late shipment rate, cancellation rate, return rate, NPS contribution score, customer complaint count, policy violation count, GMV for period, AOV (Average Order Value), conversion rate on seller listings, inventory health score, and overall performance tier rating. Used for automated tier promotion/demotion triggers, suspension threshold monitoring, and incentive program eligibility. Sourced from Seller Management Portal performance monitoring module.'
}

  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Without marketplace_id, cross-marketpl']
  marketplace_seller_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.seller_settlement.   seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the seller to whom this GMV settlement applies.']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.settlement. Business justification: Seller GMV payouts must be reconciled against the platform\'s acquiring-bank settlement batches.   business_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the settlement processing event.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Origin channel of the sales contributing to this GMV.']
  commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Platform commission charged on the net GMV.']

  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the settlement.']
  payout_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final payout amount transferred to seller after all deductions.']
  payout_date date [note: 'Date on which payout was transferred to the seller\'s bank account.']
