  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the attribution period covered by this settlement.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the attribution period covered by this settlement.']
  promotional_subsidy_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total promotional subsidies applied to the seller\'s sales.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement record.']
  returns_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of returns and refunds deducted from gross GMV.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement was executed.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the settlement.']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the settlement is routine periodic or ad-hoc adjustment.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax collected on behalf of the seller for the period.']

  Note: 'Transactional record attributing Gross Merchandise Value (GMV) to individual sellers for each settlement period. Captures gross sales amount, returns and refunds deducted, net GMV, platform commission charged, promotional subsidy applied, tax collected on behalf of seller, currency, exchange rate, attribution period start/end dates, and GMV source channel (web/mobile/API). Supports financial reporting, seller tier evaluation, and marketplace revenue recognition. Links to order and payment domains for reconciliation.'
}

Table seller.rating {
  rating_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for rating']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Seller ratings are marketplace-specific — a seller\'s reputation score on one marketplace is independent of another. Marketplace trust and safety teams moderate ratings per marketplace; buy-box eligibi']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Rating records need to be associated with the seller being reviewed; adding seller_profile_id enables this relationship and removes the need for any redundant seller fields (none exist).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rating record was first created in the system.']
  dimension_communication varchar [note: 'Buyer’s rating of the seller’s communication quality.']
  dimension_item_as_described varchar [note: 'Buyer’s rating of how accurately the item matched its description.']
  dimension_packaging varchar [note: 'Buyer’s rating of the quality of the item’s packaging.']
  dimension_shipping_speed varchar [note: 'Buyer’s rating of how quickly the seller shipped the item.']
  helpful_votes varchar [note: 'Number of other buyers who marked the review as helpful.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 code of the language used in the review text.']
  moderation_status varchar [note: 'Current moderation state of the review: pending, approved, or removed.']
  order_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with the rating, linking the purchase to the review.']
  rating_source varchar [note: 'Channel through which the rating was submitted: website, mobile app, or API.']
  removal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by moderation team when a review is removed.']
  review_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the buyer submitted the rating.']
  review_text varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual feedback provided by the buyer.']
  score varchar [note: 'Overall rating given by the buyer on a scale of 1 (lowest) to 5 (highest).']
  seller_response_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the seller posted a response to the review.']
  seller_response_text varchar [note: 'Text of the seller’s response to the buyer’s review, if any.']
  unhelpful_votes varchar [note: 'Number of other buyers who marked the review as unhelpful.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rating record.']
  verified_purchase boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rating is tied to a completed purchase (True) or not (False).']

  Note: 'Stores buyer-submitted ratings and reviews for individual sellers. Captures rating score (1-5), review text, review date, order reference, verified purchase flag, rating dimensions (communication/shipping_speed/item_as_described/packaging), seller response text, seller response date, moderation status (pending/approved/removed), removal reason, and helpfulness votes. Aggregated ratings feed into seller scorecard and tier management. Supports NPS and CSAT tracking at the seller level.'
}

Table seller.violation {
  violation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for violation']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Seller violations with fines (fine_amount field) generate AR records — the platform has a receivable from the seller for the fine. Finance AR collections and dunning processes for seller fines require']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the compliance investigator handling the case.']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Policy violations are enforced per marketplace — a suspension on Amazon US does not automatically apply to Amazon EU. Marketplace compliance teams run violation reports scoped to their marketplace; wi']
  originating_case_service_support_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_support_case. Business justification: Compliance audit and violation investigation workflows require tracing each seller violation back to the originating support case (e.g., buyer complaint triggering a policy breach investigation). Regu']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the seller to whom the violation applies.']
  appeal_deadline date [note: 'Last date by which the seller can submit an appeal.']
  appeal_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the seller may appeal the enforcement action.']
  appeal_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the seller\'s appeal, if filed.']
  compliance_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region (ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3) governing the applicable regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the violation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for the fine amount.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'How the violation was identified: automated rule, manual review, buyer report, or regulatory notice.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the violation was first detected.']
  enforcement_action varchar [note: 'Action taken against the seller as a result of the violation.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Link (URL or internal ID) to supporting evidence such as screenshots, logs, or documents.']
  expected_reinstatement_date date [note: 'Planned date for lifting the suspension, if applicable.']
  fine_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed for the violation, if any.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the investigation workflow.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'High‑level status of the violation record across its full lifecycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations recorded by investigators.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Internal code of the marketplace policy that was breached.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the violated policy.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority whose rules are implicated by the violation.']
  reinstatement_conditions varchar [note: 'Specific requirements the seller must satisfy to have the account reinstated.']
  seller_gmv_at_violation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total GMV generated by the seller at the time the violation was recorded.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business impact rating of the violation, ranging from warning to critical.']
  suspension_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the seller\'s account suspension becomes active.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the violation record.']
  violation_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number for the violation, used in communications and reporting.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Category of policy breach (e.g., counterfeit, prohibited item, pricing manipulation, review fraud, IP infringement, data misuse).']

  Note: 'Records policy breaches, enforcement actions, and account suspension/reinstatement events for sellers who violate marketplace policies, regulatory requirements, or platform terms of service. Captures violation type (counterfeit/prohibited_item/pricing_manipulation/review_fraud/IP_infringement/data_misuse), detection method (automated_rule/manual_review/buyer_report/regulatory_notice), severity level (warning/minor/major/critical), detection date, evidence references, assigned investigator, investigation status (open/under_review/upheld/dismissed/appealed), enforcement action taken (warning_issued/listing_removed/account_suspended/account_terminated/probation_applied), suspension effective date, expected reinstatement date, appeal eligibility and deadline, appeal outcome, and reinstatement conditions. Provides complete enforcement lifecycle from detection through resolution including suspension/reinstatement audit trail. Supports FTC, CPSC, and platform trust & safety compliance.'
}

Table seller.tier {
  tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tier']
  dedicated_account_manager_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.agent. Business justification: Required for Tier Management: each seller tier assigns a dedicated account manager (service.agent) to support sellers; reports on manager performance per tier depend on this link.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Seller tiers directly determine which price list applies — Gold/Silver/Bronze tiers receive different pricing terms and fee structures. Tier-based price list assignment is a core e-commerce marketplac']
  api_rate_priority boolean [note: 'Flag indicating priority access to API rate limits.']
  badge_display_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the tier badge can be displayed on the seller\'s storefront.']
  commission_rate_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the standard commission rate for sellers in this tier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tier record was created.']
  demotion_grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after a downgrade trigger before the tier is actually changed.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the tier definition becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the tier definition is retired; null if still active.']
  eligibility_criteria_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes describing any additional eligibility criteria for the tier.']
  enhanced_analytics_access boolean [note: 'Flag indicating access to advanced analytics dashboards.']
  is_default_tier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier is the default assignment for new sellers.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last tier review execution.']
  minimum_gmv_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum Gross Merchandise Value in USD a seller must achieve to qualify for this tier.']
  minimum_scorecard_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum average scorecard rating (0‑5) required for tier eligibility.']
  minimum_tenure_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days the seller must have been active on the platform to qualify.']
  premium_feature_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sellers in this tier have access to premium platform features.']
  review_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the seller\'s tier is reviewed.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Unique short code identifying the seller tier (e.g., BRZ, SLV, GLD).']
  tier_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tier, its purpose and benefits.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the seller tier such as Bronze, Silver, Gold.']
  tier_rank varchar [note: 'Ordinal rank of the tier; lower number indicates a higher tier.']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tier record.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the marketplace seller tier classification hierarchy. Captures tier name (Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum/Enterprise), qualification thresholds (minimum GMV, minimum scorecard rating, minimum tenure), benefits granted (commission rate discount percentage, premium feature access flags, dedicated account manager eligibility, API rate priority, enhanced analytics access), badge display eligibility, tier review cadence (monthly/quarterly), demotion grace period rules, and tier-specific SLA commitments. Provides the authoritative classification framework referenced by profile for current tier assignment, by scorecard for promotion/demotion evaluation, and by commission for rate resolution.'
}

  account_number varchar [note: 'Tokenized or masked bank account number stored in PCI‑DSS compliant vault.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank account (e.g., checking, savings, business).']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution where the account is held.']

  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the bank account is domiciled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bank account record was first created in the system.']

  date_verified date [note: 'Calendar date on which the bank account was successfully verified.']
  disbursement_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount that can be disbursed to this account per transaction or period.']
  disbursement_limit_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the disbursement limit amount.']

  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent payout made to this bank account.']
  primary_payout_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account is the default payout method for the seller.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Domestic routing identifier for the bank (e.g., ABA routing number in the US).']

  tokenization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to protect the account number (e.g., vault, detokenize, none).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bank account record.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification state of the bank account (pending, verified, or failed).']
  verification_status_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the verification status was last updated.']

  Note: 'Stores verified bank account and payment disbursement method records for each seller. Captures account holder name, bank name, account type (checking/savings/business), account number (tokenized/masked per PCI DSS), routing number, IBAN, SWIFT/BIC code, currency, country, verification status (pending/verified/failed), primary payout flag, and date verified. A seller may have multiple bank accounts across currencies. Directly referenced by seller_payout for disbursement routing. PCI DSS-compliant storage with tokenization.'
}

Table fulfillment.center {
  center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fulfillment center. Primary key for the center entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Maps each fulfillment center to its operational cost center, essential for budgeting, cost allocation, and expense tracking.']
  default_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Default carrier per fulfillment center drives automated carrier selection in the \'Shipment Creation\' workflow.']
  manager_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.agent. Business justification: Each fulfillment center is overseen by a manager agent responsible for operations; linking enables manager‑level performance dashboards and incident escalation.']
  operator_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: 3PL-operated fulfillment centers must be linked to their supplier record for contract management, performance scoring, and invoice reconciliation. partner_operator_name is a denormalized text field th']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Fulfillment centers operate within geographic price zones that govern regional shipping rates and delivery cost structures. E-commerce domain experts expect each fulfillment center to be mapped to a p']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Fulfillment centers are managed as profit centers in e-commerce operations — each center\'s revenue, cost, and margin are tracked separately for management reporting. center has cost_center_id but no p']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Each 3PL-operated fulfillment center is governed by a specific vendor contract covering capacity commitments, service levels, and fees. Direct FK enables contract renewal tracking, expiry alerts, and ']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Maps each fulfillment center to its physical warehouse node, essential for inventory balance reports and location‑based replenishment.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the fulfillment center physical location, including street number and name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as building, suite, or floor number.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation in fulfillment operations: manual (human-driven), semi-automated (mix of manual and automated processes), fully automated (end-to-end automation), or robotic (advanced robotics and AI).']
  available_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Current available storage capacity in the fulfillment center, representing unused storage units available for new inventory allocation.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement used to quantify storage capacity: pallets, bins, cubic feet, cubic meters, square feet, or square meters.']
  center_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the fulfillment center, used in operational systems and partner integrations.']
  center_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the fulfillment center, used for display and reporting purposes.']
  center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fulfillment center based on operational model: FBA-style (Fulfillment By Amazon-style), 3PL (Third-Party Logistics), BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) hub, dark store, micro-fulfillment center, or returns processing center.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the fulfillment center is located.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the fulfillment center ceased operations, applicable only if the center has been decommissioned or permanently closed.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the fulfillment center operations team, used for internal coordination and partner communication.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the fulfillment center operations team, used for internal coordination and partner communication.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country where the fulfillment center is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fulfillment center record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  cutoff_time_local varchar [note: 'Daily cutoff time in local time zone (HH:MM format) by which orders must be received to qualify for same-day processing and shipment.']
  daily_throughput_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of orders or units the fulfillment center can process per day under normal operating conditions, used for capacity planning and order allocation.']
  erp_site_code varchar [note: 'Site or plant code in the ERP system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA) that corresponds to this fulfillment center, used for financial and supply chain integration.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the fulfillment center location, used for distance calculations and route optimization.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the fulfillment center location, used for distance calculations and route optimization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or facility-specific information relevant to fulfillment operations.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date when the fulfillment center commenced operations and began processing orders.']
  operates_24_7 boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the fulfillment center operates continuously (true) or has scheduled operating hours (false).']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the fulfillment center if not 24/7, formatted as day ranges and time ranges (e.g., Mon-Fri 08:00-18:00, Sat 09:00-15:00).']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the fulfillment center in its lifecycle: active (fully operational), inactive (not processing orders), under construction (pre-launch), temporarily closed (planned downtime), decommissioned (permanently closed), or seasonal (operates during peak periods only).']
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'Ownership and operational model of the fulfillment center: owned (company-owned and operated), leased (company-operated but leased facility), third-party operated (3PL partner), or hybrid (shared operations).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the fulfillment center address, used for shipping and logistics routing.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service level tier assigned to this fulfillment center, defining performance commitments for order processing and delivery speed: premium (fastest), standard, economy, regional, express, or custom.']
  standard_processing_time_hours varchar [note: 'Standard time in hours from order receipt to shipment dispatch under normal operating conditions, used for SLA calculations and customer promise dates.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the fulfillment center is located.']
  supported_fulfillment_methods varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of fulfillment methods supported by this center: standard_shipping, expedited_shipping, same_day_delivery, next_day_delivery, bopis, ship_from_store, white_glove, etc.']
  supports_cold_storage boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the fulfillment center has refrigerated or frozen storage capabilities for temperature-sensitive products.']
  supports_hazmat boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the fulfillment center is certified and equipped to handle hazardous materials shipments.']
  supports_returns_processing boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the fulfillment center is equipped to receive, inspect, and process customer returns and RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) requests.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the fulfillment center location, used for scheduling and SLA calculations.']
  tms_integration_reference varchar [note: 'External system identifier used to integrate with the Transportation Management System (TMS) for carrier selection and shipment tracking.']
  total_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Maximum storage capacity of the fulfillment center measured in storage units (pallets, bins, or cubic feet), representing the total inventory holding capacity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fulfillment center record was last modified, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  wms_integration_reference varchar [note: 'External system identifier used to integrate with the WMS for real-time inventory synchronization and order orchestration.']
  wms_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Warehouse Management System (WMS) software deployed at this fulfillment center for inventory and operations management.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical fulfillment center (FC) or warehouse node in the fulfillment network. Captures FC identity, type (FBA-style, 3PL, BOPIS hub, dark store), geographic location, operational capacity, supported fulfillment methods, WMS integration identifiers, and SLA tier assignments. SSOT for all warehouse facility master data within the fulfillment domain.'
}

Table fulfillment.fulfillment_order {
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fulfillment order. Primary key for the fulfillment order entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: B2B e-commerce fulfillment orders are placed against a customer account for credit limit validation, account-level order history, and B2B invoicing. A domain expert expects fulfillment_order to refere']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: AR is created upon fulfillment completion in e-commerce. Finance teams reconcile AR aging against fulfilled orders to confirm cash application. accounts_receivable.header_id links to order header, but']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle fulfillment is a distinct e-commerce process requiring kit assembly, multi-SKU picking coordination, and bundle-level SLA tracking. Operations teams need to identify which fulfillment orders ar']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Required for order‑to‑campaign ROI attribution report; e‑commerce analysts need to trace each fulfillment order back to the marketing campaign that generated it.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Order‑level carrier assignment is used in the \'Order Fulfillment Planning\' process and carrier performance dashboards.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Tracking the specific carrier service (e.g., Express, Ground) per order supports SLA compliance reporting and rate calculations.']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: Links fulfillment orders to the structured customer address record for carrier serviceability checks (serviceable_by_carrier), BOPIS eligibility validation, and address-level fulfillment analytics. Sn']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who placed the order being fulfilled. Links to the Customer master data entity.']
  fulfillment_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: A fulfillment order is issued to a specific fulfillment center for execution. This is a core operational relationship — every fulfillment instruction must be assigned to a physical FC node. No center_']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order in the Order Management System (OMS) that triggered this fulfillment instruction.']
  product_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_listing. Business justification: Fulfillment orders originate from a specific product listing (channel, condition, seller). Marketplace sellers track fulfillment performance per listing for SLA compliance, buy-box eligibility, and ch']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables order‑level profit attribution, allowing profitability analysis per profit center in financial statements.']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Fulfillment orders generated during promotional campaigns (flash sales, event-driven surges) must reference the pricing promotional_campaign for SLA prioritization, cost allocation, and campaign perfo']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_sla. Business justification: Each fulfillment order is governed by a specific SLA definition (fulfillment_sla) that determines pick/pack/ship time targets and breach thresholds based on fulfillment method, carrier service level, ']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment center or warehouse node assigned to execute this fulfillment order. Links to the warehouse node master data.']
  allocated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when inventory was successfully allocated to this fulfillment order in the WMS. Marks transition from received to allocated status.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason why this fulfillment order was cancelled. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the fulfillment order was cancelled. Triggers inventory de-allocation and order management system notification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this fulfillment order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  delivered_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the package was successfully delivered to the customer destination address. Sourced from carrier proof-of-delivery records.']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'The fulfillment execution strategy for this order. Determines Service Level Agreement (SLA) targets, routing logic, and carrier selection. BOPIS = Buy Online Pick Up In Store, FBA = Fulfillment By Amazon-style, 3PL = Third-Party Logistics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|expedited|same_day|next_day|bopis|fba|3pl — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fulfillment_order_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the fulfillment order, typically displayed on packing slips, shipping labels, and in Warehouse Management System (WMS) interfaces.']
  fulfillment_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fulfillment order. Tracks progression through warehouse workflow stages from receipt to delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|allocated|picking|packed|shipped|delivered|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  gift_message varchar [note: 'Optional personalized message to be included with gift orders. Printed on packing slip or gift card insert.']
  is_gift boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this order is a gift. When true, pricing information is excluded from packing slip and gift wrapping may be applied.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order based on business context. B2C = Business to Consumer, B2B = Business to Business. Determines packing requirements, documentation, and invoicing rules.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'The number of individual packages or parcels created for this fulfillment order. Orders may be split into multiple packages based on size, weight, or inventory location.']
  packing_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when all items were packed, shipping label applied, and the package was ready for carrier pickup.']
  packing_slip_url varchar [note: 'URL to the generated packing slip document that accompanies the shipment. Contains itemized order details for customer reference.']
  packing_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when packing operations began for this fulfillment order at the packing station.']
  picking_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when all items for this fulfillment order were successfully picked from warehouse locations.']
  picking_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when warehouse personnel began picking items for this fulfillment order. Marks start of physical fulfillment execution.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for warehouse pick-pack-ship sequencing. High and urgent priorities are expedited through the fulfillment workflow.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the order is promised to be delivered to the customer. Used for SLA tracking and customer communication.']
  promised_ship_date date [note: 'The date by which the fulfillment center committed to ship this order to meet customer Service Level Agreement (SLA) expectations.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the fulfillment order was received by the Warehouse Management System (WMS) from the Order Management System (OMS).']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the person or business receiving the shipment. Printed on shipping label for carrier delivery verification.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the shipment recipient. Used by carriers for delivery coordination and customer notification.']
  shipped_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the package was handed over to the carrier and departed the fulfillment center. Triggers customer shipment notification.']
  shipping_address_line1 varchar [note: 'The primary street address line for the delivery destination. Contains street number, street name, and unit/apartment number if applicable.']
  shipping_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional delivery instructions, building name, or suite information.']
  shipping_city varchar [note: 'The city or municipality name for the delivery destination address.']
  shipping_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the delivery destination. Determines customs requirements and international shipping regulations.']
  shipping_label_url varchar [note: 'URL to the generated shipping label document stored in the Content Delivery Network (CDN) or document management system.']
  shipping_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal code or ZIP code for the delivery destination address. Used for carrier routing and delivery zone determination.']
  shipping_state_province varchar [note: 'The state, province, or administrative region for the delivery destination address.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for special handling requirements, delivery instructions, or customer notes. Examples: fragile, signature required, leave at door.']
  total_item_quantity varchar [note: 'The total number of individual units (across all Stock Keeping Units or SKUs) included in this fulfillment order.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(10,4) [note: 'The total packed volume of the fulfillment order in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space planning and transportation optimization.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The total packed weight of the fulfillment order in kilograms. Used for carrier rate calculation and capacity planning.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-provided tracking number for this shipment. Used for customer self-service tracking and proof-of-delivery verification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this fulfillment order record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']

  Note: 'Core operational record representing a fulfillment instruction issued to a warehouse or 3PL partner for a specific customer order or sub-order. Tracks fulfillment order status (received, allocated, picking, packed, shipped, delivered, cancelled), assigned fulfillment center, fulfillment method (standard, expedited, BOPIS, FBA-style, 3PL), promised SLA window, actual pick/pack/ship timestamps, and WMS/OMS reference identifiers. Central entity for pick-pack-ship orchestration.'
}

Table fulfillment.order_line {
  order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each line item within a fulfillment order. Primary key for the order line entity.']
  bin_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.bin_location. Business justification: During the pick operation, each fulfillment order line is picked from a specific physical bin location within the fulfillment center. Linking fulfillment_order_line to bin_location enables pick-path o']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle assembly picking requires each order line to know which parent bundle it belongs to. Warehouse systems use this to group component picks, enforce bundle completeness before packing, and report ']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Internal product master identifier linking this line to the product catalog. Used for product attribute enrichment and reporting.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fulfillment order that contains this line item. Links line-level detail to the order header.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Line-level fulfillment traceability: each fulfillment_order_line must map to its originating order_line for partial fulfillment tracking, returns eligibility, revenue recognition per line, and fulfill']
  listing_offer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.listing_offer. Business justification: Supports Offer Performance Analytics by linking each order line to the specific listing offer purchased, enabling pricing and conversion analysis.']
  po_line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line_item. Business justification: Each sales order line is sourced from a specific PO line item; linking enables cost allocation, inventory traceability, and three‑way matching.']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Traceability of the exact price list item used for each order line; needed for the Line‑Level Price Audit report.']
  price_override_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_override. Business justification: Individual fulfillment order lines may carry price overrides (manual discounts, B2B negotiated prices, CS adjustments). Linking to price_override enables audit trails and approval workflow tracking fo']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Required for the \'Pick and Pack\' process to map each order line to the exact SKU for inventory allocation and performance reporting.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: In e-commerce WMS pick operations, each order line resolves to a specific stock_position (lot, condition, location) for ATP validation, inventory deduction, and lot traceability. fulfillment_order_lin']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific bin, slot, or location within the warehouse where the item was picked from. Critical for inventory accuracy and replenishment.']
  allocated_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of inventory allocated to this order line from warehouse stock. May differ from ordered quantity due to availability constraints.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the pick has been confirmed and validated. Used for quality control and accuracy verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order line record was first created in the system. Marks the beginning of the line item lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the line item pricing. Supports multi-currency fulfillment operations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the picked item. Critical for perishable goods and FEFO inventory rotation.']
  gift_message varchar [note: 'Customer-provided gift message to be included with this line item. Contains personal message content for gift orders.']
  gift_wrap_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether gift wrapping service was requested for this line item. True if gift wrap required, false otherwise.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and shipping compliance. True if hazardous, false otherwise.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of this order line. Tracks the line through the pick-pack-ship workflow lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PENDING|ALLOCATED|PICKING|PICKED|PACKED|SHIPPED|CANCELLED|SHORT_PICK — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  line_total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value for this line item calculated as unit price multiplied by packed quantity. Represents the line-level revenue.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number of the picked item. Critical for traceability, recalls, and expiration management.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU originally ordered by the customer. Represents the demand that must be fulfilled.']
  pack_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this line item was packed into a shipping container. Indicates readiness for shipment.']
  packed_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity successfully packed into shipping containers. Represents the final quantity being shipped to the customer.']
  pick_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pick task for this line item was completed. Marks the end of the picking phase for this line.']
  pick_exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any exception or issue encountered during the pick process. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  pick_exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context about pick exceptions or issues. Captured by warehouse associates for investigation.']
  pick_method varchar [note: 'The picking strategy used for this line item. Determines how the pick task was organized and executed within the WMS workflow.']
  pick_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pick task for this line item was initiated. Used for calculating pick duration and SLA compliance.']
  picked_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual quantity picked by warehouse associate or automation system. May differ from allocated quantity due to short picks or inventory discrepancies.']
  return_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is eligible for return based on product category and business rules. True if returnable, false otherwise.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the individual item picked. Used for high-value or serialized inventory tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated or last updated this order line record. Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Any special handling requirements or instructions for this line item such as fragile, keep refrigerated, or this side up.']
  substituted_sku varchar [note: 'The SKU of the substitute product that was picked in place of the originally ordered SKU. Null if no substitution occurred.']
  substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a product substitution was made for this line item due to stockout or customer preference. True if substituted, false otherwise.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which quantities are measured for this line item. Defines how the product is counted and handled in the warehouse.']
  unit_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'Price per unit for this line item at the time of order placement. Used for order value calculation and financial reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order line record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  volume_cubic_meters decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total volume of the picked quantity for this line item in cubic meters. Used for cartonization and space optimization.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the picked quantity for this line item in kilograms. Used for shipping cost calculation and carrier selection.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each SKU within a fulfillment order, serving as the unified pick-and-allocate record. Captures SKU identifier, ordered/allocated/picked/packed quantities, unit of measure, bin/slot location at time of pick, substitution flags, line-level fulfillment status, assigned picker (associate ID or robot unit), pick zone, pick method (single-order, batch, zone, wave), pick start/completion timestamps, pick exceptions (short pick, mispick), and confirmation status. SSOT for SKU-level fulfillment execution including pick task assignment and completion within the WMS workflow.'
}

Table fulfillment.packing_slip {
  packing_slip_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the packing slip record. Primary key for the packing slip entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Carrier assignment for packing slips is required for carrier billing and performance reporting; linking provides authoritative carrier data.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: A packing slip is the operational document for the packing lifecycle of a fulfillment order. The direct link to fulfillment_order (the instruction) is essential for packing workflow management and ord']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment record associated with this packing slip. Links the packed carton to the outbound shipment.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fulfillment order that this packing slip belongs to. Links the packing slip to the originating customer order.']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment center or warehouse where the packing operation was performed.']
  actual_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual measured weight of the packed carton in kilograms, including all contents and packing materials. Captured at the pack station scale.']
  branded_insert_included boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether branded marketing inserts, promotional materials, or catalog cards were included in the shipment.']
  carton_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or barcode of the specific carton or container used for this shipment. Used for tracking and inventory management of packing materials.']
  carton_type varchar [note: 'Type or size classification of the shipping carton or box used for packing. Examples include small box, medium box, large box, envelope, poly mailer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packing slip record was first created in the system. Represents the initial record creation for audit and tracking purposes.']
  customer_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field containing any customer-provided delivery instructions or notes that should be visible to the packer or carrier. Examples include gate codes, delivery preferences, or special requests.']
  dimensional_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Calculated dimensional weight of the packed carton in kilograms, based on carton dimensions. Used for carrier billing and rate calculation.']
  document_generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packing slip document was generated and printed. Represents the official creation time of the customer-facing packing slip.']
  gift_message_included boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a gift message was included in the shipment. True if a gift message card or note was packed with the order.']
  gift_wrap_applied boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether gift wrapping was applied to the items in this shipment. True if gift wrap service was requested and applied.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, or carrier compliance. True if any packed item is classified as hazmat.']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the packed carton in centimeters. Used for dimensional weight calculation and carrier compliance.']
  invoice_included boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a printed invoice or receipt was included in the shipment. Typically false for B2C gift orders, true for B2B orders.']
  length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the packed carton in centimeters. Used for dimensional weight calculation and carrier compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packing slip record was last modified or updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  pack_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packing operation was completed and the carton was sealed. Used for cycle time analysis and productivity tracking.']
  pack_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the packing operation began at the pack station. Used for cycle time analysis and productivity tracking.']
  packing_exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any exception or issue encountered during the packing process, such as damaged item, missing item, substitution made, or oversized package. Null if no exceptions occurred.']
  packing_exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any packing exceptions or issues, providing additional context beyond the exception code. Null if no exceptions occurred.']
  packing_materials_used varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of packing materials used in the shipment, such as bubble wrap, air pillows, packing peanuts, paper fill, or dunnage. Used for cost tracking and sustainability reporting.']
  packing_slip_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the packing slip document. Printed on the physical packing slip and used for customer service reference.']
  packing_slip_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the packing slip record. Tracks the progression from draft through completion and any subsequent actions like reprinting or voiding.']
  qc_scan_result varchar [note: 'Result of the quality control scan or verification performed during or after packing. Indicates whether the packed contents match the order requirements.']
  qc_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality control scan or verification was completed. Null if QC was not performed.']
  return_label_included boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a prepaid return shipping label was included in the shipment for customer convenience.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Shipping service level or speed selected for this shipment. Examples include standard ground, two-day, next-day, same-day, or economy.']
  shipping_label_printed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shipping label has been printed for this packing slip. True once the label is generated and printed at the pack station.']
  signature_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a delivery signature is required for this shipment. True for high-value or restricted items.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field containing any special handling instructions for the packing operation, such as fragile item handling, temperature control, or hazmat requirements.']
  total_item_quantity varchar [note: 'Total count of individual items (units) packed in this carton across all SKUs. Sum of quantities for all line items in the packing slip.']
  total_sku_count varchar [note: 'Total count of distinct SKUs packed in this carton. Represents the variety of products in the shipment.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for the shipment. Used by customers and customer service to track package delivery status.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the packed carton in centimeters. Used for dimensional weight calculation and carrier compliance.']

  Note: 'Consolidated operational record and SSOT for the full packing lifecycle of a fulfillment order — from pack station assignment and carton selection through QC verification and packing slip document generation. Stores pack station ID, packer associate, carton/box type, dimensional and actual weight, packing materials, QC scan results, packed SKUs with quantities, special handling instructions, gift message and branded insert flags, pack start/end timestamps, and generation timestamp. Serves as both the operational pack execution record (replacing standalone pack task) and the customer-facing documentation reference for shipment contents and returns processing.'
}

Table fulfillment.shipping_label {
  shipping_label_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the shipping label record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Shipping label generation needs carrier reference for tracking, customs, and cost allocation; FK ensures consistency with carrier master.']
  carrier_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_rate_card. Business justification: Carrier cost reconciliation and rate audit: each shipping label is generated using a specific rate card. Finance and ops teams run carrier invoice reconciliation reports that require tracing which rat']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Label rate selection and SLA compliance reporting: a shipping label is generated for a specific carrier service (e.g., overnight, ground). Carrier performance dashboards and SLA breach analysis requir']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: A shipping label is generated for a fulfillment order\'s outbound shipment. The direct in-domain link to fulfillment_order enables label-to-order traceability without requiring a join through fulfillme']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the outbound shipment associated with this label.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with this shipping label.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Any surcharge, discount, or fee applied to the base carrier rate.']
  carrier_rate_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base rate quoted by the carrier before adjustments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipping label record was first created in the system.']
  customs_declaration_flag boolean [note: 'True if a customs declaration is required for the shipment.']
  customs_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Declared monetary value for customs purposes.']
  destination_address_snapshot varchar [note: 'Serialized snapshot of the destination address at label generation time.']
  dimensions_cm varchar [note: 'Package dimensions formatted as LxWxH in centimeters.']
  generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipping label was generated.']
  insurance_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the insurance coverage for the shipment.']
  insurance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is insured.']
  label_format varchar [note: 'File format of the generated shipping label.']
  label_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the shipping label, used for tracking and reference.']
  manual_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a user manually overrode the automated carrier selection.']
  origin_address_snapshot varchar [note: 'Serialized snapshot of the origin address at label generation time.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Physical packaging type used for the shipment.']
  rate_basis varchar [note: 'Basis used for carrier rate calculation (weight, dimensional weight, or zone).']
  rate_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the carrier rate.']
  selection_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or rule used to select the carrier for this label.']
  selection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier selection (automated or manual) occurred.']
  shipping_label_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shipping label.']
  tms_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this label to the TMS transaction or batch.']
  total_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Final cost of the shipping label after adjustments, in the specified currency.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking number assigned by the carrier for shipment visibility.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipping label record.']
  void_flag boolean [note: 'True if the label has been voided or cancelled.']
  void_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the label was voided, if applicable.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,3) [note: 'Weight of the package in kilograms used for rate calculation.']

  Note: 'Master record and SSOT for each shipping label generated for an outbound shipment, incorporating the full carrier selection, rate-shopping, and label generation lifecycle. Stores carrier SCAC code, tracking number, label format (ZPL, PDF), service level (ground, 2-day, overnight, same-day), origin and destination address snapshots, label generation timestamp, carrier rate quoted, rate basis (weight, dimensional weight, zone), carrier selection method (cheapest, fastest, preferred, TMS-optimized), manual override flag, selection timestamp, label void status, and TMS reference identifier. Unified record for carrier assignment decision and physical label artifact; integrates with TMS for carrier selection and rate shopping.'
}

  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment record.']
  accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Each shipment generates a carrier freight invoice that creates an AP record. Three-way matching (shipment proof + carrier invoice + AP record) is a standard freight audit process. Direct shipment→AP l']
  carrier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_contract. Business justification: Carrier contract compliance and volume commitment tracking: e-commerce operators must report shipment volumes against contracted minimums and verify SLA terms per contract.   carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to logistics.carrier']
  carrier_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_rate_card. Business justification: Freight audit and carrier invoice reconciliation: fulfillment_shipment records shipping_cost_gross/net/tax but has no FK to the rate card that produced those costs. Finance teams require this link to ']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Carrier performance reporting and freight cost allocation by service tier: fulfillment_shipment has carrier_id but no carrier_service_id. service_level and shipping_method are denormalized representat']

  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: Links shipments to the structured customer address for failed delivery tracking (failed_delivery_count on customer_address), carrier serviceability reporting, and address-level shipment performance an']
