This is a fundamental operational link for FC-level shipment tracking, throughput reporting, and SLA monitoring. No center_id e']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: A fulfillment shipment is the physical execution of a fulfillment order. This direct in-domain link is essential for end-to-end fulfillment traceability — connecting the instruction (fulfillment_order']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Order‑to‑Shipment association needed for shipping status dashboard and carrier billing reconciliation.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Required for shipment‑payment reconciliation report linking each shipment to the payment transaction that funded it.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Shipping cost calculations reference a price list; required for the Shipment Cost Allocation process.']
  price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Shipment shipping costs are calculated based on the destination price zone in e-commerce rate engines. Linking fulfillment_shipment to price_zone enables accurate shipping cost attribution, carrier ra']
  tax_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_record. Business justification: Shipping tax (shipping_cost_tax) must be linked to a tax_record for marketplace facilitator tax compliance and remittance reporting. E-commerce tax nexus rules require shipment-level tax tracking. No ']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse from which the shipment originated.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when the shipment was actually delivered.']
  carrier_tracking_url varchar [note: 'Web URL to the carrier\'s tracking page for this shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the system.']
  declared_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for customs and liability purposes.']
  declared_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the declared value.']
  delivery_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of delivery attempts made for this shipment.']
  delivery_status_detail varchar [note: 'Additional detail about the delivery outcome (e.g., left with neighbor, held at depot).']
  destination_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the shipment destination address.']
  destination_city varchar [note: 'City component of the destination address.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the destination.']
  destination_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the destination address.']
  destination_state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the destination address.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Projected date when the shipment is expected to be delivered.']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'Classification of freight for pricing and handling (e.g., Class 70).']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms of payment for freight charges.']
  fulfillment_shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height of the shipment package in centimeters.']
  insurance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is insured.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of the shipment package in centimeters.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual packages in the shipment.']
  proof_of_delivery_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the proof-of-delivery document or image.']
  return_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for a returned shipment, if applicable.']
  ship_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment was dispatched from the fulfillment center.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the shipment for tracking and reference.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is a normal outbound, a return, or an internal transfer.']
  shipping_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the shipping cost.']
  shipping_cost_gross decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total shipping cost before taxes and adjustments.']
  shipping_cost_net decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net shipping cost after tax adjustments.']
  shipping_cost_tax decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component of the shipping cost.']
  shipping_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the shipment.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking identifier provided by the carrier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment record.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the shipment in kilograms.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width of the shipment package in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record representing a physical outbound shipment dispatched from a fulfillment center to a customer or intermediate hub. Tracks shipment status (manifested, in-transit, out-for-delivery, delivered, exception, returned), carrier, tracking number, ship date, estimated delivery date, actual delivery date, shipment weight, dimensions, declared value, and proof-of-delivery reference. Bridges fulfillment execution and last-mile logistics; integrates with TMS for carrier tracking events.'
}

Table fulfillment.shipment_event {
  shipment_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment tracking event record. Primary key for the shipment event log.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation carrier (e.g., FedEx, UPS, USPS, DHL) that reported this tracking event. Links to carrier master data.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse, fulfillment center, or carrier facility where the event occurred. Links to facility master data for internal events.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment container that this event belongs to. Links this tracking event to the overall shipment record.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer order associated with this shipment event. Enables order-level tracking and SLA monitoring.']
  carrier_event_code varchar [note: 'The carrier-specific status code or event identifier from the carrier\'s tracking system. Raw code as provided by carrier API or EDI message.']
  carrier_event_description varchar [note: 'The human-readable description of the tracking event as provided by the carrier. Raw scan message or status text from carrier system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this shipment event record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field for data governance and compliance.']
  delivery_location_type varchar [note: 'The specific location where the package was left upon delivery. Applicable only to delivered events. Provides proof-of-delivery detail for customer service and claims. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: front_door|back_door|side_door|mailroom|reception|neighbor|parcel_locker|carrier_facility — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'The carrier\'s current estimated delivery date for the shipment as of this event. Updated dynamically as the shipment progresses. Used for customer communication and SLA tracking.']
  event_location_city varchar [note: 'The city where the tracking event was recorded by the carrier. May be null for certain event types that do not have precise location data.']
  event_location_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the tracking event occurred (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Supports international shipment tracking.']
  event_location_postal_code varchar [note: 'The postal or ZIP code of the location where the tracking event was recorded. Enables geographic analysis of shipment flow.']
  event_location_state varchar [note: 'The state or province where the tracking event occurred. Uses standard postal abbreviations (e.g., CA, NY, TX).']
  event_source varchar [note: 'The system or channel through which the tracking event was received: carrier_api (direct carrier integration), tms_webhook (Transportation Management System callback), edi_214 (Electronic Data Interchange shipment status), manual_entry (operator input), wms_scan (Warehouse Management System scan).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the tracking event occurred in the carrier\'s system. This is the real-world event time, not the system ingestion time.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The category of tracking event representing the shipment milestone: picked_up (carrier collected package), in_transit (package moving through network), out_for_delivery (on vehicle for final delivery), delivered (successfully received), exception (delay or issue), attempted_delivery (delivery tried but failed).']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the exception condition if is_exception is True. Describes the nature of the delay, damage, or delivery issue. Null for non-exception events.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this event record was ingested into the data platform. Distinct from event_timestamp (when the event actually occurred). Used for data lineage and latency monitoring.']
  is_customer_visible boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this event should be displayed to the customer in tracking interfaces. False for internal-only events (warehouse scans, carrier facility transfers).']
  is_exception boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this event represents an exception or problem condition (delay, damage, lost package, address issue, weather delay, etc.). True if exception, False for normal milestone events.']
  is_sla_breach boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this event represents or contributes to an SLA breach. True if the event timestamp exceeds the SLA target, False otherwise.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the event location in decimal degrees. Enables precise geospatial analysis and mapping of shipment routes.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the event location in decimal degrees. Used with latitude for route optimization and delivery zone analysis.']
  raw_payload varchar [note: 'The complete raw JSON or XML payload received from the carrier API, TMS webhook, or EDI message. Preserved for audit, troubleshooting, and reprocessing. May contain additional carrier-specific metadata.']
  signature_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who signed for the delivery. Captured for delivered events requiring proof of delivery. Null for events that do not involve signature capture.']
  sla_target_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The promised delivery date and time per the service level agreement. Used to detect SLA breaches by comparing against actual delivery timestamp.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient or package temperature in degrees Celsius recorded at the time of the event. Critical for cold-chain shipments (pharmaceuticals, perishables). Null for non-temperature-controlled shipments.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment. Used for customer-facing tracking and carrier API queries.']
  weight_actual_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The actual measured weight of the shipment in kilograms as recorded during this event. May be captured at pickup, in-transit weighing, or delivery. Used for freight cost reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Immutable event log capturing carrier-reported and internal tracking milestones for a shipment throughout its transit lifecycle. Each record represents a discrete tracking event: event type (picked up, in-transit, out-for-delivery, delivered, exception, attempted delivery), event timestamp, event location (city, state, country), carrier event code, raw carrier scan message, and data source (carrier API, TMS webhook, EDI 214). Enables SLA breach detection and customer-facing tracking updates.'
}

Table fulfillment.proof_of_delivery {
  proof_of_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the proof of delivery record.']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: POD records must reference the structured customer address to update failed_delivery_count, validate delivery confirmation against the registered address, and support address-level delivery success ra']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party who received the shipment.']
  delivery_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery_confirmation. Business justification: POD reconciliation: proof_of_delivery (carrier-side record) must be reconciled against delivery_confirmation (order-side record) for customer dispute resolution, SLA compliance audits, and chargeback ']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: A proof of delivery confirms the delivery of a fulfillment order. The direct in-domain link to fulfillment_order provides clean order-level delivery confirmation traceability and supports SLA complian']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment that this proof of delivery belongs to.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Proof‑of‑Delivery must be tied to originating order for compliance audit and customer dispute resolution.']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Buyer protection and seller settlement confirmation: POD records close marketplace transactions for payment release and dispute prevention. Marketplace ops teams need direct POD-to-transaction traceab']
  revenue_recognition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.revenue_recognition. Business justification: Under ASC 606/IFRS 15, delivery confirmation (POD) is the performance obligation satisfaction event triggering revenue recognition in e-commerce. Auditors require a direct audit trail from POD to reve']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier code of the carrier that performed the final delivery.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proof of delivery record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the delivery location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA — promote to reference product]']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Mode of delivery used for the order.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery was confirmed (actual event time).']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an exception occurred during delivery (e.g., failed attempt, damage).']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes describing any delivery exception details.']
  gps_accuracy_meters varchar [note: 'Reported accuracy of the GPS coordinate in meters.']
  is_bopis boolean [note: 'True if the delivery was part of a Buy‑Online‑Pick‑Up‑In‑Store fulfillment.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery location.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery location.']
  otp_verified boolean [note: 'True if a one‑time password was successfully verified at delivery.']
  photo_captured boolean [note: 'True if a photo of the delivered package was captured.']
  pod_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the proof‑of‑delivery data format.']
  proof_asset_url varchar [note: 'Link to the digital asset (image, signature file, etc.) that serves as proof.']
  proof_of_delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery proof record.']
  proof_type varchar [note: 'Method used to capture delivery proof (e.g., signature, photo, GPS coordinate, one‑time password).']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the delivery recipient.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the delivery recipient.']
  signature_captured boolean [note: 'True if a handwritten signature was captured as proof.']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes taken to complete the delivery.']
  sla_compliance varchar [note: 'Result of SLA evaluation for this delivery.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target service‑level agreement time in minutes for delivery completion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the proof of delivery record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing confirmed delivery evidence for a shipment. Stores confirmation type (signature, photo, GPS, OTP), recipient name, delivery timestamp, location coordinates, photo/signature asset references, carrier confirmation code, and exception notes. Critical for dispute resolution, chargeback defense, RMA initiation, and last-mile SLA compliance.'
}

Table fulfillment.bin_location {
  bin_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each physical storage location.']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: Bin locations belong to a specific fulfillment center; adding center_id FK enables hierarchy and eliminates silo.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: bin_location (fulfillment WES) and warehouse_location (inventory WMS) represent the same physical space in two domain systems. E-commerce operations require cross-system location reconciliation for cy']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fulfillment center or warehouse that owns this bin.']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier of the aisle containing the bin.']
  bay varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier of the bay within the aisle.']
  bin_location_level varchar [note: 'Vertical level or tier where the bin is located.']
  bin_location_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the bin (e.g., active, inactive, under maintenance).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bin location record was first created.']
  current_units varchar [note: 'Current count of units stored in the bin.']
  current_volume_cubic_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Current total volume of inventory in the bin.']
  current_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Current total weight of inventory in the bin.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the bin became available for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the bin is scheduled to be decommissioned or repurposed (nullable).']
  is_accessible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bin is accessible to standard picking equipment (e.g., wheelchair‑accessible).']
  is_hazmat_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether hazardous materials are permitted in this location.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the bin resides in a temperature‑controlled environment.']
  last_inventory_check_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory verification for this bin.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the bin within the fulfillment center.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing special characteristics or notes about the bin.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the storage location, often used in UI displays and pick‑lists.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Physical form factor of the storage element (e.g., bin, shelf, pallet, rack).']
  max_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of individual units the bin can hold.']
  max_volume_cubic_m decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum volume in cubic meters that the bin can accommodate.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight in kilograms that the bin can safely store.']
  position varchar [note: 'Specific position index within the level (e.g., slot number).']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature classification of the zone, required for perishable or temperature‑sensitive inventory.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bin location record.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the zone where the bin resides, driving pick path and handling rules.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical storage location (bin, slot, shelf, pallet position) within a fulfillment center. Captures location code, zone classification (pick, bulk, staging, returns, hazmat, cold chain), aisle/bay/level/position coordinates, location type, capacity constraints (units, weight, volume), temperature zone, hazmat flag, and active status. SSOT for warehouse slotting and zone-based pick path optimization.'
}

Table fulfillment.sla {
  sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the SLA record.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: SLA configuration and automated SLA assignment: fulfillment_sla.carrier_service_level is a plain-text denormalization of carrier_service. Automated SLA assignment engines and SLA-to-carrier-capability']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: E-commerce SLA policies are routinely defined per product category — perishables require same-day fulfillment, hazmat requires specialized handling windows, electronics may have premium SLAs. Category']
  fulfillment_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fulfillment center or warehouse to which the SLA applies.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: SLA tiers are defined per customer segment (e.g., loyalty gold gets same-day, standard gets 3-day). Linking fulfillment_sla to customer.segment enables segment-driven SLA assignment and compliance rep']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: 3PL SLA terms (pick/pack/ship targets, breach thresholds) are contractually defined in vendor contracts. Linking fulfillment_sla to vendor_contract enables automated SLA breach penalty enforcement and']
  breach_threshold_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tolerance window in days for delivery‑time breaches.']
  breach_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tolerance window in hours beyond the target after which the SLA is considered breached.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_time_target_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed days from shipment dispatch to customer receipt.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the SLA becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the SLA expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Method of order fulfillment to which the SLA applies.']
  fulfillment_sla_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the SLA purpose and scope.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, exceptions, or special handling instructions.']
  pack_time_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed time from pick completion to packing completion.']
  pick_time_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed time from order receipt to item pick completion.']
  same_day_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Latest order‑placement time (HH:MM) for same‑day fulfillment eligibility.']
  ship_time_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed time from packing completion to shipment dispatch.']
  sla_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business code for the SLA, used in operational reporting and carrier selection.']
  sla_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the SLA offering (e.g., "Prime Express SLA").']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA (e.g., standard, premium, custom).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SLA record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number for change‑management and audit tracking.']

  Note: 'Reference record defining SLA commitments by fulfillment method, carrier service level, customer segment, and fulfillment center. Captures promised pick/pack/ship SLA windows (hours), delivery window (days), same-day cutoff time, breach thresholds, and effective date range. Drives SLA compliance monitoring, fulfillment_exception breach detection, and carrier selection logic.'
}

Table logistics.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the carrier record.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the carrier\'s primary business address (optional).']
  capacity_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity the carrier can transport per shipment.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the transportation carrier.']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier (e.g., active, inactive, suspended, pending onboarding).']
  carrier_tier varchar [note: 'Internal tier used to prioritize carriers based on performance, volume, and strategic importance.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Primary service category the carrier provides (e.g., Full‑Truck‑Load, Less‑Truck‑Load, parcel, last‑mile, cross‑border).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the carrier\'s primary address.']
  compliance_ctpat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier holds a valid C‑TPAT certification.']
  compliance_iata boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier is certified by IATA for air freight operations.']
  compliance_wto boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier complies with WTO trade regulations for cross‑border shipments.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the carrier\'s primary contact.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary business contact for the carrier.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the carrier\'s primary contact.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier contract expires or is scheduled for renewal (nullable for open‑ended contracts).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the active contract with the carrier.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier contract becomes effective.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the carrier is headquartered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the carrier record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for carrier contract financials.']
  dot_number varchar [note: 'Seven‑digit identifier assigned by the U.S. Department of Transportation to the carrier.']
  edi_integration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of Electronic Data Interchange integration with the carrier.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the carrier\'s liability insurance coverage.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier\'s insurance coverage expires.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the carrier record was last reviewed for compliance or data quality.']
  max_weight_lbs decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum weight in pounds the carrier can handle for a single shipment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the carrier.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the carrier.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the carrier\'s primary address.']
  rating_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Aggregated performance rating (0‑5) based on on‑time delivery, damage rate, and service quality.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Four‑character alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the carrier in industry systems.']
  service_capabilities varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the carrier\'s capabilities (e.g., temperature‑controlled, hazardous‑material, LTL consolidation).']
  sla_delivery_time_actual varchar [note: 'Actual delivery time in minutes recorded for the carrier.']
  sla_delivery_time_target varchar [note: 'Target delivery time in minutes defined in the carrier SLA.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the carrier\'s primary address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the carrier record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address of the carrier.']

  Note: 'Master record for all transportation carriers (FTL, LTL, parcel, last-mile, cross-border) contracted by Ecommerce. Captures carrier identity (SCAC, DOT number), service capabilities, EDI integration status, compliance certifications (C-TPAT, IATA, WTO), carrier tier classification, performance SLA thresholds, and active contract references. SSOT for carrier identity across TMS and logistics operations.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_service {
  carrier_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each carrier service offering.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier that provides this service.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service fees are allocated to cost centers to reflect true cost of carrier services per business unit.']
  base_rate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base price for the service before any surcharges or discounts, in US dollars.']
  carrier_service_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the service, including any special handling notes.']
  carrier_service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service offering.']
  cost_per_kg_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Variable cost component calculated per kilogram of shipment weight.']
  cost_per_mile_usd decimal(10,4) [note: 'Variable cost component calculated per mile of distance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier service record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for pricing.']
  customs_document_required boolean [note: 'True if shipments using this service require customs paperwork.']
  destination_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic zone or region where shipments are delivered for this service.']
  dimension_limit_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum linear dimension (e.g., longest side) allowed for the service, in centimeters.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the service offering becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the service offering is retired or expires (null if indefinite).']
  fuel_surcharge_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage surcharge applied to the base rate to account for fuel price volatility.']
  is_express boolean [note: 'True if the service is classified as express (priority handling).']
  level_code varchar [note: 'Carrier‑specific code representing the service level tier.']
  max_height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum height of a package for this service, in centimeters.']
  max_length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum length of a package for this service, in centimeters.']
  max_volume_cbm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum cargo volume allowed for the service, expressed in cubic meters.']
  max_width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum width of a package for this service, in centimeters.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or special conditions for the service.']
  origin_zone varchar [note: 'Geographic zone or region where shipments originate for this service.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the service based on geographic scope.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'Carrier‑assigned alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the service.']
  service_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the service (e.g., "Express Overnight").']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the service offering (e.g., standard ground, express, LTL, FTL).']
  sla_transit_time_days varchar [note: 'Service Level Agreement guaranteed transit time in days.']
  surcharge_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service is subject to surcharges (e.g., fuel, residential).']
  tracking_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real‑time shipment tracking is available for this service.']
  transit_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of calendar days from pickup to delivery for this service.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the carrier service record.']
  weight_limit_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable shipment weight for the service, expressed in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific service offerings provided by each carrier — e.g., standard ground, express, overnight, same-day, LTL freight, FTL, cross-border. Captures service code, transit time SLA, weight/dimension limits, supported origin-destination lanes, surcharge eligibility, and carrier-assigned service level identifiers. Enables carrier selection logic in TMS route optimization.'
}

  logistics_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the shipment record.']
