  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Needed for Marketing ROI attribution linking each shipment to the campaign that generated the order, enabling shipment‑level performance reporting.']
  carrier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_contract. Business justification: A logistics shipment is executed under a specific carrier contract that governs the commercial terms, SLAs, and rates. Linking logistics_shipment to carrier_contract enables contract compliance monito']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Link logistics_shipment to carrier for carrier ownership; carrier_name string becomes redundant.']
  carrier_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_rate_card. Business justification: A logistics shipment is priced using a specific carrier rate card at the time of booking. Linking logistics_shipment to carrier_rate_card enables shipping cost reconciliation (actual cost vs. contract']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: A logistics shipment is executed using exactly one carrier service (e.g., FedEx Ground, UPS Express). logistics_shipment currently stores carrier_service_code (STRING) and service_level (STRING) as de']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for internal accounting: allocate each shipment\'s cost to a cost center for expense reporting and profitability analysis.']
  customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Required for shipment fulfillment reports linking each shipment to the customer\'s shipping address used for origin, enabling address‑level delivery performance analysis.']
  delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery_zone. Business justification: A logistics shipment is assigned to a delivery zone for carrier routing, SLA determination, and rate calculation. delivery_zone defines geographic zones with SLA days, delivery windows, and surcharge ']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipment in the Transportation Management System.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Inbound Shipment Receipt Matching report ties each shipment to the Goods Receipt that records quantity received in the warehouse.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Linking shipments to the originating order is essential for order‑to‑shipment tracking reports and customer service dashboards.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Seller shipment performance reporting, SLA compliance tracking per seller, and shipping cost attribution require direct seller identification on each shipment. Marketplace operations track late shipme']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the shipment was delivered to the recipient.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Date the shipment actually left the origin.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was initially created in the TMS.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for customs paperwork.']
  customs_value_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Declared monetary value for customs duty calculation.']
  customs_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the customs value.']
  declared_value_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for customs and insurance purposes.']
  declared_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the declared value.']
  delivery_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when delivery was confirmed by the carrier.']
  destination_address varchar [note: 'Full delivery address of the customer.']
  destination_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the delivery destination.']
  dimension_height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height of the shipment package in centimeters.']
  dimension_length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length of the shipment package in centimeters.']
  dimension_width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width of the shipment package in centimeters.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for delivery to the destination.']
  estimated_ship_date date [note: 'Planned date for the shipment to leave the origin.']
  insurance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the insurance coverage.']
  insurance_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the insurance amount.']
  insurance_required boolean [note: 'True if the shipment is insured.']
  is_hazardous_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous goods.']
  logistics_shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shipment.']
  origin_address varchar [note: 'Full address of the shipment origin (warehouse or fulfillment center).']
  origin_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the origin location.']
  proof_of_delivery_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic proof of delivery document.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the record was first captured.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  route_optimization_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the route optimization run used for this shipment.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the shipment by the transportation management system.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment based on load size.']
  shipping_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Base shipping charge before taxes and fees.']
  shipping_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the shipping cost.']
  shipping_cost_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the shipping charge.']
  shipping_cost_total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total shipping charge including taxes and fees.']
  signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recipient signature is required on delivery.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking identifier provided by the carrier.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Core transactional entity representing a physical shipment dispatched from a fulfillment node to a destination. Captures shipment reference number, origin/destination addresses, carrier assignment, service level, tracking number, weight, dimensions, declared value, shipment status (derived from tracking_event stream), estimated and actual ship/delivery dates, FTL/LTL indicator, and TMS shipment ID. Central logistics record linking orders to physical movement. SSOT for shipment identity and carrier assignment. Status is derived from tracking_event — this entity owns the shipment header, not the event history.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_package {
  shipment_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment package record.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: In multi-package shipments, individual packages may use different carrier service levels (e.g., one package express, another standard). shipment_package has carrier_service_level (STRING) as a denorma']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary product contained in the package.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: shipment_package is a child entity of logistics_shipment — a single shipment may contain multiple packages (cartons). Currently shipment_package has no FK to logistics_shipment, only to fulfillment.fu']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the package was actually delivered.']
  barcode_image_url varchar [note: 'Link to the barcode image used on the package label.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the shipping carrier (e.g., UPS, FedEx).']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the shipping carrier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the package record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the declared value.']
  customs_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to electronic customs documentation associated with the package.']
  declared_value_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for customs and insurance purposes, expressed in US dollars.']
  delivery_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when delivery was confirmed by the carrier or recipient.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for package delivery based on carrier service level.']
  height_cm varchar [note: 'External height of the package in centimeters.']
  insurance_amount_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of insurance coverage for the package, in US dollars.']
  insurance_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'True if the package is insured for loss or damage.']
  is_return boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the package is part of a return shipment.']
  label_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the shipping label was created.']
  label_url varchar [note: 'Link to the generated shipping label PDF or image.']
  length_cm varchar [note: 'External length of the package in centimeters.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Physical form factor of the package.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the product shipped in this package.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to the package.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the package within the shipment.']
  shipment_package_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the package.']
  signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if a recipient signature is required for delivery.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking identifier for the package.']
  tracking_url varchar [note: 'Web URL where the package tracking status can be viewed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the package record.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Net weight of the package in kilograms.']
  width_cm varchar [note: 'External width of the package in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Individual physical packages within a shipment. A single shipment may contain multiple packages (cartons, pallets, parcels, envelopes). Captures package sequence number, dimensions (L×W×H), weight, package type, tracking barcode, carrier label format and file reference URL, label generation timestamp, seal number, hazmat flag, and package-level status. Subsumes shipping label data — label attributes are properties of the package, not a separate entity. Enables parcel-level tracking, carrier billing reconciliation, and manifest generation.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_item {
  shipment_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each line item within a shipment.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent shipment record to which this line belongs.']
  shipment_package_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical package/container that holds this line item.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Shipment-level SKU traceability is required for hazmat compliance checks, export control classification, returns processing, and carrier audit reconciliation. E-commerce logistics experts expect shipm']
  tax_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_record. Business justification: shipment_item carries hs_tariff_code, export_control_classification, and hazmat_classification — item-level attributes that determine customs duty and import tax calculations stored in tax_record. Cro']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier responsible for transporting the shipment.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating where the product was manufactured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment item record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code of the declared value (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  declared_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value declared for a single unit of the item for customs purposes.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory export classification (e.g., EAR99, ITAR) for the item.']
  hazmat_classification varchar [note: 'Classification code indicating hazardous material type, if applicable.']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical height of the item in centimeters.']
  hs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'International customs classification code for the product.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'True if the item is classified as hazardous material.']
  length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical length of the item in centimeters.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line item within the shipment for ordering.']
  line_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value for the line (quantity × declared unit value).']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU shipped in this line.']
  restricted_flag boolean [note: 'True if the item is subject to export restrictions.']
  shipment_item_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the shipment line item.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking number assigned by the carrier for this shipment line.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment item record.']
  volume_cbm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated volume of the line item in cubic meters (derived from dimensions).']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of the line item in kilograms, used for freight calculations.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical width of the item in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail of SKUs included within a shipment package. Captures SKU reference, quantity shipped, unit of measure, item description, HS tariff code for customs, country of origin, declared unit value, and hazmat classification. Supports customs documentation, carrier manifests, and shipment-to-order reconciliation.'
}

Table logistics.tracking_event {
  tracking_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Globally unique surrogate key for each shipment tracking event.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Link tracking_event to carrier for carrier reference; carrier_code becomes redundant.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: tracking_event records all shipment lifecycle events (EDI 214, carrier integrations). Each tracking event belongs to a specific logistics shipment. Currently tracking_event only has carrier_id FK — th']
  shipment_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment_package. Business justification: In e-commerce multi-package shipments, each package has its own tracking number and generates its own tracking events. tracking_event needs a package-level FK to support package-granularity event trac']
  carrier_facility_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier\'s hub or facility where the event occurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the tracking event record was first ingested into the lakehouse.']
  delivery_attempt_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the delivery attempt.']
  delivery_attempt_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal number of the delivery attempt for this shipment.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the event record.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier generated the tracking event.']
  event_type_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the type of shipment event.']
  event_type_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the event type.']
  exception_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why an exception event occurred.']
  exception_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable explanation of the exception cause.']
  exception_resolution_action varchar [note: 'Action taken to resolve the exception (e.g., address corrected, customs cleared).']
  exception_resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was resolved.']
  exception_responsible_party varchar [note: 'Entity accountable for resolving the exception.']
  gps_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the event location in decimal degrees.']
  gps_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the event location in decimal degrees.']
  proof_of_delivery_photo_url varchar [note: 'Link to the delivery confirmation photo.']
  proof_of_delivery_recipient_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who received the shipment.']
  proof_of_delivery_signature_url varchar [note: 'Link to the captured signature image for the delivery.']
  proof_of_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when proof of delivery was captured.']
  raw_event_description varchar [note: 'Original free‑text payload received from the carrier.']
  reattempt_scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for the next delivery attempt after a failure.']
  scan_location varchar [note: 'Physical address or location description where the scan was performed.']
  sla_met_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment met the SLA delivery time (true) or missed it (false).']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Committed delivery time defined by the SLA.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the tracking event record.']

  Note: 'Unified event stream recording all shipment lifecycle events from carrier integrations (EDI 214, carrier APIs). Event types include: pickup scan, in-transit scan, out-for-delivery, delivery attempt (with attempt sequence, outcome, and reattempt scheduling), exception (address correction, customs hold, damage, carrier delay — with exception reason code, responsible party, resolution action, and resolution timestamp), and proof-of-delivery confirmation (recipient name, signature capture, delivery photo URL, GPS coordinates). Captures event timestamp, event type code, scan location, carrier facility code, GPS coordinates, and raw carrier event description. SSOT for ALL shipment status derivation, last-mile visibility, exception management, delivery confirmation, and SLA compliance monitoring. No other entity in this domain records shipment events or delivery outcomes.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_rate_card {
  carrier_rate_card_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the carrier rate card record.']
  carrier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_contract. Business justification: Rate cards are negotiated and governed under carrier contracts. carrier_rate_card has negotiation_reference (STRING) and rate_card_reference (STRING on carrier_contract) as loose text references. Addi']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the carrier organization that owns this rate card.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: A carrier rate card defines contracted rates for a specific carrier service (e.g., FedEx Ground rates for lane X). carrier_rate_card currently stores service_level_code (STRING) and service_level_desc']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rate‑card charges are allocated to cost centers for chargeback and profitability reporting.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Carrier rate cards drive freight cost postings to specific GL freight expense accounts. Finance teams reconcile carrier spend against GL during period-end close. Rate card charges must map to GL accou']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: In e-commerce, carrier rate cards map to customer-facing price lists for shipping fee pass-through at checkout. This link enables shipping cost pricing reports, dynamic shipping fee calculation by zon']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Seller‑specific rate cards are used in the \'Rate Card Assignment\' workflow for volume‑based pricing and discount calculations.']
  accessorial_charges_included varchar [note: 'Flag indicating whether standard accessorial fees are bundled into the base rate.']
  accessorial_rate_schedule varchar [note: 'Reference to the schedule or code list that defines additional fees (e.g., lift‑gate, residential delivery).']
  base_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Fundamental freight charge per kilogram before surcharges, expressed in the specified currency.']
  carrier_rate_card_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the rate card (e.g., active, inactive, expired, pending approval).']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory frameworks applicable to the rate card (e.g., WTO, ISO 27001, PCI DSS).']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement (master agreement, spot rate, or contract rate).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate card record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for all monetary values in the rate card.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the rate card becomes legally binding and usable for pricing.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the rate card expires; null indicates an open‑ended agreement.']
  fuel_surcharge_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage applied to the base rate to account for fuel price volatility.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Term governing responsibility and cost allocation (e.g., EXW, FCA, CPT, CIP, DAT, DAP, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAT|DAP|DDP — promote to reference product]']
  is_accessorial_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accessorial rates from the schedule are applicable for this lane.']
  is_fuel_surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fuel surcharge percentage should be applied to shipments under this rate.']
  lane_destination varchar [note: 'Standardized code (e.g., IATA, UN/LOCODE) representing the shipment destination point for the lane.']
  lane_origin varchar [note: 'Standardized code (e.g., IATA, UN/LOCODE) representing the shipment origin point for the lane.']
  lane_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the origin‑destination lane is domestic or crosses international borders.']
  negotiation_reference varchar [note: 'Internal reference or document ID linking the rate card to its contract negotiation record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks, exceptions, or special conditions related to the rate card.']
  rate_card_category varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of the rate card such as lane‑based, zone‑based, or region‑based.']
  rate_card_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the rate card by the carrier or logistics contract team.']
  rate_card_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the rate card (e.g., Transportation Management System, ERP).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rate card record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version identifier for the rate card to track revisions over time.']
  weight_tier_max_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the weight range (in kilograms) for which the base rate applies.']
  weight_tier_min_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the weight range (in kilograms) for which the base rate applies.']

  Note: 'Contracted rate structures negotiated with carriers for specific lanes, service levels, and weight breaks. Captures rate card version, effective/expiry dates, origin-destination lane, service level code, weight tier, base rate, fuel surcharge percentage, accessorial rate schedule, currency, and negotiation reference. SSOT for freight cost estimation and carrier selection optimization in TMS.'
}

Table logistics.delivery_zone {
  delivery_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each delivery zone record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Link delivery_zone to its preferred carrier; carrier_preferred string becomes redundant.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Zone‑level delivery costs are charged to cost centers for regional budgeting and performance tracking.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Allows the \'Default Delivery Zone\' setting for each seller, used in shipping cost and SLA calculations.']
  carrier_zone_mapping varchar [note: 'Mapping string that links carrier‑specific zone identifiers to this internal zone.']
  cost_per_shipment decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard base cost charged for a shipment to this zone before surcharges.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code representing the country covered by the zone.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values associated with the zone.']
  delivery_sla_days varchar [note: 'Committed number of calendar days for delivery within this zone.']
  delivery_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'Latest time of day (HH:MM) when delivery can be performed in this zone.']
  delivery_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'Earliest time of day (HH:MM) when delivery can be performed in this zone.']
  delivery_zone_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the zone, including any special handling notes.']
  delivery_zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the zone record.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the zone definition becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the zone definition expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone is the default fallback for unmapped addresses.']
  is_express_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether express (same‑day/next‑day) service is offered in this zone.']
  max_volume_cbm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum total shipment volume allowed for a single package in this zone.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum total shipment weight allowed for a single package in this zone.']
  postal_code_range varchar [note: 'Range of postal codes (e.g., 10000‑19999) included in the zone.']
  region varchar [note: 'State, province, or region name within the country that the zone covers.']
  requires_signature boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recipient signature is required for deliveries in this zone.']
  surcharge_remote_area boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a remote‑area surcharge applies to shipments in this zone.']
  surcharge_residential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a residential‑delivery surcharge applies to this zone.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the zone record.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the delivery zone, used in carrier rate tables and routing logic.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the delivery zone.']
  zone_tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification used for pricing and service level differentiation.']

  Note: 'Geographic delivery zone definitions used for carrier assignment, rate calculation, and SLA determination. Captures zone code, zone name, country/region/state coverage, postal code ranges, carrier-specific zone mapping, delivery day SLA, surcharge applicability (remote area, residential), and zone tier classification. Reference entity supporting route optimization and last-mile planning.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_contract {
  carrier_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each carrier contract record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_agreement. Business justification: When a platform negotiates a carrier contract on behalf of a seller, the carrier contract must reference the seller agreement governing shipping obligations, SLA commitments, and termination condition']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier entity associated with this contract.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Carrier contracts are budgeted to specific cost centers; this FK supports cost‑center level budgeting and variance tracking.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Contract expenses must be posted to a GL account; linking contracts to general_ledger enables accurate financial statements.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Carrier contracts are executed by specific legal entities — critical for intercompany logistics arrangements, regulatory compliance (CTPAT, IATA, WTO), and multi-entity e-commerce operations. Legal en']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Contracts are negotiated per seller; the \'Carrier Contract Management\' process requires linking each contract to the seller it serves.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date when the latest amendment was executed.']
  amendment_description varchar [note: 'Summary of changes introduced by the latest amendment.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the most recent amendment to the contract.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at expiry.']
  carrier_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract.']
  compliance_ctpat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier complies with the Customs‑Trade Partnership Against Terrorism program.']
  compliance_iata boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with International Air Transport Association standards where applicable.']
  compliance_wto boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with World Trade Organization regulations for cross‑border shipments.']
  confidentiality_flag boolean [note: 'Marks the contract as confidential for internal handling.']
  contract_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the contract applies to domestic or international shipments.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic copy of the signed contract.']
  contract_manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the contract manager.']
  contract_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person managing day‑to‑day contract activities.']
  contract_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the contract manager.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract reference number assigned by the carrier or Ecommerce.']
  contract_owner varchar [note: 'Internal stakeholder responsible for the contract\'s overall performance.']
  contract_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region covered by the contract.']
  contract_signed_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was formally signed by both parties.']
  contract_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract status last changed.']
  contract_status_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or narrative explaining the most recent status change.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract: master, spot, or dedicated.']
  contracted_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total volume of freight (e.g., tons) the carrier commits to transport under the contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts in the contract.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes binding.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract grants exclusive rights to the carrier.']
  is_preferred boolean [note: 'Marks the carrier as a preferred partner for routing decisions.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last audit or compliance review of the contract.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or special conditions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition (e.g., Net30) agreed with the carrier.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty applied for SLA breaches or contract violations.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the penalty amount.']
  rate_card_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the carrier\'s rate card that defines pricing structures.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Number of months for each renewal period when auto‑renewal is enabled.']
  sla_delivery_time_actual_hours varchar [note: 'Actual delivery time recorded for the contract period.']
  sla_delivery_time_target_hours varchar [note: 'Targeted delivery time in hours as defined in the SLA.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Required notice period in days before contract termination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the contracted volume.']

  Note: 'Master records of commercial contracts with transportation carriers. Captures contract number, carrier reference, contract type (master, spot, dedicated), effective and expiry dates, contracted volume commitments, rate card references, SLA terms, penalty clauses, auto-renewal flag, and contract status. Distinct from carrier_rate_card which holds rate structures — this entity holds the legal and commercial agreement metadata.'
}

Table pricing.price_list {
  price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price list record.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Price lists with currency_code and region attributes must be scoped to a legal entity for intercompany transfer pricing, tax compliance, and financial consolidation in multi-entity e-commerce. Domain ']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Price lists are frequently scoped to a specific marketplace (e.g., a price list valid only on a given marketplace). Marketplace-scoped price list management, activation, and reporting require this dir']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: REQUIRED: Segment‑based pricing; marketing analytics join price list to customer segment to evaluate segment‑specific discount performance.']
  base_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Core price value before any adjustments, expressed in the list currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the prices are expressed.']
  discount_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'True if additional discounts may be stacked on top of this price list.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the price list expires; null for open‑ended lists.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the price list becomes active for transactions.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'True if this list is the default for its scope when no other list matches.']
  max_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Ceiling price for the list; used to enforce pricing caps.']
  max_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity for which the price is valid; null for no upper bound.']
  min_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Floor price for the list; used to enforce pricing floors.']
  min_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity required for the price to apply.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or operational comments.']
  price_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the prices in this list.']
  price_elasticity decimal(10,4) [note: 'Historical elasticity metric used for dynamic pricing decisions.']
  price_list_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the price list in external systems and contracts.']
  price_list_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context, purpose, and any special rules for the price list.']
  price_list_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the price list (e.g., "US Retail MSRP").']
  price_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price list.']
  price_precision varchar [note: 'Number of decimal places used for price values in this list.']
  price_rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rounding behavior applied to calculated prices.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price data (e.g., manually entered, system‑generated, partner feed).']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Logical tier or band within the price list (e.g., "Gold", "Silver").']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Category of the price list indicating its purpose (e.g., base list, discount list, promotional list).']
  promotional_flag boolean [note: 'True if the price list is part of a promotional campaign.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region to which the price list applies.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether taxes are already included in the listed prices.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price list record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a named price list (e.g., MSRP, wholesale, member, regional) with effective date ranges, currency, applicable customer segments, and channel scope. Serves as the authoritative base price catalog from which all pricing rules derive. Owned by the Pricing domain as the SSOT for base price structures.'
}

Table pricing.price_list_item {
  price_list_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each price list item record.']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundles require dedicated price list entries for bundle-level discount management (e.g., bundle price vs. sum-of-parts). A direct bundle_id FK on price_list_item enables bundle pricing management, bun']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Price list items are attributed to cost centers for P&L reporting and budget variance analysis. In e-commerce, each pricing tier must be owned by a business unit (cost center) to track margin impact. ']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent price list to which this item belongs.']
  primary_price_product_variant_sku_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier for the specific product variant when SKU alone is insufficient.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Enables seller‑specific SKU price overrides; pricing engine looks up seller_profile_id to apply custom base prices per seller.']
  supplier_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_price_list. Business justification: Sell-side price list items are derived from supplier cost prices. Linking price_list_item to supplier_price_list enables real-time margin calculation, cost-plus pricing enforcement, and margin reporti']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Location-based price list items require warehouse node to apply inventory‑aware pricing per fulfillment center.']
  audit_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the user who performed the last audit action (e.g., pricing_manager).']
  base_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard unit price before any tiered, volume, or promotional adjustments.']
  bundle_component_skus varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of SKUs that constitute the bundle.']
  bundle_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount applied to the total of bundled SKUs when price_type = \'bundle\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the price list item record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when this price becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when this price expires; null for open‑ended pricing.']
  is_promotional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price is part of a promotional campaign.']
  last_price_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent price modification.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered to qualify for this price.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason explaining why the price was changed.']
  price_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the pricing rule or context.']
  price_elasticity_score decimal(5,3) [note: 'Calculated elasticity metric used for dynamic pricing decisions.']
  price_list_item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price list item.']
  price_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the price value (e.g., manually entered, generated by pricing algorithm, or imported from external feed).']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pricing structure applied to this item.']
  promotional_end_date date [note: 'End date of the promotional pricing period.']
  promotional_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the promotional pricing period.']
  tax_inclusive boolean [note: 'True if the base_price already includes applicable taxes.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage; used when tax_inclusive = false.']
  tier_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price applied when the ordered quantity meets the tier_quantity_threshold.']
  tier_quantity_threshold varchar [note: 'Quantity breakpoint for tiered pricing; applicable when price_type = \'tiered\' or \'volume\'.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the price (e.g., each, kg, liter).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the price list item.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number for optimistic concurrency control and change tracking.']
  volume_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied for volume purchases; used when price_type = \'volume\'.']

  Note: 'Line-level entry within a price list associating a specific SKU or product variant to its base unit price, minimum order quantity (MOQ), unit of measure, and pricing structure type (flat, tiered, volume-based, bundle). For tiered/volume pricing, captures quantity thresholds and per-tier unit prices. For bundle pricing, captures constituent SKUs and bundle discount. Serves as the authoritative base price per SKU per price list supporting all pricing structure variants.'
}

Table pricing.dynamic_price_rule {
  dynamic_price_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the dynamic pricing rule.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules in e-commerce are scoped to specific audience segments (e.g., loyalty tier members receive different floor/ceiling prices). This link enables audience-targeted dynamic repricing ']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules enforce brand-level MAP (Minimum Advertised Price) policies. brand_code is a denormalized text reference to product.brand; replacing with FK enables MAP compliance enforcement, b']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules are scoped to product categories (e.g., margin-floor rules for Electronics). category_code is a denormalized text reference to product.category; replacing with FK enables the rep']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules drive margin adjustments that must be budgeted and tracked per cost center. The rule_owner and scope fields indicate organizational ownership; cost center linkage enables financi']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules with compliance_flag and is_global=false operate within specific legal entity boundaries for regulatory compliance. In multi-entity e-commerce, pricing algorithms must be legally']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Dynamic price rules with global or marketplace-wide scope (is_global=true) must be anchored to a specific marketplace for rule execution and compliance auditing. The existing marketplace_listing_id co']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketplace channel (e.g., third‑party marketplace) the rule applies to.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: dynamic_price_rule is defined for a specific price list; linking provides parent context']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules can be campaign-scoped — for example, a flash sale promotional campaign may activate specific dynamic pricing rules that adjust prices algorithmically during the campaign window.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the seller to which the rule is scoped (if seller‑specific).']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Inventory-level-triggered dynamic repricing (scarcity pricing when ATP is low, overstock discounting when on_hand_qty exceeds max) is a fundamental e-commerce revenue optimization process. This FK lin']
  supplier_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_price_list. Business justification: Dynamic pricing rules enforce floor prices to protect margins. Linking to supplier_price_list allows the pricing engine to automatically set floor_price from live supplier costs, preventing below-cost']
  activation_end timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule expires or is deactivated.']
  activation_start timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule becomes effective.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the rule adjusts price by a percentage, a fixed amount, sets a floor, or sets a ceiling.']
  adjustment_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the adjustment value, matching the adjustment_type (percentage or currency).']
  adjustment_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric value used with the adjustment_type (e.g., 5.0 for 5% or 2.50 for $2.50).']
  applicable_sku_pattern varchar [note: 'Wildcard or regex pattern that matches SKUs to which the rule applies.']
  audit_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes from auditors or reviewers about the rule.']
  ceiling_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum price that the rule will not allow the SKU to exceed.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule complies with MAP and other regulatory pricing constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rule record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for price adjustments.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the rule becomes effective for reporting purposes.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the rule expires; null if open‑ended.']
  execution_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the rule has been applied to transactions.']
  floor_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum price that the rule will not allow the SKU to drop below.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is currently enabled for execution.']
  is_experimental boolean [note: 'Marks the rule as part of an A/B pricing experiment.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'True if the rule applies across all marketplaces and sellers.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of the rule.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Any supplemental information or comments about the rule.']
  price_elasticity_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Pre‑computed elasticity metric used by the rule engine to gauge demand sensitivity.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple rules apply; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the rule\'s purpose.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the rule\'s purpose and logic.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the pricing rule for identification.']
  rule_owner varchar [note: 'Team or individual responsible for the rule\'s lifecycle.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the rule is calculated in real‑time (dynamic), pre‑computed (static), or a combination (hybrid).']
  scope varchar [note: 'Defines the applicability level of the rule (entire platform, specific seller, product category, brand, or marketplace).']
  test_group varchar [note: 'Indicates the A/B test group assignment for the rule.']
  trigger_condition varchar [note: 'Serialized condition expression (e.g., JSON) that determines when the rule fires, based on demand, inventory, competitor price, etc.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the rule.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the rule for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system account that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Defines algorithmic or rules-based dynamic pricing logic applied to SKUs, categories, or seller listings across platform-owned and marketplace channels. Includes trigger conditions (demand signals, inventory levels, competitor price thresholds, MAP violations, seller compliance triggers), adjustment type (percentage, fixed, floor/ceiling), rule scope (platform-wide, seller-specific, category-level, brand-level), priority rank, activation schedule, and enforcement actions. For marketplace-scoped rules: MAP enforcement thresholds, maximum price caps, commission-adjusted floor prices, seller-specific pricing agreements, and brand protection policies. Powers real-time price optimization, surge/markdown automation, MAP enforcement, marketplace pricing integrity, and seller pricing governance.'
}

Table pricing.markdown_schedule {
  markdown_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the markdown schedule.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment.   promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: A markdown schedule is a planned price reduction calendar that is frequently driven by a promotional campaign (e.g., a seasonal sale or flash sale campaign triggers a series of markdown schedules). Th']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Markdown schedules target specific customer segments (e.g., VIP-early-access markdowns). Linking to customer.segment enables segment-targeted markdown execution and markdown performance reporting by s']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: REQUIRED: Sellers create markdown schedules for their products; linking to seller_profile ties schedule ownership for audit and reporting.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. This FK enables the ']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_node. Business justification: Warehouse-level markdown schedule clears overstock by applying discounts specific to each fulfillment node.']

  channel varchar [note: 'Channel(s) where the markdown is applied.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the markdown schedule record was first created in the system.']

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the markdown becomes applicable.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule should block other overlapping markdowns for the same SKU.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule is a test (non‑production) run.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the schedule.']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of the markdown schedule.']
  markdown_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the schedule.']
  markdown_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the markdown is a percentage reduction or an absolute monetary amount.']
  markdown_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The reduction amount: either a percentage (e.g., 0.15 for 15%) or a fixed currency amount, depending on markdown_type.']

  min_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum purchase quantity required for the markdown to apply.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Execution priority when multiple schedules overlap; lower numbers run first.']

  run_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the schedule has been applied to orders.']

  Note: 'Planned markdown calendar for SKUs or product categories, specifying markdown percentage or absolute reduction, start and end dates, markdown reason (end-of-season, clearance, overstock), and approval status. Supports seasonal clearance cycles and inventory liquidation workflows.'
}

  promotional_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the promotional campaign.']

Legal entity scoping is required for t']

  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Vendor-funded promotions (trade promotions) are a major e-commerce revenue stream where suppliers co-fund discounts. Linking promotional_campaign to supplier enables vendor funding reconciliation, co-']
  a_b_test_variant varchar [note: 'Variant assignment for A/B test campaigns.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary budget allocated for the campaign.']
  budget_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the budget currency.']

  discount_value_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the discount value – either percent or currency.']
  eligibility_rule_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the rules that determine customer eligibility.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the campaign expires or is deactivated.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the campaign.']
  gmv_target decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target GMV the campaign aims to generate.']
  gmv_target_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the GMV target.']
  is_a_b_test boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the campaign is part of an A/B testing experiment.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the campaign is a test (non‑production) run.']
  max_uses varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times the campaign can be applied across all customers.']
  owning_business_unit varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the campaign (e.g., marketing, pricing).']
  promotional_campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the campaign objectives, mechanics and any special notes.']
  promotional_campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the promotional campaign.']

  redemption_total_limit varchar [note: 'Overall cap on the number of redemptions across all customers.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code representing the region when geographic_scope is regional or local.']

  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last updated the campaign record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Running total of how many times the campaign has been used.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the campaign record.']

  Note: 'Master record for a pricing-driven promotional campaign (e.g., flash sale, seasonal event, loyalty reward event), capturing campaign name, type, budget cap, GMV target, start/end timestamps, eligibility rules, and owning business unit. Acts as the parent entity for all promotional mechanics and discount structures within a campaign.'
}
