  promotion_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the promotion rule.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: Promotion rules are scoped to marketing audience segments (e.g., win-back segment gets 20% off). The existing customer_segment plain-text column is a denormalization of audience_segment. FK enables pr']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Brand-scoped promotions (e.g., \'Buy 2 Nike items, save 10%\') are standard e-commerce mechanics. applies_to_brand is a denormalized text reference to product.brand; replacing with FK enables brand part']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle promotions (e.g., \'Bundle deal: 15% off kit\') are a core e-commerce mechanic. applies_to_bundle is a denormalized text reference to product.bundle; replacing with FK enables the promotion engin']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Promotion rules are category-scoped (e.g., \'20% off all Footwear\'). applies_to_category is a denormalized text reference to product.category; replacing with FK enables the promotion engine to resolve ']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Promotion rules are channel-specific in e-commerce (e.g., email-only 15% off, in-app exclusive discount). The channel plain-text column denormalizes marketing.channel. FK enables channel-scoped promot']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: A promotion rule can be scoped to a specific price list — for example, a BOGO rule may only apply to items in the MSRP price list, or a loyalty discount rule may only apply to the member price list. A']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: promotion_rule belongs to a promotional campaign; linking enables campaign-level rule management']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Promotion rules restrict eligibility to customer segments (e.g., B2B-only bulk discount rules). Linking to customer.segment enables rule engine to enforce segment eligibility programmatically and repl']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code that customers must enter to trigger the rule, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion rule record was first created.']
  discount_unit varchar [note: 'Indicates whether discount_value is a percentage or a monetary amount.']
  discount_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the discount; interpretation depends on discount_unit (e.g., 10.00 for $10 off or 15.0 for 15% off).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the promotion rule becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the promotion rule expires (null for open‑ended).']
  eligible_sku_criteria varchar [note: 'Expression or list defining which SKUs qualify for the promotion (e.g., SKU prefixes, category codes).']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'If true, the rule cannot be combined with any other promotion.']
  max_redemptions_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer can use the rule.']
  max_redemptions_total varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times the rule can be redeemed across all customers.']
  minimum_cart_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum order subtotal required for the rule to apply.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple stackable rules apply (lower number = higher priority).']
  promotion_rule_code varchar [note: 'Business code or SKU‑like identifier that uniquely identifies the rule in external systems.']
  promotion_rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the promotion rule and its business intent.']
  promotion_rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the promotion rule used for display and reporting.']
  promotion_rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rule.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Current count of how many times the rule has been redeemed.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discount mechanic (e.g., Buy‑One‑Get‑One, percentage off, fixed amount off, free shipping, bundle deal).']
  stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule can be combined with other promotions.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified the rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion rule.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the rule.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific discount mechanics within a promotional campaign, including rule type (BOGO, percentage off, fixed amount off, free shipping, bundle deal), qualifying conditions (minimum cart value, eligible SKUs, customer segment, channel), discount value, stacking priority, and maximum redemption limits.'
}

Table pricing.coupon {
  coupon_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each coupon record.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: Coupons are distributed to specific marketing audience segments (e.g., lapsed-buyer segment receives reactivation coupon). The eligible_customer_segment plain-text column denormalizes audience_segment']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated or is associated with the coupon.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Coupons represent financial liabilities (discount_amount, total_redemption_cap) requiring GL accrual for deferred revenue and contra-revenue accounting under ASC 606/IFRS 15. In e-commerce, coupon lia']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Coupons are marketplace-specific instruments — an Amazon coupon cannot be redeemed on eBay. Coupon issuance, redemption eligibility enforcement, and promotional reporting all require knowing which mar']
  promotion_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotion_rule. Business justification: A coupon implements specific discount mechanics defined by a promotion rule (e.g., a BOGO rule, a percentage discount rule, or a minimum cart value rule). The promotion_rule table defines the discount']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: A coupon is issued as part of a pricing-driven promotional campaign. The coupon table already has campaign_id pointing to marketing.campaign (the marketing execution), but lacks a FK to pricing.promot']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Coupons are issued to specific customer segments (e.g., win-back coupons for lapsed customers). A FK to customer.segment enables automated eligibility validation at redemption and segment-level coupon']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable alphanumeric identifier used by customers to redeem the discount.']
  coupon_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the coupon purpose and terms.']
  coupon_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the coupon.']
  coupon_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the coupon instrument (e.g., public coupon, targeted voucher, batch‑issued code, gift card).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the coupon record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency identifier for monetary fields.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary value subtracted from the order total when discount_type is fixed_amount.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage value applied to the order total when discount_type is percentage.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism of the discount applied by the coupon.']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'Channel through which the coupon code was delivered to the customer.']
  eligible_category_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product category identifiers for which the coupon is valid.']
  eligible_product_scope varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list or pattern of product identifiers (SKU, ASIN, etc.) that the coupon can be applied to.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time after which the coupon can no longer be redeemed.']
  is_one_time_use boolean [note: 'True if the coupon may be redeemed only once across all customers.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the coupon can be combined with other promotions.']
  issuance_channel varchar [note: 'System or process that generated the coupon (e.g., campaign engine, manual upload).']
  max_discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper cap on the monetary value that can be deducted by the coupon.']
  min_order_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum order subtotal required for the coupon to be applicable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for internal comments or special handling instructions.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Current count of how many times the coupon has been redeemed.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the coupon becomes valid for redemption.']
  total_redemption_cap varchar [note: 'Overall maximum number of redemptions allowed across all customers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the coupon record.']
  usage_limit_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer may redeem this coupon.']

  Note: 'Master record for all discount code instruments including public promotional coupons, customer-specific vouchers, batch-issued codes, and gift card discount codes. Captures code identifier, code type (public coupon, targeted voucher, batch-issued, gift card), discount type (percentage, fixed amount, free shipping), discount value, minimum order threshold, maximum discount cap, eligible product/category scope, customer segment eligibility, distribution method, usage limit per customer, total redemption cap, expiry date, issuance channel, and linked campaign. For voucher-type codes: tracks individual issuance records with customer assignment, face value, issuance date, redemption status, and linked loyalty program. Serves as the SSOT for all discount code lifecycle management across all code types.'
}

Table pricing.coupon_redemption {
  coupon_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each coupon redemption event.']
  coupon_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the coupon definition.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who redeemed the coupon.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with the coupon redemption.']
  marketplace_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotional campaign linked to the coupon.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Coupon redemption tracking requires knowing the specific SKU discounted for fraud detection, promotion analytics, and financial reconciliation. applied_to_sku is a denormalized text reference to produ']
  applied_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the SKU that the coupon discount covered.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the coupon was redeemed (e.g., web, mobile app, in‑store, call center).']
  coupon_redemption_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the redemption attempt: success, failure, or partial.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the redemption record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied by the coupon.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the redemption was flagged for potential fraud.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional information or comments about the redemption.']
  redemption_code varchar [note: 'The coupon or voucher code that was redeemed.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the coupon was redeemed.']
  redemption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the coupon can be used: single‑use, multi‑use, or auto‑apply.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream operational system that generated the redemption record (e.g., OMS, PIM).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the redemption record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each coupon or voucher redemption event, including the redeemed code, order reference, customer reference, discount amount applied, redemption timestamp, channel, and redemption outcome (success, failure, partial). Provides the audit trail for all discount code usage and supports fraud detection and budget tracking.'
}

Table pricing.price_override {
  price_override_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the price override record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Price overrides with approval workflows and compliance_flag require cost center attribution for financial variance reporting. In e-commerce, approved overrides exceeding thresholds are charged to a co']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for whom the override is scoped, if applicable.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Price overrides affect revenue; finance requires a GL posting to reflect adjusted sales amounts and maintain audit trail of overrides.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with the price override.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: SOX compliance and internal audit require price override audit trails at the line item level. CSR or system price overrides are applied to specific order lines; without this FK, override reports canno']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Price overrides are applied to specific marketplace listings (e.g., manual price corrections on a listing). Marketplace compliance, buy-box audit trails, and override approval workflows require tracin']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: price_override overrides a specific price list item; linking eliminates redundant sku column']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Price overrides (e.g., CSR goodwill adjustments, competitive match) are applied to specific SKUs. A direct sku_id FK enables SKU-level override audit trails and compliance reporting; price_list_item_i']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price override took effect.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the approval workflow for the price override.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the override was approved by the designated approver.']
  audit_note varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text note added during audit actions.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the override complies with pricing governance policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the override record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price values.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the overridden price becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the overridden price expires; null if indefinite.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price override is currently active (true) or has been superseded/revoked (false).']
  original_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The price of the SKU before the override was applied.']
  overridden_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'The new price after the override was applied.']
  override_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the price override was applied manually, by an automated system, or via a pricing rule.']
  price_change_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Absolute difference between overridden price and original price.']
  price_change_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage change represented by the price override.']
  price_override_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price override.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the price override was applied.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing additional context for the override reason.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Defines the granularity of the override – whether it applies to the whole order, a single line, a specific customer, or globally.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the override record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the override record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a manual or system-initiated price override applied to a specific order line or SKU, capturing the original price, overridden price, override reason code, approving user, approval timestamp, and override scope (order-level, line-level, customer-specific). Supports exception pricing workflows and audit compliance.'
}

Table pricing.price_history {
  price_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each price change event.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Historical price changes are linked to GL for audit of revenue impact and compliance with financial reporting standards.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: FTC and marketplace compliance require listing-level price change history (e.g., reference price substantiation). price_history has sku_id but not listing_id — multiple listings can share a SKU with d']
  marketplace_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion or coupon that triggered the price change, if applicable.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the price list (e.g., base, promotional, regional) that the price belongs to.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Price History records must be tied to a SKU to support price audit, margin analysis, and regulatory reporting.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation or business justification for the price modification.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the price values.']
  effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment when the new price became effective in the marketplace.']
  initiated_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the automated system or service (e.g., pricing engine, rule engine) that generated the change.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the price change was manually overridden (true) or system‑driven (false).']
  price_after decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price value after the change was applied.']
  price_before decimal(12,4) [note: 'Price value before the change was applied.']
  price_change_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric difference between new and previous price (price_after - price_before).']
  price_change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change mechanism that caused the price update.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this price history record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record (e.g., correction of metadata).']

  Note: 'Immutable append-only log of all price changes for each SKU across all price lists, capturing previous price, new price, effective timestamp, change source (dynamic rule, manual override, markdown, promotion, experiment adoption), triggering entity reference, and the user or system that initiated the change. Supports price elasticity computation, regulatory compliance (price display laws, consumer protection), competitive analysis, rollback capability, and historical price auditing for dispute resolution.'
}

Table pricing.price_zone {
  price_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pricing zone.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Price zones are defined per marketing channel to support channel‑specific pricing; the link is required for channel pricing compliance reports.']
  delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery_zone. Business justification: Pricing zones are derived from delivery zones; the FK lets the Pricing engine fetch zone attributes (e.g., distance, surcharge) for accurate shipping cost calculations.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Price zones with geographic_boundary and currency_code must be scoped to a legal entity for transfer pricing compliance and tax jurisdiction assignment in multi-entity e-commerce operations. Domain ex']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Price zones are scoped to specific marketplaces (e.g., Amazon US vs. Amazon EU have distinct price zones). Pricing engines and zone management reports require knowing which marketplace a price zone go']
  parent_price_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_zone (parent_price_zone_id)']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the base price list associated with this zone.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Price zones are defined by customer segment criteria (e.g., B2B vs B2C zones with different base prices). Linking to customer.segment enables proper zone-to-segment assignment for pricing engine looku']
  channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel(s) to which the zone applies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zone record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency used for pricing within this zone.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the pricing zone becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the pricing zone expires or is superseded.']
  geographic_boundary varchar [note: 'Geospatial definition of the zone boundaries in GeoJSON format.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this zone is the default fallback zone.']
  is_experimental boolean [note: 'Indicates if the zone is part of an experimental pricing strategy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes about the zone.']
  price_elasticity_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Score representing price sensitivity for the zone.']
  price_zone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the pricing zone purpose and scope.']
  price_zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pricing zone.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Integer indicating priority order when multiple zones match; lower number higher priority.']
  region varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary region of the zone.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the zone record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the zone record.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the pricing zone.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the pricing zone.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the zone record.']

  Note: 'Geographic or customer-segment-based pricing zone definitions that determine which price list applies to a given buyer. Captures zone code, zone name, geographic boundaries, customer segment criteria, and effective date ranges.'
}

Table marketing.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign (primary key).']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: REQUIRED: Campaigns are created per brand; brand‑level ROI reports need a direct link to product.brand.brand_id.']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle promotions are a core e-commerce marketing tactic. Campaign managers run dedicated campaigns for product bundles (e.g., \'Buy X+Y, save 20%\'). Linking campaign to bundle enables bundle-level cam']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Tracks which carrier is featured in a shipping‑promotion campaign, supporting cost analysis and carrier‑specific promotional reporting.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: E-commerce campaigns frequently promote specific shipping services (e.g., \'free 2-day shipping this weekend\'). Linking campaign to carrier_service enables shipping-promotion campaign management, cost ']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Product launch and sponsored-product campaigns target specific catalog items. Merchandising and campaign management teams need to link a campaign to the exact catalog item being promoted for PDP boost']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: REQUIRED: Category‑targeted campaigns need to reference product_category.product_category_id for category performance dashboards.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Campaign uses a textual channel; replace with FK to channel for referential integrity and remove redundant column.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Campaign Cost Allocation Report linking each campaign to the cost center that bears its expenses.']
  delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.delivery_zone. Business justification: E-commerce campaigns are scoped to delivery zones for geo-targeted promotions (e.g., \'same-day delivery available in your area\'). Replacing the denormalized target_geography text field with a structur']
  fulfillment_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: Geo-targeted campaigns (same-day delivery, local promotions) are operationally tied to specific fulfillment centers. Operations and marketing teams jointly plan campaign eligibility based on center ca']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Supports GL posting of campaign‑related expenses for statutory accounting and audit trails.']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Marketing campaigns in multi-marketplace e-commerce are scoped to specific marketplaces (e.g., campaign runs only on marketplace A). Campaign budget allocation, performance reporting, and compliance r']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables Profitability Dashboard by attributing campaign revenue and costs to the appropriate profit center.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Seller co-op and sponsored campaigns (e.g., Sponsored Brands, Promoted Listings) are tied to a specific seller. This FK enables seller-level campaign spend reporting, co-op budget reconciliation, and ']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_sla. Business justification: Campaigns that promise specific delivery windows (e.g., \'Order today, delivered tomorrow\') must reference the fulfillment SLA backing that promise. This link enables campaign-SLA compliance validation']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: SUPPLIER-SPONSORED CAMPAIGN: tracks which supplier funds each marketing campaign for compliance, ROI attribution, and budget approval.']
  target_audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: Campaign targets an audience segment; replace string with FK to audience_segment and remove redundant column.']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'Creative format used in the campaign.']
  ad_group_hierarchy varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded representation of nested ad group structure for paid media.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Model used to attribute conversions to campaign interactions.']
  bidding_strategy varchar [note: 'Algorithmic approach used to bid for ad placements.']
  brand varchar [note: 'Brand under which the campaign is executed.']
  budget_adjustments decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of discounts, fees, or other adjustments applied to the gross budget.']
  budget_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total allocated budget before any adjustments.']
  budget_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final usable budget after adjustments.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the campaign purpose, messaging, and creative theme.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the marketing campaign.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Category of campaign based on delivery mechanism.']
  click_through_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of impressions that resulted in clicks.']
  conversion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of clicks that resulted in a conversion.']
  cost_per_acquisition decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average cost incurred for each conversion.']
  cost_per_click decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average cost incurred for each click.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the campaign record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency identifier for the budget.']
  daily_budget_cap decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum spend allowed per day.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned end date or expiration of the campaign.']
  expected_roi decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected ROI for the campaign based on budget and performance forecasts.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign is a test or production run.']
  lifetime_budget_cap decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total spend allowed for the entire campaign lifecycle.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary business goal the campaign is intended to achieve.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the campaign.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the campaign record.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter identifying the specific campaign.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter indicating the marketing medium.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter for tracking traffic origin.']

  Note: 'Master record for all marketing campaigns across owned (email, push, SMS), paid (SEM, display, social, shopping, video), and earned (referral, affiliate, organic social) channels. Captures campaign identity, type, channel, objective (acquisition, retention, upsell, brand awareness), budget allocation, flight dates, target audience segment, campaign owner, status lifecycle, UTM parameters, bidding strategy, daily/lifetime budget caps, target geography, platform-specific IDs, ad group hierarchy (as nested structure for paid media), ad format, and associated brand or product line. SSOT for all campaign definitions — performance, attribution, budget, and creative records reference this master. For paid media campaigns, includes ad group-level detail (keyword themes, audience targeting, bid amounts, group status) as child records within the campaign structure.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_performance {
  campaign_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each campaign performance record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Campaign performance records belong to a campaign; add FK and remove duplicated campaign descriptive fields.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: campaign_performance.channel is a STRING column storing a denormalized channel reference. Normalizing this to a FK channel_id -> marketing.channel.channel_id enforces referential integrity, enables co']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Campaign performance is reported and benchmarked per marketplace (ROAS, CTR, CVR vary by marketplace). Marketplace-level campaign performance dashboards and budget optimization decisions require this ']
  aov decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average monetary value of orders generated from the campaign.']
  audience_size varchar [note: 'Number of unique contacts targeted by the campaign.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch job that executed the campaign.']
  bounce_count varchar [note: 'Number of messages that bounced back undelivered.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Allocated monetary budget for the campaign.']
  cac decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average cost to acquire a new customer through this campaign.']
  campaign_objective varchar [note: 'Primary business objective the campaign aims to achieve (e.g., drive traffic, increase sales, boost engagement).']
  campaign_performance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign execution.']
  clicks varchar [note: 'Number of click actions generated by the campaign.']
  conversions varchar [note: 'Number of desired conversion events (e.g., purchases, sign‑ups) attributed to the campaign.']
  cpc decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average cost incurred for each click (spend_amount ÷ clicks).']
  cpm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Cost per one thousand impressions (spend_amount ÷ (impressions/1000)).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this performance record was first created in the data lake.']
  ctr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of clicks to impressions (clicks ÷ impressions).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  cvr decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of conversions to clicks (conversions ÷ clicks).']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Overall delivery outcome for the campaign batch.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total discount or rebate applied to the campaign spend.']
  impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of times the campaign content was displayed.']
  marketing_owner_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal marketing owner or manager responsible for the campaign.']
  net_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Revenue attributed to the campaign after discounts and adjustments.']
  platform_job_reference varchar [note: 'Reference ID from the marketing platform for the execution job.']
  roas decimal(10,4) [note: 'Revenue generated per unit of spend (net_revenue ÷ spend_amount).']
  rpm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Revenue generated per one thousand impressions (net_revenue ÷ (impressions/1000)).']
  scheduled_end timestamp [note: 'Planned end date‑time for the campaign execution.']
  scheduled_start timestamp [note: 'Planned start date‑time for the campaign execution.']
  send_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the campaign was sent or activated.']
  spend_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary spend on the campaign (gross).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this performance record.']

  Note: 'Operational record capturing each campaign deployment event and its resulting performance metrics. Combines execution metadata (send date/time, channel, audience size, delivery status, bounce count, batch ID, platform job reference) with engagement metrics (impressions, clicks, CTR, conversions, CVR, spend, revenue attributed, ROAS, CPC, CPM, RPM, CAC, AOV). One record per campaign activation per reporting period. Supports real-time campaign monitoring, ROI analysis, and budget optimization. Subsumes former campaign_execution concept.'
}

Table marketing.audience_segment {
  audience_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audience segment record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Brand-affinity audience segments (e.g., \'Nike loyalists\', \'Apple buyers\') are a core e-commerce targeting strategy. Linking audience segments to brands enables brand-level audience sizing, lookalike m']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Category-affinity audience segments (e.g., \'Electronics buyers\', \'Apparel shoppers\') are a foundational e-commerce marketing concept. Audience segment definitions must reference the product category d']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: audience_segment.channel is a STRING column storing a denormalized channel reference. Audience segments are scoped to specific marketing channels (email, SMS, push, display). Normalizing to channel_id']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: In multi-marketplace e-commerce, audience segments are scoped to specific marketplaces (e.g., segment valid only for marketplace X vs. Y). Marketplace-specific segmentation drives personalized targeti']
  source_customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Marketing audience segments are operationally derived from CRM customer segments. This link enables traceability from activation audience back to the CRM segment definition, supporting segment governa']
  audience_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or market scope of the segment.']
  audience_segment_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code for the segment, unique within the marketing domain.']
  audience_segment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the segment purpose, business rationale, and target audience.']
  audience_segment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the segment used for reporting and UI display.']
  audience_segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment.']
  audience_segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segment based on its creation logic (e.g., demographic, behavioral, predictive).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment record was first created.']
  creation_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the segment was created manually by a marketer or generated automatically by a model.']
  definition_logic varchar [note: 'Serialized logic (e.g., JSON, SQL, rule engine syntax) that defines inclusion criteria for the segment.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the segment becomes active for targeting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the segment is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  exclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Human‑readable summary of rules that exclude customers from the segment.']
  inclusion_criteria varchar [note: 'Human‑readable summary of rules that include customers in the segment.']
  is_dynamic boolean [note: 'True if the segment membership updates continuously; false if static snapshot.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the segment is used for A/B testing or experimentation.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the most recent update.']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent membership refresh operation.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Current number of customers included in the segment.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the segment owner.']
  owner_user_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing user or team that owns the segment.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Business objective of the segment (e.g., acquisition, retention, upsell, testing).']
  refresh_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the segment membership is recomputed.']
  region varchar [note: 'Region code (ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3) where the segment is applicable.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the segment definition is retained before archival.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system or data source used to build the segment.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of free‑form tags for ad‑hoc categorization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any segment attribute.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number tracking changes to the segment definition.']

  Note: 'Master record for reusable customer audience segments including membership composition. Captures segment definition (name, type, logic, refresh frequency, owner, status) and per-member association records (customer ID, membership dates, inclusion reason, membership status). Enables historical tracking of segment composition changes, CLTV-based cohort analysis, and real-time personalization decisioning. SSOT for marketing audience definitions and their evaluated membership.'
}

Table marketing.creative_asset {
  creative_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for creative_asset']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that uses this creative asset.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Creative assets (product images, videos, banners) are produced for specific catalog items. E-commerce creative management and compliance workflows require tracking which catalog item a creative repres']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Sellers create or sponsor creative assets for co-op advertising.   supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Supplier-funded co-op advertising requires tracking which supplier provided or funded each creative asset. E-commerce brands routinely receive supplier-provided product imagery and brand assets; linki']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the asset.']

  body_content varchar [note: 'Main body copy or description of the creative asset.']
  compliance_can_spam boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset complies with CAN‑SPAM regulations.']
  compliance_gdpr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset complies with GDPR data‑privacy requirements.']
  content_block_refs varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of content block identifiers used within the asset.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset record was created.']
  creative_asset_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the creative asset.']
  creative_asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the asset.']
  destination_url varchar [note: 'Target URL where the user is directed after clicking the creative.']
  display_url varchar [note: 'URL shown to users in the creative (e.g., in email footer).']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of the asset in seconds (applicable to video or audio).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the asset should no longer be used.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., DAM, CMS) to reference the asset.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the asset file in bytes.']
  format varchar [note: 'File format or rendering type of the asset.']

  personalization_tokens varchar [note: 'Tokens/placeholders for dynamic personalization (e.g., {{first_name}}).']
  platform_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the target marketing platform (e.g., SFMC, Google Ads).']
  subject_line varchar [note: 'Subject line used when the asset is an email template.']
  template_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the creative template.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the asset record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all marketing creative assets across email templates, ad creatives (text, image, video, carousel, responsive), push notification content, and SMS templates. Captures asset name, format type (email_template, ad_text, ad_image, ad_video, ad_carousel, push, sms), subject line/headline, body content/description, display URL, destination URL, media references (image/video URLs), content block references, personalization tokens, language/locale, compliance flags (CAN-SPAM, GDPR), approval status, platform-specific IDs, template version, and active status. SSOT for all creative content deployed in campaign execution across owned and paid channels. Supports creative performance comparison and A/B test variant tracking.'
}

  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Linking email_event to fulfillment_order enables tracking of ']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Linking email_event to logistics_shipment enables delivery notificat']
  bounce_category varchar [note: 'Classification of bounce when event_type is bounced.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of device used to view the email.']
  email_client varchar [note: 'Email client or application used to open the email.']
  email_subject varchar [note: 'Subject line of the email message.']
  event_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the event (e.g., success for delivered/opened, failure for bounce).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the email event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of email interaction event (e.g., sent, delivered, opened, clicked, bounced, unsubscribed). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spam_complaint — promote to reference product]']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device that generated the event.']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the email event was generated from a test send.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter ISO 639‑1 code representing the language of the email.']
  link_url varchar [note: 'Destination URL that was clicked in the email (populated for click events).']
  location_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 country code inferred from the IP address.']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was ingested into the lakehouse.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that produced the event record (e.g., salesforce_marketing_cloud).']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Full user‑agent string reported by the email client/device.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual email interaction events at the recipient level. Tracks event type (sent, delivered, opened, clicked, bounced, unsubscribed, spam complaint), event timestamp, recipient customer ID, campaign execution ID, email message ID, link URL clicked, device type, email client, IP address, and bounce category. Sourced from Salesforce Marketing Cloud tracking extracts. Enables deliverability monitoring and engagement analysis.'
}

Table marketing.attribution_touchpoint {

Attribution touchpoints must link to the catalog item viewed or converted on, enabling product-level ROAS, assisted-conv']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Attribution touchpoint records a channel; replace string with FK to channel.']
  converted_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.header. Business justification: Conversion attribution requires linking each touchpoint to the order it converted — the foundational marketing attribution join in e-commerce. Role-prefix \'converted_\' distinguishes this as the result']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Product-level marketing attribution (which ad drove purchase of a specific SKU) is fundamental to e-commerce ROAS reporting. Linking attribution_touchpoint to order_line enables SKU-level revenue attr']

Business justification: Seller-level marketing attribution (which channels drive traffic to which seller storefronts) is a core e-commerce analytics need. This FK enables seller ROAS reporting, seller storefront attribution ']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Attribution model applied to this touchpoint for revenue allocation.']
  attribution_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric score representing the weight of this touchpoint in the chosen attribution model.']
  browser varchar [note: 'Name of the web browser used (e.g., Chrome, Safari).']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the conversion event linked to this touchpoint, if any.']
  cost_attributed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Marketing cost associated with this touchpoint.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the customer\'s location at touchpoint time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the touchpoint record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the customer (web, mobile, tablet).']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the interaction recorded (click, view, conversion, impression).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device generating the touchpoint.']
  is_attributed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the touchpoint contributed to a conversion.']
  is_converted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the touchpoint ultimately led to a conversion.']
  keyword varchar [note: 'Search keyword or term that triggered the ad (if applicable).']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the landing page associated with the touchpoint.']
  operating_system varchar [note: 'Operating system of the device (e.g., iOS, Android, Windows).']
  page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page where the interaction occurred.']
  referrer_url varchar [note: 'URL of the page that referred the customer to the touchpoint.']
  revenue_attributed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount attributed to this touchpoint based on the selected model.']
  screen_resolution varchar [note: 'Screen resolution of the device (e.g., 1920x1080).']
  session_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the user session ended.']
  session_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the user session began.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that generated the touchpoint record (e.g., Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Google Ads).']
  touchpoint_position varchar [note: 'Position of the touchpoint within the customer\'s conversion journey.']
  touchpoint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the marketing touchpoint was recorded.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the touchpoint record.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'Browser user‑agent string captured for the touchpoint.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter identifying the specific campaign.']
  utm_content varchar [note: 'UTM content parameter used to differentiate ads or links.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter indicating the marketing medium (e.g., cpc, email).']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter indicating the origin of the traffic.']
  utm_term varchar [note: 'UTM term parameter for paid search keywords.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each marketing touchpoint in a customer\'s pre-conversion journey. Tracks customer ID, session ID, touchpoint timestamp, channel (paid search, organic, email, display, affiliate, social, direct), campaign ID, ad group, keyword, UTM source/medium/campaign/content/term, device type, and touchpoint position (first, middle, last). Feeds multi-touch attribution models (linear, time-decay, data-driven) for ROAS and CAC calculation.'
}

  incentive_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the referee incentive amount.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the referral record.']

  Note: 'Master record for customer referral program instances where an existing customer refers a new prospect. Captures referrer customer ID, referred prospect identifier, referral code, referral channel (email, social share, link), referral date, referral status (pending, converted, expired, rewarded), conversion order ID, referrer reward type and amount, and referee incentive type and amount. Supports referral program ROI measurement and viral acquisition tracking.'
}

Table marketing.channel {
  channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing channel.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Marketing channels (email, paid search, social) are assigned to cost centers for channel-level P&L reporting and spend accountability. E-commerce finance teams report marketing ROI by channel cost cen']
  parent_channel_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on channel (parent_channel_id)']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'Ownership category of the channel (owned, partner, third‑party).']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Business‑defined unique code for the channel.']
  channel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the channel purpose and characteristics.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the marketing channel.']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the channel.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the channel (e.g., email, SMS, push notification, social media, display advertising, affiliate).']
  compliance_ccpa boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel complies with CCPA requirements.']
  compliance_gdpr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel complies with GDPR requirements.']
  cost_model varchar [note: 'Pricing model applied to the channel (cost per mille, cost per click, cost per acquisition, fixed fee, subscription).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the channel record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days personal data associated with the channel is retained.']
  default_bid_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Default bid amount used for bidding‑based cost models.']
  default_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard budget allocated for the channel per campaign or period.']
  default_language_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter language code for default content in the channel.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the channel becomes active for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the channel is retired or no longer usable (nullable).']
  integration_method varchar [note: 'Technical integration method used to connect to the platform.']
  is_test_channel boolean [note: 'True if the channel is used for testing or sandbox purposes.']
  max_send_rate_per_minute varchar [note: 'Maximum number of messages that can be sent through the channel per minute.']
  opt_in_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit customer opt‑in is required to use the channel.']
  owner_user_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal party (team or individual) that owns the channel.']
  performance_benchmark_ctr decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target click‑through rate (percentage) used as a benchmark.']
  performance_benchmark_cvr decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target conversion rate (percentage) used as a benchmark.']
  performance_benchmark_impressions varchar [note: 'Target number of impressions used as a performance benchmark for the channel.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Underlying platform or service provider used to deliver the channel (e.g., Mailchimp, Twilio, Facebook Ads).']
  privacy_policy_url varchar [note: 'Link to the privacy policy governing data collected via the channel.']
  send_window_end_time timestamp [note: 'Local time (HH:mm) when the channel must stop sending messages.']
  send_window_start_time timestamp [note: 'Local time (HH:mm) when the channel is allowed to start sending messages.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary audience segment targeted by the channel.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the channel record.']

  Note: 'Master record defining marketing channels and their configurations (email, SMS, push notification, social media, display advertising, affiliate). Captures channel type, platform integration details, cost model, and performance benchmarks.'
}

  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the budget.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget was approved for execution.']

  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the budget record.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the budget record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking marketing campaign-level budget allocations, spend tracking, and ROI calculations. Captures allocated amount, spent amount, remaining budget, cost-per-acquisition targets, and budget period.'
}

Table marketing.ad_group {
  ad_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for ad_group']

Business justification: Sponsored-product ad groups in e-commerce (analogous to Amazon Sponsored Products) are organized around specific catalog items for item-level bid management. Ad group → catalog item is required for in']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Sponsored product advertising (Amazon-style) directly associates ad groups with specific marketplace listings. Ad group bid management, listing-level ad spend reporting, and buy-box-aware bidding all ']
  parent_ad_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ad_group (parent_ad_group_id)']
  ad_group_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the ad group used in reporting and UI.']
  ad_group_source varchar [note: 'Originating advertising platform for the ad group.']
  ad_group_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ad group.']
  ad_group_type varchar [note: 'Category of ad group based on the ad format or channel.']
  ad_rotation_mode varchar [note: 'How ads within the group are rotated during delivery.']
  bid_strategy varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to set bids for the ad group.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum amount allocated per day for this ad group.']
  budget_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the budget.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ad group record was initially created in the system.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current delivery condition of the ad group as reported by the platform.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the ad group stops serving (nullable for ongoing).']
  external_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the advertising platform (e.g., Google Ads ID).']

  frequency_cap_window varchar [note: 'Number of days over which the frequency cap applies.']
  is_experiment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ad group is part of an A/B test or experiment.']
  label varchar [note: 'User‑defined label or tag for grouping ad groups in UI.']
  optimization_goal varchar [note: 'Primary performance metric the ad group is optimized for.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first ingested into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the latest audit update to the record.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the ad group becomes eligible to serve.']
  targeting_criteria varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded rules defining audience, geography, device, and other targeting parameters.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the ad group record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the ad group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ad_group. Referenced by ad_group_id.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_creative_assignment {
  campaign_creative_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the campaign_creative_assignment association']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign that this creative asset is assigned to']
  creative_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the creative asset deployed within this campaign assignment']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifies which A/B or multivariate test arm this creative asset represents within the campaign. The same asset may be the control in one campaign and a variant in another, so this designation belongs to the assignment record.']
  is_primary_creative boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this creative asset is the designated hero or primary creative for the campaign. A campaign may have one primary creative and multiple supporting or variant creatives. Belongs to the assignment, not to the asset or campaign independently.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The specific ad slot, channel placement, or content zone this creative asset occupies within the campaign. Belongs to the assignment because the same asset may serve different placement roles across different campaigns.']
  usage_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this creative asset was retired or replaced within this campaign assignment. Enables historical tracking of which assets were active during which periods of a campaign.']
  usage_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this creative asset became active for this specific campaign assignment. Distinct from the campaign flight start date — an asset may be introduced mid-campaign or rotated in during optimization.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Assignment (Role) between campaign and creative_asset. It captures the operational record of a specific creative asset being deployed within a specific campaign, including placement context, active date range, primary/variant designation, and A/B test role. Each record links one campaign to one creative_asset with attributes that exist only in the context of this assignment — a creative asset may be assigned to many campaigns, and a campaign may deploy many creative assets, each assignment carrying its own placement and variant metadata.. Existence Justification: In e-commerce marketing operations, creative assets are reusable brand assets managed in a DAM system and deliberately assigned to multiple campaigns (e.g., a hero banner image used across a summer sale campaign AND a retargeting campaign simultaneously). Conversely, a single campaign deploys multiple creative assets (e.g., A/B test variants, different ad formats for different placements). The business actively manages these assignments through a creative assignment workflow, tracking placement type, date ranges, and variant roles — this is not derivable from transactional data but is an operational record humans create and update.'
}

Table finance.general_ledger {
  general_ledger_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for general_ledger']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: GL accounts belong to a specific legal entity\'s chart of accounts. general_ledger.company_code is a denormalized code; adding legal_entity_id provides a proper FK to the legal_entity master, enabling ']
  account_balance_gc decimal(28,6) [note: 'Current cumulative balance of the GL account in the group/consolidation currency. Used for multi-entity financial consolidation, group-level reporting, and GMV-to-revenue reconciliation across all company codes. Supports MRR and ARR tracking at the enterprise level.']
  account_balance_lc decimal(28,6) [note: 'Current cumulative balance of the GL account in the company code local currency. Represents the net of all debit and credit postings to this account for the current fiscal period. Used for trial balance generation, financial statement preparation, and period-end close reporting.']
  account_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification grouping GL accounts into functional categories such as cash and equivalents, accounts receivable, inventory, fixed assets, accounts payable, accrued liabilities, deferred revenue, cost of goods sold, operating expenses, etc. Supports financial statement line-item grouping and management reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash_and_equivalents|accounts_receivable|inventory|fixed_assets|accounts_payable|accrued_liabilities|deferred_revenue|cost_of_goods_sold|operating_expenses|other — promote to reference product]']
  account_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the GL account\'s business purpose, the types of transactions it records, and any special posting rules or restrictions. Used for financial governance documentation, new employee training, and SOX control documentation.']
  account_effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this GL account became active and available for financial postings. Used for account lifecycle management, historical reporting, and SOX audit trails. Postings with a document date before this date are not permitted.']
  account_effective_until date [note: 'The date after which this GL account is no longer available for financial postings. Null for accounts with no planned end date. Used for account decommissioning, chart of accounts rationalization, and SOX-compliant account lifecycle management.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'Organizational grouping of GL accounts within the chart of accounts that controls the number range and field selection for account master data maintenance. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA account group (KTOKS). Examples include balance sheet accounts, P&L accounts, cost accounts.']
  account_long_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the GL account providing complete context for financial reporting and audit purposes. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA GL account long text (TXT50). Used in detailed financial statements and regulatory filings.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the GL account as displayed in financial reports, trial balances, and the chart of accounts. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA GL account short text (TXT20).']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known, human-readable GL account number as defined in the chart of accounts. Used in all financial postings, journal entries, and financial statement line items. Corresponds to the SAP S/4HANA GL account key (SAKNR).']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GL account master record. Active accounts accept financial postings; blocked accounts are temporarily restricted from new postings; marked_for_deletion accounts are pending archival after period-end close. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA account blocking indicators.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Fundamental financial classification of the GL account per the accounting equation. Determines how the account appears on financial statements: asset and liability/equity accounts appear on the balance sheet; revenue and expense accounts appear on the income statement. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA account type (KTOKS).']
  alternative_account_number varchar [note: 'Alternative or country-specific account number used for local statutory reporting requirements. Enables mapping between the operational chart of accounts and country-specific or group chart of accounts. Supports multi-GAAP reporting and local statutory compliance.']
  chart_of_accounts_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the chart of accounts grouping to which this GL account belongs. In SAP S/4HANA, a chart of accounts is a classified directory of all GL accounts used by one or more company codes. Enables multi-COA structures for operational and group reporting.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA company code representing the legal entity or organizational unit for which the GL account is defined. Supports multi-company-code chart of accounts and financial consolidation across the enterprise.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA cost center code linked to this GL account for cost center accounting. Enables expense tracking and allocation by organizational unit, department, or function. Supports management reporting and budget variance analysis.']
  cost_element_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is also defined as a cost element in SAP Controlling (CO) module. Primary cost elements correspond to GL expense/revenue accounts; secondary cost elements are used for internal cost allocations and have no corresponding GL account. Drives cost center accounting and profitability analysis.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA user ID of the person who created this GL account master record. Required for SOX-compliant audit trails, segregation of duties controls, and financial governance. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA ERNAM field.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this GL account master record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation point for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA creation date and time fields.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the account currency. When set, all postings to this account must be in this currency. If null, the account accepts postings in any currency and balances are maintained in the company code currency. Critical for multi-currency financial reporting and foreign exchange management.']
  field_status_group varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA field status group code assigned to this GL account that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during document entry for postings to this account. Ensures data completeness and consistency for financial postings.']
  financial_statement_section varchar [note: 'Identifies which primary financial statement this GL account contributes to. Drives automated financial statement preparation and regulatory reporting. Critical for SOX-compliant period-end close and IFRS/GAAP financial reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (accounting period) within the fiscal year to which the current account balance applies. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, with additional special periods (13-16) for year-end adjustments. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA posting period (MONAT).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which the current account balance and period-end close data applies. Supports multi-year financial reporting, year-over-year comparisons, and SOX-compliant audit trails. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA fiscal year (GJAHR).']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA fiscal year variant code that defines the fiscal year structure (calendar year, non-calendar year, or shortened fiscal year) and the number of posting periods. Determines how fiscal periods map to calendar months for financial reporting.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA functional area classification that categorizes GL accounts by business function (e.g., sales, marketing, production, administration, research and development). Enables cost-of-sales accounting and functional income statement reporting per IFRS IAS 1.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this GL account within the account hierarchy tree, where level 1 represents the top-most grouping (e.g., total assets) and higher numbers represent more granular accounts. Used for financial statement rollup logic and management reporting drill-down.']
  is_balance_sheet_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account carries a balance that is reported on the balance sheet. Balance sheet accounts (assets, liabilities, equity) carry forward their balances at year-end; P&L accounts are reset to zero. Drives automated year-end closing and balance carryforward processes.']
  is_posting_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether direct financial postings are permitted to this GL account. When false, the account is a summary/header account used only for hierarchical reporting and cannot receive direct journal entry postings. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA posting block indicator.']
  is_reconciliation_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this GL account is a reconciliation account that is automatically updated when postings are made to subledger accounts (accounts receivable, accounts payable, asset accounting). Reconciliation accounts cannot receive direct manual postings. Critical for AR/AP subledger-to-GL reconciliation.']
  last_posting_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent financial posting made to this GL account. Used for account activity monitoring, dormant account identification, and chart of accounts maintenance. Supports period-end close validation and SOX control testing.']
  line_item_display boolean [note: 'Indicates whether individual line items posted to this GL account are stored and displayable for audit and analysis purposes. When true, each posting line is retained for drill-down reporting. Required for SOX audit trails and financial investigation. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA line item display indicator (XKRES).']
  normal_balance varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the GL account normally carries a debit or credit balance per double-entry bookkeeping rules. Assets and expenses normally carry debit balances; liabilities, equity, and revenue normally carry credit balances. Used for trial balance validation and automated posting rules.']
  open_item_management boolean [note: 'Indicates whether open item management is activated for this GL account. When true, individual line items remain open until they are cleared against offsetting entries (e.g., payment clearing for bank accounts, GR/IR clearing accounts). Essential for bank reconciliation and clearing account management.']
  parent_account_number varchar [note: 'Account number of the parent GL account in the account hierarchy. Enables multi-level account hierarchies for financial reporting rollups, management reporting, and financial consolidation. Null for top-level accounts in the hierarchy.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA profit center code associated with this GL account for profitability analysis and segment reporting. Enables revenue and cost attribution to business segments, product lines, or geographic regions. Supports IFRS 8 operating segment disclosures.']
  reconciliation_account_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the subledger type this reconciliation account is linked to. Determines which subledger transactions automatically update this GL account. Null or \'none\' for non-reconciliation accounts. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA reconciliation account type (MITKZ).']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Business segment identifier associated with this GL account for segment reporting. Supports IFRS 8 and US GAAP ASC 280 operating segment disclosures. Enables financial reporting by business segment such as marketplace, fulfillment, advertising, or subscription services.']
  sort_key varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA sort key code that determines which field is used to populate the allocation field in line items posted to this account. Controls how line items are sorted and displayed in account statements. Examples: 001 (posting date), 003 (document number), 031 (order number).']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Specifies the tax treatment applicable to postings on this GL account. Controls whether tax codes are required, optional, or not applicable for journal entries. Relevant for VAT/GST compliance, sales tax reporting, and indirect tax management. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA tax category (MWSKZ). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: input_tax|output_tax|not_relevant|all_tax_codes|minus_sign — promote to reference product]']
  tolerance_group varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA tolerance group assigned to this GL account that defines the permitted payment differences and rounding tolerances for clearing transactions. Controls automatic write-off of small differences during payment processing and bank reconciliation. Supports SOX controls over financial accuracy.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA user ID of the person who last modified this GL account master record. Required for SOX-compliant audit trails, change management controls, and financial governance. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA AENAM field.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this GL account master record. Tracks changes to account attributes for SOX-compliant audit trails and data lineage. Essential for change management controls and period-end close integrity.']

  Note: 'SSOT for the enterprise chart of accounts and GL account master data. Defines account codes, account types (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense), account hierarchies, and natural account groupings. Serves as the structural backbone for all financial postings, trial balance generation, and financial statement preparation. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA GL for multi-company-code chart of accounts and supports SOX-compliant period-end close.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each journal entry record in the general ledger. Serves as the primary key for the journal_entry data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  accounts_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Journal entries are generated from AP invoice postings and payment events. Linking journal_entry to accounts_payable enables AP-to-GL drill-through for three-way match reconciliation and audit trails.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Journal entries are generated from AR invoice postings, cash application, and write-off events. Linking journal_entry to accounts_receivable enables AR-to-GL drill-through for subledger reconciliation']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center responsible for the expenditure or revenue captured in this journal entry. Enables cost allocation, departmental P&L reporting, and budget variance analysis.']
  finance_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_bank_account. Business justification: Bank-related journal entries (bank receipts, payments, reconciliation postings) reference specific bank accounts. Linking journal_entry to finance_bank_account enables bank-to-GL reconciliation and tr']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Journal entries are posted within a specific legal entity\'s books. journal_entry.company_code is a denormalized code; adding legal_entity_id provides a proper FK to the legal_entity master for entity-']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Journal entries for COGS, revenue, and line-level tax are posted at the order line level. Finance teams need drill-down from GL journal entries to source order lines for transaction-level audit, COGS ']
