  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Contract management: contracts are negotiated between a specific seller and a supplier; linking enables contract compliance and SLA reporting per seller.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier/vendor party who is the counterparty to this contract. Links to the vendor master record in the procurement domain.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal amendments (change orders) that have been executed against this contract since its original execution. Provides a quick indicator of contract stability and negotiation history. Incremented each time a change order is approved.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is configured to automatically renew upon reaching its expiry date without requiring a new negotiation cycle. True = auto-renews; False = requires explicit renewal action. Critical for contract lifecycle management and budget planning.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this contract document indicating the sensitivity of its commercial terms and conditions. Restricted: highly sensitive strategic terms. Confidential: standard commercial terms not for external disclosure. Internal: accessible to all internal staff. Public: terms disclosed publicly (rare for vendor contracts).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the vendor contract as assigned by the procurement or ERP system (e.g., SAP scheduling agreement or outline agreement number). Used in all vendor communications, purchase orders, and audit trails.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor contract governing its operational validity. Draft: under internal review and not yet binding. Active: fully executed and in force. Amended: modified via change order but still active. Suspended: temporarily halted pending resolution. Expired: reached end date without renewal. Terminated: ended before expiry by either party.']
  contract_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable title or short name of the vendor contract (e.g., \'Annual Packaging Materials Supply Agreement FY2025\'). Used for display in procurement dashboards and contract registers.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement contract by commercial structure. Framework agreements establish terms for future orders; blanket purchase orders cover recurring purchases up to a ceiling value; spot contracts are one-time buys; consignment contracts allow inventory held at buyer premises billed on consumption; scheduled agreements define delivery schedules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: framework|blanket|spot|consignment|scheduled — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this vendor contract record was first created in the procurement system (SAP S/4HANA or data lakehouse). Represents the system-of-record creation time in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the contract value, pricing, and payment obligations are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and FX exposure reporting.']
  delivery_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the designated delivery destination (warehouse, fulfillment center, or plant) where the vendor must deliver goods under this contract. Maps to the WMS facility or SAP S/4HANA plant/storage location.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed mechanism for resolving commercial disputes between the buyer and vendor (e.g., binding arbitration, mediation, or court litigation). Determines the legal process to follow in case of contract breach or disagreement.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor contract becomes legally binding and commercially effective. Marks the start of the contract period for SLA enforcement, payment terms, and procurement eligibility.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract grants the vendor exclusive supply rights for the specified goods or services category, preventing the buyer from sourcing from competing vendors during the contract term. True = exclusive; False = non-exclusive.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the scope of exclusivity when the exclusivity flag is True. Defines the product categories, geographic regions, or channels covered by the exclusive arrangement (e.g., \'Exclusive supplier of private-label packaging materials for APAC fulfillment centers\').']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor contract is scheduled to expire and cease to be binding, unless renewed or extended. Nullable for open-ended evergreen contracts. Used to trigger renewal workflows and compliance alerts.']
  gdpr_dpa_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a GDPR-compliant Data Processing Agreement (DPA) has been executed with this vendor, required when the vendor processes personal data on behalf of the company. True = DPA in place; False = DPA not required or not yet executed. Mandatory for GDPR Article 28 compliance.']
  governing_law_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this contract (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Critical for cross-border procurement and dispute resolution.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the allocation of delivery costs, risks, and responsibilities between buyer and vendor. Governs logistics cost accounting and insurance obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent formal amendment or change order to this contract was executed and became effective. Null for contracts that have never been amended. Used to track contract evolution and trigger re-approval workflows.']
  last_amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the business reason for the most recent contract amendment (e.g., \'Price adjustment due to raw material cost increase Q3 2025\', \'Extended term by 12 months following supplier performance review\'). Supports audit trail and change management documentation.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The contractually agreed number of calendar days from purchase order placement to expected delivery at the designated receiving location. Used in inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and WMS receiving scheduling.']
  minimum_order_qty decimal(18,3) [note: 'The minimum quantity per purchase order that the vendor requires under this contract, as negotiated in the commercial terms. MOQ is a standard e-commerce and supply chain procurement metric used for inventory planning and replenishment decisions.']
  moq_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure applicable to the minimum order quantity (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram, PAL for pallet). Follows ISO 80000 / UN/CEFACT unit codes as used in SAP S/4HANA.']
  nda_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is in place with this vendor as part of or alongside this contract. True = NDA executed; False = no NDA. Relevant for IP protection, proprietary pricing, and technology sharing arrangements.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The agreed method by which payments to the vendor will be made under this contract. Bank transfer (ACH/wire) is most common for B2B procurement; letter of credit is used for international suppliers; open account for trusted long-term vendors.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Coded payment terms agreed with the vendor governing invoice due dates and early payment discounts (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment terms key (ZTERM) used in accounts payable processing.']
  penalty_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum cumulative financial penalty amount (in contract currency) that can be levied against the vendor under this contract over its full term. Caps total liability exposure for both parties. Null when no penalty cap is defined.']
  penalty_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contract contains financial penalty clauses for SLA breaches, late delivery, or quality failures. True = penalty clauses exist; False = no financial penalties defined. Drives penalty calculation workflows in accounts payable.']
  penalty_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The contractually defined financial penalty rate expressed as a percentage of the affected order value or invoice amount, applied when the vendor breaches SLA commitments. Null when penalty_clause_flag is False.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the contract expiry date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate. Used to trigger automated procurement alerts and prevent unintended auto-renewals.']
  signed_date date [note: 'The date on which the contract was formally executed (signed) by all authorized parties. May differ from the effective date when contracts are backdated or post-dated. Critical for legal enforceability and audit.']
  sla_defect_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually agreed maximum acceptable defect/rejection rate (as a percentage) for goods delivered under this contract. Exceeding this threshold constitutes an SLA breach and may trigger return merchandise authorization (RMA) processes and financial penalties.']
  sla_fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed minimum order fill rate (as a percentage) representing the proportion of ordered quantity that the vendor must fulfill completely per shipment. A fill rate below this threshold triggers SLA breach notifications and potential penalties.']
  sla_on_time_delivery_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed minimum on-time delivery rate (as a percentage) that the vendor must achieve. Measured as the percentage of purchase orders delivered on or before the agreed delivery date. Used for vendor performance scorecards and penalty clause triggers.']
  termination_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Null for contracts that expired naturally or are still active. Distinct from expiry_date which is the scheduled end; this captures unplanned or negotiated early termination.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Coded reason for early contract termination. Mutual agreement: both parties agreed to end early. Vendor breach: vendor failed to meet contractual obligations. Buyer breach: buyer defaulted. Force majeure: unforeseeable external event. Regulatory: compliance or legal requirement. Convenience: buyer exercised termination for convenience clause.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total maximum committed monetary value of the vendor contract over its full term, expressed in the contract currency. For blanket/framework agreements this is the ceiling value; for spot contracts it is the fixed order value. Used for spend analytics, budget commitment tracking, and SOX financial controls.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this vendor contract record in the procurement system. Updated on every change including amendments, status transitions, and field updates. Used for incremental ETL processing and change data capture in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master procurement contract governing the commercial relationship with a supplier, including full amendment and change order lifecycle. Captures contract type (framework/blanket/spot/consignment), contract number, effective and expiry dates, total contract value, currency, auto-renewal flag, payment terms, SLA commitments, penalty clauses, exclusivity terms, amendment history (change reason, effective date, revised terms, approver), and contract status (draft/active/amended/expired/terminated). Serves as the authoritative agreement record for procure-to-pay processes in SAP S/4HANA.'
}

Table procurement.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the goods receipt record in the procurement domain. Primary key for this transactional entity. Entity Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: In e-commerce warehouse receiving, the carrier delivering supplier goods must be formally tracked for carrier performance scoring, freight claims, SLA compliance reporting, and dock scheduling. The ex']
  po_line_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line_item. Business justification: A goods receipt record is tied to a specific PO line item for three-way match (PO line → GR → invoice). The existing FK to purchase_order covers the header level, but line-level traceability requires ']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase order against which this goods receipt is posted. Enables three-way match (PO, GR, invoice) in accounts payable and ERP procure-to-pay workflow.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Inventory: goods receipt records must be tied to the seller whose inventory is being replenished for accurate stock and cost tracking.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being received. Links to the product catalog for item-level identification, valuation, and inventory update in WMS.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Goods receipt directly updates a specific stock position (SKU+warehouse+lot). Inventory receiving workflow and on-hand quantity reconciliation require tracing which stock_position was incremented by e']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) who dispatched the shipment. Used for supplier performance monitoring, compliance tracking, and accounts payable settlement. Maps to the Vendor Master in SAP S/4HANA.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Goods receipts are put away to a specific bin/shelf location. storage_location_code is a denormalized reference to warehouse_location. Linking enables WMS putaway routing, bin capacity validation, and']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination warehouse or fulfillment center where the inbound shipment is received. Drives WMS inventory allocation and put-away workflows.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Date the shipment physically arrived at the destination warehouse dock. Compared against estimated arrival date to calculate supplier on-time delivery performance (OTD KPI).']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Advance Shipment Notice number provided by the supplier prior to dispatch. Used to pre-receive inventory in WMS, schedule dock appointments, and validate shipment contents against the PO. Format: ASN- followed by 10 digits.']
  batch_lot_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-assigned batch or lot number for the received goods. Critical for traceability, recall management, FIFO inventory management, and quality inspection correlation. Mandatory for perishable, regulated, or serialized products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the goods receipt record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage, compliance reporting, and record lifecycle management.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Customs entry or import declaration number assigned by customs authorities for cross-border shipments. Required for import duty reconciliation, trade compliance audit, and WTO regulatory reporting.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Supplier-issued delivery note or packing slip number accompanying the physical shipment. Used for receiving verification, three-way match, and dispute resolution with the supplier.']
  discrepancy_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net difference between quantity shipped (per ASN) and quantity received (physically counted). Positive value indicates shortage; negative value indicates overage. Used for supplier dispute resolution and accounts payable adjustment.']
  estimated_arrival_date date [note: 'Expected date of shipment arrival at the destination warehouse as communicated by the supplier or carrier via ASN. Used for dock scheduling, labor planning, and lead time performance measurement.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date of the received batch/lot, applicable for perishable, food, pharmaceutical, or time-sensitive products. Used for FIFO/FEFO inventory management and shelf-life compliance in WMS.']
  freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight cost associated with the inbound shipment as invoiced by the carrier or allocated from the purchase order freight terms. Used for landed cost calculation, freight accrual in accounts payable, and procurement cost analysis.']
  freight_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the freight cost is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency freight reconciliation and FX conversion in SAP S/4HANA.']
  gr_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the warehouse receiving team during the goods receipt process. Captures observations about shipment condition, packaging damage, partial delivery reasons, or special handling instructions not captured in structured fields.']
  gr_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the goods receipt document, used in supplier communications, three-way match, and ERP posting. Corresponds to the Material Document number in SAP S/4HANA MM (MIGO). Format: GR- followed by 10 digits.']
  gr_posting_date date [note: 'Accounting date on which the goods receipt is posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA. Determines the financial period for inventory valuation and accounts payable accrual. Critical for period-end close and SOX compliance.']
  gr_posting_status varchar [note: 'ERP posting status of the goods receipt document in SAP S/4HANA. Indicates whether the GR has been financially posted to the general ledger, is pending, has been reversed, or is blocked pending quality inspection clearance. Triggers three-way match in accounts payable upon \'posted\' status.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the transfer of risk and freight responsibility between supplier and buyer. Determines who bears freight cost and at what point title transfers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  is_hazardous_material boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods are classified as hazardous materials (HazMat) requiring special handling, storage, and transportation compliance. Triggers HazMat receiving protocols in WMS and regulatory reporting.']
  is_over_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quantity received exceeds the quantity ordered on the purchase order, beyond the configured over-delivery tolerance. Triggers procurement workflow for approval or return-to-supplier action.']
  is_under_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quantity received falls below the quantity ordered on the purchase order, beyond the configured under-delivery tolerance. May trigger a follow-up delivery or partial invoice processing in accounts payable.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment origin. Required for customs declarations, import duty calculations, and trade compliance reporting under WTO regulations.']
  origin_location varchar [note: 'Supplier dispatch location or origin facility from which the shipment was sent. May be a supplier warehouse address, factory location, or 3PL origin hub. Used for logistics planning, lead time analysis, and customs documentation.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of the quality inspection performed upon receipt of goods. Determines whether inventory can be released to available stock in WMS. \'conditionally_accepted\' indicates partial acceptance with documented deviations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_required|pending|in_progress|passed|failed|conditionally_accepted — promote to reference product]']
  quantity_ordered decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU originally ordered on the purchase order line. Baseline for over/under delivery calculation and three-way match validation in accounts payable.']
  quantity_received decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of the SKU physically counted and accepted at the destination warehouse during the receiving process. Posted to WMS inventory and used for GR document creation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  quantity_rejected decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU rejected during quality inspection due to damage, non-conformance, or specification failure. Drives Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) creation and supplier debit note processing.']
  quantity_shipped decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU declared by the supplier on the Advance Shipment Notice (ASN) as dispatched. Used to identify pre-receipt discrepancies between ordered and shipped quantities.']
  receiving_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the physical receiving process was completed and the GR document was confirmed in WMS. Used to calculate dock-to-stock cycle time and warehouse throughput metrics.']
  receiving_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the physical receiving process was initiated at the warehouse dock. Used to measure receiving cycle time, dock-to-stock time KPI, and WMS labor productivity.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inbound shipment from supplier dispatch through GR completion. Drives operational workflows in WMS and TMS. Values: in_transit (carrier has picked up), arrived (at dock), receiving_in_progress (WMS receiving active), completed (GR posted), exception (discrepancy or damage identified).']
  supplier_invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Supplier-provided invoice number associated with this goods receipt, used to link the GR to the corresponding accounts payable invoice for three-way match processing and payment settlement.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Status of the three-way match process between the purchase order, goods receipt, and supplier invoice in accounts payable. \'matched\' enables automatic invoice payment; \'exception\' requires manual review. Critical for SOX procure-to-pay controls.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number for the inbound shipment. Used to monitor shipment progress in TMS and correlate with carrier proof-of-delivery documentation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure in which shipped and received quantities are expressed (e.g., EA=Each, CS=Case, PK=Pack, KG=Kilogram, PAL=Pallet). Must align with the PO unit of measure for accurate three-way match and inventory valuation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|KG|LB|L|M|M2|M3|PAL — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the goods receipt record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization between WMS and ERP, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the full inbound lifecycle from supplier dispatch through physical receipt at a warehouse. Records ASN (Advance Shipment Notice) number, carrier, tracking number, origin location, destination warehouse, shipped and received quantities per SKU, unit of measure, batch/lot number, estimated and actual arrival dates, quality inspection status, discrepancy quantity, over/under delivery flag, freight cost, shipment status (in_transit/arrived/receiving_in_progress/completed/exception), and GR posting status. SSOT boundary: procurement owns inbound shipment tracking and GR posting; inventory domain owns post-receipt stock position updates. Triggers three-way match in accounts payable and inventory update in WMS.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_invoice {
  supplier_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the supplier invoice record in the procurement data platform. Primary key for the supplier_invoice data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Supplier invoice expense must be charged to a cost center for budgeting and variance analysis; replaces denormalized codes.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Links each supplier invoice to the GL account for proper posting, required by the accounts payable workflow.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Goods Receipt (GR) document confirming physical receipt of goods or services. Required for three-way match validation (PO/GR/Invoice). Maps to MIGO goods receipt posting in SAP S/4HANA.']
  payment_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: AP disbursement tracking: when a supplier invoice is paid, the outgoing payment transaction records the disbursement. Finance teams reconcile supplier_invoice against payment_transaction to confirm pa']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating Purchase Order (PO) against which this invoice is submitted. Central to three-way match validation — the PO defines the agreed quantities, prices, and terms that the invoice must reconcile against.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Finance: invoices are issued to sellers for goods received; linking supports accounts payable and tax reporting per seller.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) who submitted this invoice. Links to the supplier master record for vendor details, payment terms, and compliance status.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the invoice in the procurement approval workflow. Distinct from invoice_status which tracks the full lifecycle; approval_status specifically tracks the authorization decision required before payment release. ESCALATED indicates the invoice has been routed to a higher approval authority due to amount threshold or discrepancy.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the invoice was approved (or rejected) by the designated approver in the procurement workflow. Critical for SLA (Service Level Agreement) measurement of invoice processing cycle time and SOX audit trail.']
  bank_account_reference varchar [note: 'Masked or tokenized reference to the supplier\'s bank account designated for payment of this invoice. Stored as a reference token rather than raw account number to comply with PCI DSS. Full banking details are maintained in the secure vendor master.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier invoice record was first created in the procurement data platform. Serves as the audit trail creation marker and is used for invoice aging calculations from receipt date. Aligns with RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category requirement.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency AP processing and foreign exchange revaluation. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MIRO WAERS currency key.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cash discount amount available if payment is made within the early payment discount period as defined by the payment terms. Supports working capital optimization and dynamic discounting programs. Maps to SAP S/4HANA SKFBT cash discount base amount.']
  discount_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which payment must be made to qualify for the early payment discount specified in the payment terms. After this date, the full gross_amount is due by payment_due_date.']
  discrepancy_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary value of the discrepancy between the invoiced amount and the expected amount based on PO and GR. Quantifies the financial exposure of the mismatch for prioritization of resolution and escalation decisions.']
  discrepancy_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific discrepancy identified during three-way match validation or manual review. Provides context for resolution workflow, supplier dispute management, and audit trail. Populated when three_way_match_status is FAILED or PARTIAL_MATCH.']
  discrepancy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discrepancy identified during three-way match validation. PRICE_VARIANCE: invoiced unit price differs from PO price beyond tolerance; QUANTITY_VARIANCE: invoiced quantity exceeds GR quantity; MISSING_GR: no goods receipt found; DUPLICATE_INVOICE: invoice number already processed; INCORRECT_PO: PO reference invalid or mismatched; TAX_MISMATCH: tax amount inconsistency.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'Foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the invoice currency to the company\'s functional/local currency at the time of invoice posting. Relevant for cross-border supplier invoices. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MIRO KURSF exchange rate field.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal accounting period (month) within the fiscal year in which the invoice is posted. Two-digit format (01-12). Used for monthly accruals, period-end AP aging, and financial close reporting. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MONAT posting period.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the invoice is posted for accounting and financial reporting purposes. Used for period-end close, budget consumption tracking, and SOX-compliant financial reporting. Maps to SAP S/4HANA GJAHR fiscal year field.']
  functional_currency_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross invoice amount converted to the company\'s functional (local) currency using the exchange_rate at posting time. Used for GL posting, financial reporting, and SOX-compliant AP ledger entries in the finance domain.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total invoice amount inclusive of all taxes and charges (net_amount + tax_amount). Represents the total liability to be settled with the supplier. Used for AP aging, cash flow forecasting, and payment run in SAP S/4HANA.']
  invoice_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the goods or services being invoiced. GOODS: physical merchandise; SERVICES: professional or managed services; FREIGHT: logistics and shipping charges; UTILITIES: energy, water, telecom; CAPEX: capital expenditure items; INTERCOMPANY: invoices between related entities. Used for spend analytics and GL account determination.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date printed on the supplier\'s invoice document, representing the date the supplier issued the invoice. Used as the principal business event date for payment due date calculation and aging analysis. Distinct from the system receipt date.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned invoice number as submitted by the supplier on the invoice document. Used as the primary business identifier for three-way match (Purchase Order/Goods Receipt/Invoice) validation and AP correspondence. Maps to MIRO BELNR field in SAP S/4HANA.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the invoice within the procurement AP verification workflow. RECEIVED: invoice logged; UNDER_REVIEW: validation in progress; MATCHED: three-way match successful; DISCREPANCY: match failed; APPROVED: cleared for payment; REJECTED: invoice rejected; PAID: payment disbursed; CANCELLED: voided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RECEIVED|UNDER_REVIEW|MATCHED|DISCREPANCY|APPROVED|REJECTED|PAID|CANCELLED — promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice document type. STANDARD: regular goods/services invoice; CREDIT_NOTE: supplier-issued credit reducing liability; DEBIT_NOTE: additional charge from supplier; PRO_FORMA: preliminary invoice for customs/planning; RECURRING: periodic service invoice.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The invoiced amount for goods or services before tax, representing the base payable amount. Used as the primary monetary value for PO price comparison in three-way match. Part of the monetary triplet (net_amount + tax_amount = gross_amount).']
  payment_block_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice has been blocked from payment processing. True when a payment block is active due to discrepancy, pending approval, compliance hold, or manual block. Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment block indicator (ZLSPR) on the AP document. Finance domain uses this flag to exclude the invoice from payment run.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment must be made to the supplier to comply with agreed payment terms and avoid late payment penalties. Derived from invoice date and supplier payment terms but stored explicitly for operational payment scheduling and cash flow planning.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or method designated for settling this invoice. ACH: Automated Clearing House bank transfer; WIRE: international wire transfer; CHECK: paper check; VIRTUAL_CARD: single-use virtual procurement card; DYNAMIC_DISCOUNT: early payment via supply chain finance program. Informs the payment run configuration in SAP S/4HANA.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the agreed payment terms for this invoice (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for early payment discount). Determines the payment due date and any applicable early payment discount. Maps to SAP S/4HANA ZTERM payment terms key.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was physically or electronically received by the procurement team. Used to calculate payment due dates based on payment terms and to measure invoice processing cycle time. Maps to MIRO BUDAT (posting date) in SAP S/4HANA.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the approver when the invoice is rejected. Used for supplier communication, dispute resolution, and process improvement analytics. Populated only when approval_status is REJECTED.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA accounting document number (BELNR) generated upon posting of the invoice in MIRO. Serves as the cross-reference key between the procurement data product and the finance domain\'s AP ledger and GL entries. Null until the invoice is posted in SAP.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system or channel through which the invoice was received and entered into the procurement platform. SAP_MIRO: direct SAP AP verification entry; EDI: Electronic Data Interchange automated feed; SUPPLIER_PORTAL: self-service supplier submission; EMAIL: email-based invoice receipt; MANUAL: manual data entry by AP team.']
  supplier_reference_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s own internal reference or order number associated with this invoice, as provided by the vendor. Used for supplier reconciliation, dispute resolution, and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) matching with supplier systems.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax amount charged on the invoice, including VAT, GST, or applicable sales tax as specified by the supplier. Used for tax reconciliation and input tax credit recovery. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MIRO WMWST tax amount field.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applied to the invoice for VAT/GST determination and input tax recovery. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MIRO MWSKZ tax code, which drives the tax calculation procedure and GL tax account assignment.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of the three-way match validation comparing the Purchase Order (PO), Goods Receipt (GR), and supplier invoice for quantity and price alignment. PENDING: match not yet executed; MATCHED: all three documents reconcile within tolerance; PARTIAL_MATCH: some line items match; FAILED: material discrepancy detected. Core control in the procure-to-pay process per SAP MIRO.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier invoice record was last modified in the procurement data platform. Tracks the most recent status change, discrepancy update, or approval action. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.']

  Note: 'Supplier-submitted invoice record for goods or services delivered, owned by procurement for three-way match validation (PO/GR/invoice) and discrepancy resolution. Captures invoice number, invoice date, supplier reference, PO reference, invoiced amount, tax amount, currency, payment due date, three-way match status, discrepancy reason, approval status, and payment block flag. SSOT boundary: procurement owns invoice receipt, validation, and match status; finance domain owns payment posting, GL entry, and cash disbursement. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA MIRO for AP invoice verification.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_performance {
  supplier_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each supplier performance scorecard record. Primary key for the supplier_performance data product in the procurement domain.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.category. Business justification: Category-level supplier performance scorecards are a standard procurement KPI report in e-commerce. Category managers evaluate supplier on-time delivery, defect rates, and fill rates within their spec']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Performance monitoring: supplier performance scores are evaluated per seller to drive procurement decisions and SLA enforcement.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) being evaluated in this performance scorecard period. Links to the master supplier/vendor record managed in SAP S/4HANA Supplier Master.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: A supplier performance scorecard measures KPIs (on-time delivery, fill rate, defect rate) against SLA targets defined in the governing vendor contract. vendor_contract carries sla_on_time_delivery_pct']
  avg_actual_lead_time_days decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average number of calendar days between purchase order placement and goods receipt for all POs evaluated during the period. Compared against contracted lead time to compute lead_time_adherence_rate and inform replenishment planning.']
  contract_renewal_recommendation varchar [note: 'Procurement team\'s formal recommendation for the supplier\'s contract renewal based on this evaluation period\'s performance scorecard. Drives contract lifecycle management decisions in SAP S/4HANA. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity.']
  contracted_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The agreed-upon lead time in calendar days as specified in the supplier\'s active vendor contract or purchase agreement. Serves as the benchmark for lead_time_adherence_rate calculation and SLA compliance assessment.']
  corrective_action_count varchar [note: 'Number of formal corrective action requests (CARs) issued to the supplier during the evaluation period due to quality, delivery, or compliance failures. High counts indicate systemic supplier issues requiring escalation or probation review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this supplier performance scorecard record was first created in the system. Audit trail field aligned with ISO 27001 data governance requirements and SOX internal control documentation.']
  critical_sla_breach_count varchar [note: 'Number of high-severity SLA breaches that caused material business impact (e.g., stockouts, production stoppages, customer order cancellations) during the evaluation period. Subset of sla_breach_count; used for escalation and probation decisions.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which the KPI data for this scorecard was primarily sourced. SAP_S4HANA for ERP-driven metrics; WMS for warehouse receiving data; TMS for delivery performance; SELLER_PORTAL for supplier self-reported data; MANUAL for procurement team input.']
  defect_rate_dppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of defective units received per one million units delivered by the supplier during the evaluation period. Industry-standard quality metric (DPPM - Defective Parts Per Million) used to assess product quality compliance and drive supplier quality improvement programs.']
  development_program_enrolled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has been enrolled in a formal supplier development program as a result of this evaluation period\'s performance. Typically triggered by bronze tier or probation classification.']
  dispute_count varchar [note: 'Number of formal disputes raised by either Ecommerce or the supplier regarding PO terms, invoice discrepancies, or delivery claims during the evaluation period. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA dispute management module.']
  documentation_compliance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of shipments accompanied by complete and accurate documentation (packing lists, certificates of conformance, customs documents, safety data sheets) as required by contract and regulatory standards during the evaluation period.']
  evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the procurement evaluator to capture qualitative observations, context, or exceptions that are not reflected in the quantitative KPI scores (e.g., force majeure events, supply chain disruptions, extraordinary performance).']
  evaluation_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the performance evaluation window. Paired with evaluation_period_start_date to define the full measurement interval for all KPIs.']
  evaluation_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the performance evaluation window. Used to define the measurement boundary for all KPIs captured in this scorecard.']
  evaluation_period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the performance evaluation cycle: monthly for operational monitoring, quarterly for strategic reviews, and annual for contract renewal decisions.']
  fill_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ordered quantity actually fulfilled and shipped by the supplier during the evaluation period. Calculated as (units shipped / units ordered) × 100. Measures supplier\'s ability to meet demand without shortfalls, critical for inventory replenishment planning.']
  invoice_accuracy_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of supplier invoices that matched the corresponding purchase order without discrepancies (price, quantity, or terms) during the evaluation period. Calculated as (accurate invoices / total invoices) × 100. Impacts accounts payable efficiency and procure-to-pay cycle time in SAP S/4HANA.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this supplier performance scorecard record was most recently modified. Tracks revisions during the draft, review, and dispute resolution lifecycle stages. Required for data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  lead_time_adherence_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of purchase orders where the supplier\'s actual lead time met or was within the contracted lead time during the evaluation period. Calculated as (orders within lead time / total orders) × 100. Critical for inventory replenishment planning and MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) management.']
  moq_compliance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of purchase orders where the supplier honored the contracted MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) terms during the evaluation period. Non-compliance may indicate supply constraints or contract violations requiring procurement intervention.']
  on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of purchase orders delivered by the supplier on or before the confirmed delivery date during the evaluation period. Calculated as (on-time deliveries / total deliveries) × 100. Key operational KPI for SLA compliance monitoring.']
  open_corrective_action_count varchar [note: 'Number of corrective action requests (CARs) issued to the supplier that remain unresolved as of the scorecard evaluation date. Unresolved CARs beyond SLA timelines may trigger escalation to probation tier.']
  overall_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite weighted performance score (0–100) aggregating all KPI dimensions (on-time delivery, fill rate, defect rate, invoice accuracy, lead time adherence, return rate, SLA compliance, NPS) per the procurement scoring model. Primary driver for performance tier classification and contract renewal decisions.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Classified performance tier assigned to the supplier based on the weighted overall performance score for the evaluation period. Gold = top-tier preferred supplier; Silver = satisfactory; Bronze = needs improvement; Probation = at risk of contract termination. Drives supplier development programs and contract renewal decisions.']
  previous_performance_tier varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s performance tier from the immediately preceding evaluation period. Used to detect tier reclassification events and trigger automated supplier development workflows.']
  procurement_cost_variance_pct decimal(6,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between the contracted unit price and the actual invoiced price across all POs in the evaluation period. Positive value indicates supplier overcharged vs. contract; negative indicates undercharge. Flags pricing compliance issues for AP reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  procurement_nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the internal procurement team rating the supplier\'s responsiveness, communication quality, and overall partnership experience during the evaluation period. Scale: -100 to +100. Captures qualitative relationship health not reflected in operational KPIs.']
  return_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of received units returned to the supplier due to defects, non-conformance, or incorrect shipments during the evaluation period. Calculated as (units returned / units received) × 100. Feeds into RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) processing and supplier quality programs.']
  scorecard_published_date date [note: 'The date on which the finalized supplier performance scorecard was formally published and shared with the supplier. Marks the start of the supplier\'s response and dispute window.']
  scorecard_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this supplier performance scorecard record, used in procurement communications, supplier development meetings, and audit trails. Format: SPC-YYYY-MM-SUPPID.']
  scorecard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the supplier performance scorecard. Draft indicates data collection in progress; pending_review awaits procurement manager sign-off; approved is finalized; published is shared with supplier; disputed is under supplier challenge; closed is archived.']
  sla_breach_count varchar [note: 'Total number of SLA (Service Level Agreement) violations recorded against the supplier during the evaluation period. Includes delivery, quality, and communication SLA breaches as defined in the vendor contract. Triggers penalty clauses and escalation workflows.']
  supplier_acknowledged_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has formally acknowledged receipt and review of the published performance scorecard via the Seller Management Portal. Required for audit trail and dispute resolution process initiation.']
  supplier_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date on which the supplier formally acknowledged the performance scorecard in the Seller Management Portal. Null if supplier_acknowledged_flag is False. Used to track response timeliness.']
  sustainability_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0–100) reflecting the supplier\'s adherence to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) standards and Ecommerce\'s supplier code of conduct during the evaluation period. Assessed via audits, certifications, and self-reported data. Increasingly required for regulatory and investor reporting.']
  tier_reclassification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier\'s performance tier changed between the previous and current evaluation period. True triggers automated supplier development program enrollment or contract review workflows in the ERP.']
  total_pos_evaluated varchar [note: 'Total number of purchase orders (POs) included in the performance evaluation for this period. Provides the denominator context for rate-based KPIs and ensures statistical significance of the scorecard.']
  total_units_received varchar [note: 'Total quantity of units received from the supplier across all purchase orders during the evaluation period. Used as the volume denominator for defect rate, return rate, and fill rate calculations. Sourced from WMS (Warehouse Management System) receiving records.']

  Note: 'Periodic supplier performance scorecard capturing operational KPIs per supplier per evaluation period (monthly/quarterly/annual). Tracks on-time delivery rate, fill rate, defect rate (DPPM), invoice accuracy rate, lead time adherence, return rate, SLA breach count, NPS from procurement team, overall weighted performance score, and performance tier classification (gold/silver/bronze/probation). Drives supplier development programs, contract renewal decisions, and automated supplier tier reclassification. SSOT for supplier operational performance metrics; does not own financial credit risk scores (finance domain).'
}

Table procurement.supplier_price_list {
  supplier_price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the supplier price list record. Primary key for this entity in the procurement domain Silver layer.']
  buyer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: B2B e-commerce supports account-specific supplier pricing (negotiated rates for enterprise buyers). supplier_price_list already has segment_id for segment-level pricing, but account-level pricing is a']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Price‑list maintenance needs cost‑center attribution for budgeting of negotiated pricing programs.']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Ensures price‑list changes are reflected in the GL for expense forecasting and audit compliance.']
  marketplace_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_listing. Business justification: Supplier price lists define the buy-side cost for products listed on the marketplace. Linking supplier_price_list to marketplace_listing enables real-time gross margin calculation (listing price minus']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Enables segment‑specific pricing; the pricing engine selects a price list based on the customer.segment of the buyer.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Pricing: sellers receive negotiated price lists from suppliers; linking allows price‑list selection and margin calculations per seller.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this price list entry applies. Each price list record covers exactly one SKU.']
  superseded_by_supplier_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer supplier_price_list record that replaced this one when status is \'superseded\'. Enables version chain traversal for price history analysis and audit. Null if this is the current active version.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) who owns this price list. Links to the supplier master record in the procurement domain.']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor contract or purchasing outline agreement under which this price list is governed. Null if the price list is a standalone purchasing info record not tied to a formal contract.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this price list was formally approved by the authorized procurement officer. Required for SOX compliance audit trails and procurement governance reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the procurement officer or manager who approved this price list for use in purchase order processing. Supports SOX procurement controls and audit trail requirements.']
  auto_po_pricing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price list is enabled for automatic price determination during purchase order creation in the ERP system. When True, the system automatically applies this price list during PO generation without manual price entry.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this price list record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker required for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking in the Silver layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit price and all pricing tiers are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency procurement and FX conversion in cost-of-goods calculations.']
  discount_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The agreed percentage discount applied to the list price to arrive at the net unit price. Represents the base-level trade discount negotiated with the supplier, separate from volume-tier discounts. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 = 5%).']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this price list becomes valid and can be used for automated purchase order price determination and cost-of-goods calculations. Aligns with the validity start date in the ERP purchasing info record.']
  erp_info_record_number varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA Purchasing Info Record (PIR) number corresponding to this price list entry. Enables direct traceability between the Silver layer data product and the ERP system of record for procurement price determination.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this price list is no longer valid for purchase order pricing. Null indicates an open-ended price list with no defined expiration. Used to trigger price list renewal workflows.']
  freight_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether freight/shipping costs are included in the quoted unit price. When True, no separate freight charge is added during PO creation. When False, freight is billed separately or calculated via the TMS.']
  has_volume_tiers boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price list includes a volume discount schedule (quantity break tiers) in addition to the base unit price. When True, the associated supplier_price_list_tier records define the tiered pricing structure.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between the supplier and the e-commerce platform for shipments under this price list (e.g., FOB, DDP, EXW). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterm_location varchar [note: 'The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code (e.g., \'Port of Shanghai\' for FOB, \'Buyer Warehouse\' for DDP). Required to fully specify the delivery terms per ICC Incoterms 2020 rules.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The agreed supplier lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to expected delivery at the warehouse. Used in inventory replenishment planning, safety stock calculations, and SLA monitoring.']
  moq decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum quantity of the SKU that must be ordered per purchase order line to qualify for the agreed unit price. Orders below the MOQ may be rejected by the supplier or subject to a surcharge. Key procurement constraint for replenishment planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for procurement team annotations, special conditions, or supplier-communicated caveats associated with this price list (e.g., \'Price valid only for orders placed before end of quarter\', \'Subject to raw material surcharge above $X/tonne\').']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country of origin for the SKU under this price list. Used for import duty calculation, customs compliance, trade restriction checks, and landed cost estimation.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms code applicable to purchases under this price list (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Aligns with the payment terms configured in the ERP vendor master and accounts payable module.']
  price_basis varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the unit price represents the gross list price (before discounts), the net agreed price (after discounts), or the landed cost (inclusive of freight, duties, and insurance). Critical for correct COGS calculation and PO price validation.']
  price_list_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the price list indicating its commercial purpose. \'standard\' = regular agreed pricing; \'promotional\' = time-limited promotional pricing; \'seasonal\' = seasonal pricing adjustment; \'clearance\' = end-of-life or excess inventory pricing; \'emergency\' = expedited sourcing price.']
  price_list_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the price list, such as \'Q1 2025 Electronics Pricing\' or \'Annual Volume Agreement - Supplier XYZ\'. Used for display and search in procurement portals.']
  price_list_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this price list, used in supplier communications, purchase order references, and ERP purchasing info records. Follows the format PL-{alphanumeric code}.']
  price_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price list by its commercial basis. \'contract_based\' = tied to a formal vendor contract; \'standalone_pir\' = standalone Purchasing Info Record (PIR) in ERP; \'spot_quote\' = one-time quoted price; \'catalog\' = supplier-published catalog price; \'blanket_order\' = price under a blanket purchase order.']
  price_list_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this price list, enabling tracking of successive revisions for the same supplier-SKU combination. Incremented each time the supplier submits a revised pricing schedule.']
  price_tolerance_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The maximum allowable percentage deviation between the price list unit price and the actual invoice price before a three-way match exception is raised. Used in automated invoice verification to flag price discrepancies requiring manual review.']
  price_unit_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity basis for which the unit price is quoted (e.g., price per 1 unit, per 100 units, per 1000 units). Used to correctly compute per-unit cost when the supplier quotes prices in bulk multiples. Corresponds to the SAP Purchasing Info Record \'Price Unit\' field.']
  renewal_reminder_days varchar [note: 'The number of days before the expiry date at which a renewal reminder notification should be triggered to the procurement team. Supports proactive price list renewal management and prevents procurement disruption due to expired pricing.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number of the source document from which this price list was derived, such as a supplier quotation number, RFQ response number, or EDI catalog transmission ID. Provides traceability back to the originating supplier communication.']
  supplier_price_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price list. \'active\' = currently valid for PO pricing; \'expired\' = past expiry date; \'superseded\' = replaced by a newer version; \'draft\' = under preparation; \'pending_approval\' = awaiting procurement approval; \'suspended\' = temporarily deactivated.']
  supplier_sku_code varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s own part number or item code for this SKU, as used in the supplier\'s catalog and order acknowledgements. Required for EDI purchase order transmission and supplier invoice matching. Distinct from the internal SKU identifier.']
  supplier_sku_description varchar [note: 'The supplier\'s description of the SKU as it appears in their catalog or price list document. Used for three-way matching during invoice verification and for resolving discrepancies between internal and supplier item descriptions.']
  tax_classification_code varchar [note: 'The tax classification code assigned to this SKU-supplier combination for VAT/GST determination during purchase order processing. Maps to the tax condition type in the ERP pricing procedure.']
  tier_count varchar [note: 'The number of quantity break tiers defined in the volume discount schedule for this price list. Zero if has_volume_tiers is False. Provides a quick summary of pricing complexity without joining to tier detail records.']
  unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'The agreed supplier unit price for one unit of the SKU at the base quantity tier. This is the SSOT for supplier-agreed cost used in automated PO price validation and cost-of-goods (COGS) calculations. Does not represent the selling price or invoice amount.']
  uom_code varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the SKU is priced and ordered (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, KG for kilogram, L for litre). Aligns with the UOM defined in the product master and used in purchase order line items.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this price list record was last modified. Used for incremental ETL processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Agreed pricing catalog between the e-commerce platform and a supplier for specific SKUs, maintained under a contract or as a standalone purchasing info record. Captures supplier, SKU, price list version, unit price, currency, quantity break tiers (volume discount schedules), discount percentage, effective date, expiry date, and price list status (active/expired/superseded). SSOT for supplier-agreed pricing used in automated PO price validation, cost-of-goods calculations, and price comparison during requisition-to-PO conversion. Does not own dynamic selling prices (pricing domain) or actual invoice amounts (supplier_invoice).'
}

Table procurement.approved_vendor_list {
  approved_vendor_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each record in the approved vendor list.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.brand. Business justification: Brand-authorized supplier qualification is a critical e-commerce procurement process: brand owners (e.g., Apple, Nike) authorize specific suppliers to source their products. Brand managers and procure']
  listing_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.listing_category. Business justification: Vendor approval is often category-scoped — a supplier approved for Electronics may not be approved for Hazmat or Age-Restricted categories. This FK links AVL entries to marketplace listing categories,']
  marketplace_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace. Business justification: Vendor approval is marketplace-specific in multi-marketplace platforms — a supplier approved for one marketplace may not be approved for another due to differing compliance, regulatory, or category re']
  primary_approved_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor (supplier) referenced in the approved list.']
  superseded_approved_vendor_list_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on approved_vendor_list (superseded_approved_vendor_list_id)']
  vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: The approved_vendor_list carries denormalized contract fields (contract_number, contract_start_date, contract_end_date, contract_status) that duplicate data already authoritatively stored in vendor_co']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the vendor\'s approval within the list.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor was officially approved.']
  compliance_iso_certified boolean [note: 'True if the vendor holds a current ISO certification relevant to the business.']
  cpsc_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiration of the vendor\'s CPSC product safety certification.']
  cpsc_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with Consumer Product Safety Commission regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approved vendor record was first created.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Logical source identifier used for data lineage tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s approved status becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s approved status expires, requiring re‑evaluation.']
  gdpr_compliant boolean [note: 'True if the vendor adheres to GDPR data‑protection obligations.']
  is_diversity_supplier boolean [note: 'True if the vendor qualifies under the company\'s diversity supplier program.']
  is_preferred_vendor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is marked as a preferred supplier for strategic sourcing.']
  iso_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s ISO certification.']
  last_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent periodic review of the vendor\'s approval status.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days the vendor takes to deliver after order receipt.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered from the vendor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the vendor.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for payments to the vendor.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the agreed payment terms (e.g., NET30, EOM).']
  pci_dss_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor complies with PCI DSS requirements.']
  pci_dss_expiry date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s PCI DSS compliance certification expires.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the vendor\'s primary contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main point‑of‑contact for the vendor.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the vendor\'s primary contact.']
  qualification_criteria_met varchar [note: 'Textual description of the specific criteria the vendor satisfied for approval.']
  qualification_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score reflecting how well the vendor meets qualification criteria.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from the risk score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated risk rating assigned to the vendor based on financial, compliance, and performance factors.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system that supplied the vendor data (e.g., SAP, Oracle).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  vendor_category varchar [note: 'Broad product or service category the vendor supplies (e.g., electronics, apparel).']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor based on its business role.']

  Note: 'Master list of pre-qualified and approved vendors eligible for purchase order issuance, capturing approval status, qualification criteria met, and expiration dates.'
}

Table service.support_case {
  support_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer support case or service ticket. Primary key for the service support case entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Account‑level case reporting KPI for B2B customers requires linking each support case directly to the billing account.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Billing dispute cases require direct reference to the AR record for resolution, dunning holds, and dispute status updates. E-commerce customer service teams routinely open cases against specific invoi']
  assigned_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent currently assigned to work on this case. May be null if case is unassigned or in queue.']
  assigned_team_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.team. Business justification: service_support_case currently stores assigned_queue as a denormalized STRING representing the team or queue a case is routed to. Adding assigned_team_id → service.team.team_id normalizes this relatio']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Support cases are raised for bundle-specific issues (missing component, bundle pricing dispute, partial return eligibility). Agents need to identify the bundle to apply correct return policy and calcu']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Support case investigations often need the carrier responsible for the shipment to resolve delivery issues; agents reference carrier_id for accurate root‑cause analysis.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: When a case concerns service level or transit time, the specific carrier service used must be known for SLA compliance reporting.']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Product quality reporting, CPSC compliance tracking, and recall management require grouping all support cases by catalog item across all SKUs. Agents investigating product-level defects need this link']
  center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.center. Business justification: Support cases often arise from issues at a specific fulfillment center (e.g., inventory or pickup problems); linking enables Center‑Level case reporting and SLA tracking.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Omnichannel support routing and channel-level SLA assignment require linking each case to its originating marketing channel. E-commerce operations report CSAT and resolution time by channel; `originat']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: In B2B e-commerce, support cases are raised by a specific contact (buyer persona within an account). Contact-level case tracking drives SLA reporting per contact, escalation routing, and communication']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocation report requires each support case to be charged to a cost center for internal accounting.']
  coupon_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.coupon_redemption. Business justification: Coupon failure and misapplication are among the top support case drivers in e-commerce. Linking the case directly to the coupon_redemption record allows agents to inspect redemption status, fraud flag']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_profile. Business justification: GDPR subject access requests and B2C NPS attribution require direct case-to-profile linkage. Service platforms universally link cases to the individual customer profile for personalization, data subje']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Support cases for missing items, wrong items, or late delivery are opened against a specific fulfillment order. Agents need direct access to fulfillment order status, method, and SLA tier during case ']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Goods receipt discrepancy case management: when buyers report receiving wrong or damaged goods, the service case must trace to the GR record for three-way match investigation and discrepancy resolutio']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order associated with this support case, if the case relates to a specific order (e.g., shipping issue, order cancellation, item defect).']
  knowledge_article_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the knowledge base article used to resolve this case. Links case resolution to documented solutions for knowledge management and self-service improvement.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Support cases for wrong/damaged/missing items require line-level order linkage for accurate case routing, seller accountability reporting, and SLA tracking. The existing header_id identifies the order']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment.   loyalty_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.loyalty_enrollment. Business justification: customer_entitlement_tier on service_support_case requires real-time loyalty tier validation for SLA routing and VIP case handling. Direct FK to loyalty_enrollment enables tier-based case prioritizati']
  marketplace_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_promotion. Business justification: Support cases frequently arise from marketplace promotion disputes (promotional price not honored, eligibility errors). Direct FK to marketplace_promotion enables promotion-specific case routing, FTC ']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Required for support case reporting: each case must reference the specific marketplace transaction that triggered the issue, enabling accurate SLA tracking and root‑cause analysis.']
  parent_case_service_support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent case if this case is a child or follow-up case. Used for linking related cases and tracking case hierarchies (e.g., original case and subsequent escalation or related inquiry).']
  price_override_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_override. Business justification: Pricing dispute cases — where customers question why they were charged a non-standard price — require agents to directly inspect the price_override record. This is one of the most common case types in']
  product_listing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_listing. Business justification: Cases originating from listing-level issues (incorrect price displayed, suppressed listing, wrong description) require direct listing linkage for listing quality reporting, suppression root-cause anal']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis of support services links cases to profit centers to measure contribution.']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Required for Promotion Impact on Support Cases report, linking each case to the promotional campaign that triggered the purchase, enabling analysis of support volume per campaign.']
  proof_of_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.proof_of_delivery. Business justification: Delivery dispute resolution is a named e-commerce support process: when a customer claims non-delivery, the agent must access the POD record (photo, GPS, signature) directly from the case. No existing']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: PO dispute case management: buyers raise service cases referencing a specific purchase order for unfulfilled, incorrect, or billing-disputed POs. This link enables procurement dispute resolution workf']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RMA record if this support case resulted in or is associated with a product return authorization.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit of the product related to this support case, if the case involves a specific product (e.g., defect, return, compatibility issue).']
  sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_policy. Business justification: service_support_case currently stores sla_policy_code as a denormalized STRING reference to the governing SLA policy. Adding a proper FK sla_policy_id → service.sla_policy.sla_policy_id normalizes thi']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier.   supplier_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_invoice.   vendor_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Contract dispute/SLA breach case management: service cases raised for vendor contract violations (missed SLAs, exclusivity breaches, penalty triggers) must reference the governing vendor contract.   case_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique case identifier displayed to customers and agents. Typically follows a format like CS-12345678 or CASE-0001234567.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the support case by the nature of the customer issue: billing inquiry, shipping problem, product defect, account access issue, returns processing, refund request, marketplace dispute, or general inquiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing|shipping|product_defect|account_access|returns|refund|marketplace_dispute|general_inquiry — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was formally closed and removed from active case inventory. May occur after resolution once customer confirms satisfaction or after a waiting period.']
  compensation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary compensation or goodwill credit provided to the customer as part of case resolution (e.g., account credit, discount voucher, refund). Null if no compensation was provided.']
  contact_attempts varchar [note: 'Number of times the support team attempted to contact the customer regarding this case. Used for tracking customer responsiveness and case aging.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country from which the customer initiated the support case (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the support case was first created in the system. Marks the beginning of the case lifecycle and SLA clock.']
  csat_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Customer satisfaction score collected after case resolution, typically on a scale of 1.00 to 5.00. Measures customer satisfaction with the support experience and resolution quality.']
  customer_entitlement_tier varchar [note: 'The service entitlement level of the customer at the time the case was created: standard (basic support), premium (priority support), VIP (dedicated support), enterprise (white-glove service). Influences SLA targets and routing.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether and to what level the case has been escalated: not_escalated (standard handling), tier2 (specialist support), tier3 (expert/engineering), management (supervisor review), executive (C-level attention).']
