  feedback_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text feedback comments provided by the customer in post-case surveys. Captures qualitative insights about the support experience, agent performance, and areas for improvement.']
  first_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the first substantive response was provided to the customer by an agent. Key SLA metric for measuring initial response time.']
  interaction_count varchar [note: 'Total number of customer-agent interactions (calls, emails, chats) associated with this case. Indicates case complexity and effort required for resolution.']
  is_vip_case boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this case involves a VIP or high-value customer requiring special handling, expedited service, or executive attention. True for VIP cases, False otherwise.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the case was handled (e.g., en for English, es for Spanish, fr for French).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to any field in the case record. Used for tracking case activity and audit purposes.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score collected from the customer after case resolution, on a scale of 0 to 10. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend the company based on the support experience.']
  outcome_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the case: resolved (issue successfully addressed), unresolved (could not be resolved), duplicate (merged with another case), transferred (moved to another system or team), withdrawn (customer cancelled request).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the case based on urgency and impact. P1 (critical/urgent), P2 (high), P3 (medium), P4 (low). Determines SLA targets and routing rules.']
  reopened_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this case has been reopened after being marked as resolved or closed. Indicates resolution quality issues or recurring customer problems.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the resolution provided to the customer, actions taken, root cause identified, and any follow-up required. Populated when case is resolved or closed.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was marked as resolved, indicating that a solution was provided to the customer. Key SLA metric for measuring time-to-resolution.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying root cause identified during case resolution (e.g., system_error, process_gap, product_defect, user_error, policy_exception, carrier_issue). Used for trend analysis and continuous improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: system_error|process_gap|product_defect|user_error|policy_exception|carrier_issue|payment_failure|inventory_issue|data_quality — promote to reference product]']
  sentiment_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Automated sentiment analysis score derived from customer communications (emails, chat transcripts) during the case lifecycle. Scale typically -1.00 (very negative) to +1.00 (very positive).']
  service_support_case_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the customer issue, inquiry, or request as captured during case creation. May include customer-provided details, agent notes, and initial diagnostic information.']
  service_support_case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the support case: new (just created), open (assigned), pending_customer (awaiting customer response), pending_internal (awaiting internal action), in_progress (actively being worked), escalated (moved to higher tier), resolved (solution provided), closed (finalized), cancelled (withdrawn). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new|open|pending_customer|pending_internal|in_progress|escalated|resolved|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this case breached its SLA targets for first response time or resolution time. True indicates SLA was breached, False indicates SLA was met.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief summary or subject line describing the customer issue or inquiry. Typically 100-200 characters.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a customer support case or service ticket. Tracks the full lifecycle from creation through resolution including case type (billing, shipping, product defect, account access, returns, marketplace dispute referral), priority level (P1-P4), originating channel (phone, email, chat, social, self-service), assigned agent, current queue, escalation status, SLA policy reference, customer entitlement tier, first response timestamp, resolution timestamp, CSAT score, NPS score, and outcome disposition (resolved, unresolved, duplicate, transferred). Serves as the central operational record linking all service interactions, escalations, RMAs, refunds, and feedback for a single customer issue.'
}

Table service.case_interaction {
  case_interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the case interaction record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Interaction logs are aggregated by account to compute enterprise support metrics and SLA compliance per account.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Channel-level interaction analytics (cost-per-interaction, handle time by channel) are standard e-commerce service KPIs. Each interaction occurs on a specific channel; `channel` plain string is a deno']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Case interactions (calls, chats, emails) are conducted with a specific contact person in B2B e-commerce. Linking interaction to contact enables contact-level service history, communication preference ']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer involved in this interaction. Links to customer master entity.']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Interaction logs need the transaction context to surface correct communication history in support dashboards.']
  primary_case_agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer service agent who handled this interaction. Links to employee or agent entity.']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent support case that this interaction belongs to. Links to the service case entity.']
  attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of file attachments associated with this interaction (screenshots, documents, logs).']
  attachment_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of attachment identifiers or URIs pointing to files stored in external document management system.']
  contact_method varchar [note: 'The specific contact method used for this interaction (phone number, email address, chat handle, social media handle).']
  content_summary varchar [note: 'Summarized content or transcript of the interaction, capturing key points discussed without full verbatim text.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country from which the interaction originated (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interaction record was first created in the system in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  csat_score varchar [note: 'Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score provided by the customer for this specific interaction, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Direction of the interaction: inbound (customer-initiated) or outbound (agent-initiated).']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the interaction in seconds, applicable for calls and chat sessions.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this interaction resulted in an escalation to a higher support tier or management (True/False).']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or justification for escalating the interaction, if escalation occurred.']
  external_interaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this interaction in the external source system, used for cross-system reconciliation and traceability.']
  feedback_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text feedback or comment provided by the customer about this interaction.']
  full_transcript_reference varchar [note: 'Reference pointer or URI to the full transcript or recording stored in external system (e.g., CDN (Content Delivery Network), document management system).']
  interaction_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the interaction, typically formatted as INT- followed by numeric sequence.']
  interaction_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of this interaction within the parent case lifecycle, starting from 1.']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the interaction: completed, pending follow-up, escalated to higher tier, abandoned by customer, or transferred to another agent.']
  interaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the interaction event occurred in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'The type or category of interaction event: call, email, chat, SMS, social media, or web form submission.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or comments added by the agent for internal reference, not visible to the customer.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code representing the language used in the interaction (e.g., ENG for English, SPA for Spanish).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interaction record was last modified or updated in the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) provided by the customer for this interaction, typically on a scale of 0-10.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome or result of the interaction: resolved, unresolved, escalated, referred to another department, information provided, or callback scheduled.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this interaction: low, medium, high, or critical.']
  resolution_provided_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a resolution or answer was provided during this interaction (True/False).']
  response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Time in seconds taken by the agent to provide the first substantive response after interaction initiation.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Automated sentiment analysis score for the interaction, typically ranging from -1.00 (negative) to +1.00 (positive), derived from NLP (Natural Language Processing) analysis of interaction content.']
  sla_breach_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or explanation for SLA (Service Level Agreement) breach, if the interaction did not meet SLA targets.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this interaction met the defined SLA (Service Level Agreement) targets for response and resolution time (True/False).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the source system that captured this interaction (e.g., CRM (Customer Relationship Management), call center platform, chat system).']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief subject line or title summarizing the purpose or topic of the interaction.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of tags or labels applied to categorize or classify the interaction (e.g., billing, technical, complaint, refund).']
  transfer_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the interaction was transferred to another agent or department (True/False).']
  wait_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Time in seconds the customer waited before the interaction was answered or acknowledged by an agent.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every individual interaction or communication event within a support case lifecycle. Tracks inbound and outbound messages, call logs, chat transcripts, email threads, internal notes, and agent responses. Stores interaction channel, direction (inbound/outbound), agent ID, timestamp, content summary, attachment references, and interaction outcome. Enables full audit trail of case communications and supports agent performance measurement.'
}

Table service.rma {
  rma_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the return merchandise authorization record. Primary key for the RMA entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: RMA processing often needs the customer\'s account to validate credit limits and apply account‑level return policies.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: RMA approval triggers an AR credit memo against the original invoice. E-commerce returns processing requires linking the RMA to the AR record to apply credits, update aging buckets, and reconcile open']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Return processing reports require linking each RMA to the carrier handling the return; replaces the free‑text return_carrier field.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_service. Business justification: Return RMA tracking includes the specific carrier service tier used, enabling cost and SLA analysis for returned shipments.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: In B2B e-commerce, returns are authorized and coordinated by a specific contact (procurement or operations contact). Linking RMA to contact enables return authorization tracking, contact-level return ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Return processing costs are allocated to cost centers for internal budgeting and expense tracking.']
  delivery_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery_confirmation. Business justification: RMA return window eligibility is calculated from confirmed delivery date; linking RMA to delivery_confirmation enables automated return eligibility validation, proof-of-delivery dispute resolution, an']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: RMA processing requires the original fulfillment order to reconcile inventory, validate return eligibility, and generate accurate financial adjustments.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_shipment. Business justification: Returns management requires validating the original shipment\'s actual delivery date to confirm return window eligibility and generate return labels. E-commerce ops teams link every RMA to its originat']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: RMA processing generates journal entries for inventory write-backs, restocking fees, and refund liabilities. Linking rma to journal_entry enables financial impact tracking of returns, supports invento']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: RMAs are initiated against marketplace transactions in e-commerce. Seller settlement deduction, commission reversal, and dispute resolution all require tracing which marketplace transaction generated ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability of return handling is measured by linking each RMA to a profit center.']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.promotional_campaign. Business justification: Items purchased under a promotional campaign often have special return policies (e.g., final sale, reduced return window, non-refundable). Linking RMA to promotional_campaign enables return eligibilit']
  return_from_customer_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_address. Business justification: Return label generation and carrier pickup scheduling require the customer\'s return-from address. The rma table has no address FK currently. Role-prefixed return_from_ distinguishes pickup address fro']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Marketplace RMAs for seller-fulfilled orders must reference the responsible seller for return routing, seller-funded refund chargebacks, and violation tracking. Returns management and seller performan']
  tax_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_record. Business justification: Product returns require tax reversals on the original transaction. E-commerce tax compliance (marketplace facilitator laws) requires RMAs to reference the originating tax record to process accurate ta']
  warehouse_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse facility designated to receive and process the returned item. Used for routing and capacity planning.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this RMA requires manual approval by customer service or management. Triggered by policy exceptions or high-value returns.']
  approved_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service representative or manager who approved the RMA request. Used for audit trail and performance tracking.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the RMA request was approved by the system or customer service. Marks the start of the return fulfillment process.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the RMA case was fully closed and all associated workflows completed. Marks the end of the return lifecycle for reporting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this RMA record. Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency operations.']
  customer_declared_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the returned item as declared by the customer at the time of RMA request. Used for initial eligibility assessment and compared against inspection findings.']
  customer_service_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by customer service representatives regarding the RMA case. Captures context, customer interactions, and special handling instructions.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision on how the returned item will be handled post-inspection. Drives inventory allocation, financial write-offs, and reverse logistics.']
  expected_return_date date [note: 'Estimated date by which the returned item is expected to arrive at the warehouse. Used for receiving planning and exception alerts.']
  inspected_condition varchar [note: 'Actual condition of the returned item as determined by warehouse inspection team. Used to validate customer declaration and determine disposition.']
  inspection_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the warehouse inspection of the returned item was completed. Triggers disposition decision and refund processing.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by warehouse inspection team regarding the physical condition and findings of the returned item.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the physical inspection process for the returned item. Tracks warehouse receiving and quality control workflow.']
  is_within_policy boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the RMA request complies with the applicable return policy rules. Used for auto-approval workflows and exception handling.']
  label_issued_date date [note: 'Date when the return shipping label was generated and provided to the customer. Used for tracking customer action and carrier pickup scheduling.']
  label_paid_by varchar [note: 'Party responsible for paying the return shipping cost. Determines financial settlement and customer experience.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  received_date date [note: 'Actual date when the returned item was physically received at the warehouse. Triggers inspection workflow and service level measurement.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount to be refunded to the customer in the transaction currency. May differ from original order amount due to restocking fees or partial returns.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the refund will be issued to the customer. Drives payment processing workflow and customer communication.']
  refund_processed_date date [note: 'Date when the refund transaction was successfully processed and funds were released to the customer. Used for financial reconciliation and customer satisfaction tracking.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the refund transaction. Tracks financial settlement workflow and customer notification triggers.']
  requested_date date [note: 'Date when the customer initiated the RMA request. Used for return window calculation and service level tracking.']
  restocking_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for processing the return, deducted from the refund amount. Applied per return policy and product category.']
  return_label_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost of the return shipping label in the transaction currency. Used for return cost analysis and customer charge-back decisions.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the return. Used for root cause analysis, product quality tracking, and return rate analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defective|wrong_item|not_as_described|damaged_in_transit|no_longer_needed|size_fit_issue|buyer_remorse|duplicate_order|late_delivery|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for the return shipment. Enables real-time visibility into return transit status.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return resolution method requested by the customer or determined by policy. Drives downstream financial and fulfillment workflows.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from original order delivery date within which the return must be initiated per policy. Used for eligibility validation.']
  rma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RMA. Tracks the return authorization workflow from initial request through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_approval|approved|rejected|label_issued|in_transit|received|inspecting|completed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Return Merchandise Authorization master record managing the end-to-end RMA lifecycle for e-commerce returns. Captures RMA number, originating order reference, return reason code, return type (refund, exchange, store credit), customer-declared condition, carrier return label details, expected return window, actual receipt date, inspection status, disposition decision (restock, liquidate, destroy), and financial settlement status. SSOT for all return authorization records across the platform.'
}

Table service.rma_line {
  rma_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RMA line item. Primary key for the RMA line detail record.']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Bundle returns require component-level disposition rules and restocking eligibility checks defined on the bundle. Existing rma_line FKs cover catalog_item and sku but not bundle, leaving bundle return']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product master record for the item being returned. Links to the product catalog.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the new order created for the exchange shipment, if applicable. Links the return to the replacement order.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Return eligibility validation, return_window_days enforcement, and unit_price reconciliation require tracing each RMA line back to the specific order line. E-commerce return processing and seller char']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order_line. Business justification: Each RMA line must trace back to the original fulfillment order line for refund amount validation, inventory restocking disposition, and return fraud detection. E-commerce returns processing always re']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: RMA line processing requires the original price_list_item to validate unit_price for refund computation, determine restocking fee eligibility based on pricing tier, and confirm return window policy. R']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent RMA authorization under which this line item is processed. Links this line to the RMA header.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Needed for accurate inventory and refund calculations; RMA line must reference the SKU entity to determine restocking fees, unit price, and stock adjustments.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: After RMA inspection, disposition (restock, quarantine, scrap) maps to a specific stock_position. rma_line has restocking_eligible_flag and disposition_code but no FK to stock_position. Returns proces']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific warehouse location where the returned item was received and inspected. Supports multi-warehouse return processing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RMA line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line. Ensures proper currency handling for international returns.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'The final disposition decision for the returned item. Determines how the item will be handled after inspection and processing.']
  exchange_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has requested an exchange rather than a refund for this item. True if exchange requested, false if refund only.']
  exchange_sku varchar [note: 'The SKU of the replacement product requested by the customer if this is an exchange. Null if this is a refund-only return.']
  inspected_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse staff member who performed the inspection of this returned item. Supports quality control and accountability.']
  inspected_date date [note: 'The date the returned item completed quality inspection. Marks the completion of the inspection process and readiness for disposition.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes recorded by warehouse inspection staff regarding the condition, completeness, and quality of the returned item. Supports disposition decisions.']
  item_condition_code varchar [note: 'Standardized assessment of the physical condition of the returned item upon receipt at the warehouse. Determines restocking eligibility and disposition.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of this RMA line item. Tracks the line through the return workflow from initial request to final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PENDING_RECEIPT|RECEIVED|INSPECTING|APPROVED|REJECTED|REFUNDED|EXCHANGED|CLOSED — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  line_subtotal decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total amount for this line before taxes and fees, calculated as unit price multiplied by quantity approved. Represents the base refund amount.']
  line_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The tax amount to be refunded for this line item. Calculated based on the approved quantity and applicable tax rates.']
  quantity_approved varchar [note: 'The number of units approved for refund or exchange after inspection. May be less than quantity received if some units fail inspection criteria.']
  quantity_received varchar [note: 'The actual number of units physically received back from the customer for this SKU. May differ from quantity requested if customer sends partial return.']
  quantity_requested varchar [note: 'The number of units the customer has requested to return for this SKU. Represents the customer\'s initial return request quantity.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date the returned item was physically received at the warehouse. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking and refund processing timelines.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total refund amount for this line item including subtotal, tax, minus any restocking fees. Represents the actual amount to be credited to the customer.']
  refund_processed_date date [note: 'The date the refund for this line item was processed and initiated to the customer\'s payment method. Marks completion of the financial return transaction.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this line item was rejected for refund or exchange, if applicable. Provides transparency to customer and supports dispute resolution.']
  restocking_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned item is in suitable condition to be restocked and resold as new inventory. True if eligible for resale, false if must be liquidated or disposed.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The restocking fee charged to the customer for this line item, if applicable. Typically applied for non-defective returns or opened items per return policy.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the customer is returning this specific item. Used for return analytics and quality improvement.']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free-text explanation provided by the customer or service agent describing the specific reason for returning this item.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial number of the specific unit being returned, if applicable for serialized products. Enables unit-level tracking and warranty validation.']
  unit_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'The original unit price paid by the customer for this SKU at the time of purchase. Used to calculate refund amounts.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RMA line record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the record.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail record for each SKU included in an RMA. Captures the specific product SKU, quantity requested for return, quantity actually received, unit return reason, item condition on receipt, restocking eligibility flag, refund amount per line, exchange SKU if applicable, and inspection notes. Supports multi-item returns under a single RMA authorization and enables SKU-level return analytics and inventory disposition.'
}

Table service.service_refund {
  service_refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service refund record. Primary key for the service refund entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account. Business justification: Refunds must be associated with the billing account for financial reconciliation and audit reporting.']
  accounts_receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Refund issuance directly reduces or offsets an AR balance. E-commerce financial reconciliation requires linking each service refund to the originating AR record to apply credit memos, update open amou']
  coupon_redemption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.coupon_redemption. Business justification: When a refund reverses a coupon discount, the service agent must reference the specific coupon_redemption record to determine the discount amount to reverse and update redemption status. Coupon refund']
  general_ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Refund transactions must be posted to the general ledger for financial reconciliation and reporting.']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original order associated with this refund. Links the refund to the transaction being remediated.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Every refund disbursement generates a journal entry (debit revenue/liability, credit cash). Linking service_refund to journal_entry enables financial audit trail, SOX compliance, and period-close reco']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: In multi-entity e-commerce platforms, refunds must be processed and reported under the correct legal entity for regulatory compliance, VAT/GST reporting, and intercompany settlement. Domain experts ex']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Partial refunds on specific line items require direct line-level linkage for financial reconciliation, tax recalculation, and seller payout adjustments. Traversing via rma_id is insufficient for goodw']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Refunds issued for shipping failures (lost, damaged, late delivery) must reference the logistics_shipment to support carrier chargeback processing and cost-recovery workflows. service_refund has heade']
  loyalty_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.loyalty_enrollment. Business justification: service_refund.loyalty_points_granted requires knowing which loyalty enrollment to credit. Direct FK to loyalty_enrollment enables automated point posting for refund compensation — a standard loyalty ']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Refunds are processed against the original marketplace transaction; linking provides auditability and financial reconciliation across systems.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: Enables audit of refunds by payment method, supporting compliance reports and method‑level performance metrics.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list. Business justification: Needed for Refund Pricing Source Audit, tracking which price list was used for the original sale to ensure correct refund calculations and compliance.']
  price_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_list_item. Business justification: Refund calculation validation requires referencing the exact price_list_item applied to the original sale to confirm the unit price, tier pricing, and discount percent used. E-commerce refund audits a']
  price_override_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.price_override. Business justification: Refunds on orders with price overrides must reference the override record to validate the overridden price used and ensure the refund amount matches the actual amount charged. Price override audit tra']
  primary_service_issuing_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent who initiated the refund request. Links to the employee or agent record.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RMA that initiated this refund or compensation. Links the refund to the originating return case.']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Marketplace refunds are often charged back to the responsible seller (seller-funded refunds). Finance and seller operations teams require seller attribution on refund records for chargeback processing']
  seller_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.seller_settlement. Business justification: Refunds issued on marketplace transactions must be deducted from the seller\'s settlement disbursement. Finance and marketplace ops require this link for seller payout reconciliation, net disbursement ']
  settlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.settlement. Business justification: Finance teams require direct linkage between service-initiated refunds and payment settlement batches for period-end reconciliation and GL posting. settlement_reference_number is a plain-text denormal']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service case that authorized this refund or compensation. Links to the support ticket or inquiry.']
  tax_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.tax_record. Business justification: Refunds require tax reversals or credit adjustments against the original tax record. E-commerce marketplace facilitator tax compliance mandates linking refund transactions to their originating tax rec']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the refund or compensation issued to the customer. Represents the gross amount before any adjustments.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Authorization state of the refund request. Indicates whether the refund has been approved and by what authority level.']
  approval_tier varchar [note: 'Authorization level required for this refund based on amount thresholds. Defines the approval hierarchy tier.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was authorized by the agent or manager. Null if still pending approval or if rejected.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was successfully settled and funds were returned to the customer. Marks the end of the refund lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this refund record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the refund amount. Defines the monetary unit of the refund value.']
  customer_satisfaction_impact varchar [note: 'Assessed impact of the refund on customer satisfaction based on follow-up surveys or agent assessment. Used for service quality tracking.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the compensation instrument (store credit, gift card, coupon) can no longer be redeemed. Null for direct refunds with no expiration.']
  is_goodwill_gesture boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund was issued as a proactive goodwill gesture rather than in response to a specific complaint or return. True for retention offers.']
  is_retention_offer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the compensation was issued as part of a customer retention strategy to prevent churn. True for proactive retention gestures.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this refund record was most recently updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  loyalty_points_granted varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points awarded as compensation. Zero if compensation was not in the form of loyalty points.']
  manager_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the refund required manager override of standard policy or amount thresholds. True if exception was granted.']
  net_refund_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost to the business including refund amount and processing fees. Used for financial impact analysis and reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments from service agents regarding the refund. Used for context and audit trail documentation.']
  nps_survey_response varchar [note: 'Customer NPS score (0-10) collected after refund resolution. Null if no survey response was received. Used for service quality measurement.']
  original_payment_method_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment method used in the original order. Used to determine refund routing and processing requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|bank_transfer|gift_card|store_credit|cash_on_delivery|buy_now_pay_later — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  override_justification varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the approving manager for policy override or exception. Required when manager_override_flag is true.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was submitted to the payment system for execution. Marks handoff from service to payment domain.']
  processing_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged by payment processor or financial institution for processing the refund transaction. Represents cost to business.']
  reason_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of why the refund or compensation was issued. Used for root cause analysis and service improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defective_product|wrong_item_shipped|damaged_in_transit|late_delivery|service_failure|customer_dissatisfaction|pricing_error|goodwill_gesture|retention_offer|policy_exception — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_detail varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the specific reason for issuing the refund or compensation. Free-text field for agent notes.']
  redemption_code varchar [note: 'Unique code issued for store credit, gift card, or coupon compensation. Used by customer to claim the value. Confidential business data.']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Tracks whether non-monetary compensation (store credit, gift card, coupon) has been used by the customer. Not applicable for direct refunds.']
  redemption_value_remaining decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unredeemed value for partial-use compensation instruments like store credit or gift cards. Null for one-time refunds.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the refund or compensation is delivered to the customer. Defines the channel for value return. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: original_payment_method|store_credit|gift_card|bank_transfer|check|loyalty_account|promotional_code — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique business identifier for the refund. Used in customer communications and tracking.']
  remediation_type varchar [note: 'Category of customer remediation issued. Defines the form of compensation provided to the customer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: full_refund|partial_refund|store_credit|gift_card|coupon_code|free_shipping_voucher|loyalty_points|goodwill_compensation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  requested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was initially requested by the customer or initiated by the service agent. Marks the start of the refund lifecycle.']
  service_refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the refund. Tracks the refund through authorization, processing, and completion stages. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|processing|completed|failed|redeemed|expired|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system or channel where the refund request was initiated. Used for data lineage and integration tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crm|oms|rma_system|service_portal|call_center|chat_system|email_system — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing all customer remediation, refunds, and compensation issued through the service domain. Covers full refunds, partial refunds, store credits, gift cards, coupon codes, free shipping vouchers, loyalty point grants, and goodwill compensation for service failures or proactive retention gestures. Tracks remediation type (refund, exchange credit, compensation, goodwill gesture, retention offer), amount/value, currency, method (original payment method, store credit, gift card), status lifecycle (pending, approved, processing, completed, failed, redeemed, expired), initiating RMA or case reference, approval workflow with authorization thresholds by amount tier, issuing agent, manager override flag, redemption status, expiry date, compensation reason category, and settlement confirmation reference. Owns the service-side authorization decision while payment domain owns financial execution. SSOT for all service-originated customer remediation and compensation records.'
}

Table service.agent {
  agent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer service agent. Primary key for the agent entity.']
  supervisor_agent_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.agent']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current employment and operational status of the agent. Determines whether the agent is eligible for case assignment.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current real-time availability status of the agent. Used by the case routing engine to determine if the agent can accept new cases.']
  average_handle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rolling average time in minutes the agent takes to resolve a case from assignment to closure. Key performance indicator for agent efficiency.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the agents current service certification expires and requires renewal. Null if certification does not expire.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agent record was first created in the service domain system. Audit field for data lineage.']
  customer_satisfaction_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average customer satisfaction score for cases handled by this agent, typically on a 1-5 scale. Derived from post-interaction surveys.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Corporate email address for the agent. Used for internal communication and system notifications.']
  employee_code varchar [note: 'External employee identifier from the HR system. Links the agent to their employee record in the Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system.']
  escalation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of cases handled by the agent that required escalation to a supervisor or specialist. Lower rates indicate higher agent competency.']
  first_contact_resolution_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of cases resolved by the agent on first contact without escalation or follow-up. Key quality metric for agent performance.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the customer service agent as recorded in the HR system.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the agent was hired into the customer service organization. Used for tenure analysis and performance benchmarking.']
  language_proficiencies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of languages the agent can support with proficiency level (e.g., EN-native, ES-fluent, FR-conversational). Enables multilingual customer support routing.']
  last_login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the agents most recent login to the customer service platform. Used for activity monitoring and availability tracking.']
  max_concurrent_cases varchar [note: 'Maximum number of cases the agent can handle simultaneously. Used by the workload balancing engine to prevent agent overload.']
  net_promoter_score decimal(4,2) [note: 'Average Net Promoter Score (NPS) for interactions handled by this agent. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend based on agent service.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Agent performance classification tier based on experience, skill level, and performance metrics. Used for case complexity routing and escalation paths.']
  phone_extension varchar [note: 'Internal phone extension number for the agent within the contact center phone system.']
  quality_assurance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average quality assurance audit score for the agent based on case review and compliance checks. Typically scored 0-100 by QA team.']
  shift_schedule_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the agents current shift schedule or roster. Links to workforce management system for capacity planning.']
  skill_set_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of agent competencies and specializations (e.g., returns, billing, technical, escalations, fraud, international). Used for intelligent case routing and workload balancing.']
  sla_compliance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of cases where the agent met or exceeded the defined Service Level Agreement (SLA) response and resolution time targets.']
  supported_channels varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of customer service channels the agent is trained and authorized to handle (e.g., phone, email, chat, social media, SMS).']
  team_assignment varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the service team to which the agent is assigned (e.g., Returns Team, Billing Support, Technical Support, Escalations).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the agent left the organization or was terminated. Null for active agents. Used for historical case ownership tracking.']
  total_cases_handled varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all cases assigned to and handled by the agent since hire date. Used for productivity and experience analysis.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date the agent completed initial onboarding and training program and was certified to handle customer cases independently.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the agent record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']

  Note: 'Master record for customer service agents and support staff operating within the service domain. Captures agent name, employee ID reference, team assignment, skill set tags (returns, billing, technical, escalations), supported channels, language proficiencies, current availability status, shift schedule reference, performance tier, and active/inactive status. Serves as the SSOT for agent identity within the service domain and supports case routing, workload balancing, and performance measurement.'
}

Table service.team {
  team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer service team. Primary key.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or manager who leads this team. Used for escalation paths and team performance accountability.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finance cost center to which this team\'s expenses are allocated. Enables financial reporting and budget tracking.']
  parent_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent team in a hierarchical team structure. Null for top-level teams. Enables organizational hierarchy and escalation path modeling.']
  primary_escalation_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the team to which cases are escalated when this team cannot resolve them. Null if this is the highest escalation tier. Enables escalation path configuration.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Service teams are P&L-accountable units in e-commerce operations. Linking team to profit_center enables revenue and cost attribution per service team for management reporting, budgeting, and profitabi']
  capacity_fte decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total team capacity expressed in Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) headcount. Used for workforce planning, capacity forecasting, and case load balancing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the team record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  csat_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score for the team, expressed as a percentage (0-100). Key performance indicator (KPI) for customer experience quality.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the team became operational and started accepting cases. Used for team lifecycle tracking and historical reporting.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the team, used for case assignment notifications and internal communication. Organizational contact data, classified as confidential.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market this team serves, using 3-letter ISO country codes or region identifiers (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR, EUR, APAC, GLOBAL). Supports geo-based routing and regional SLA (Service Level Agreement) management.']
  is_remote boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the team operates remotely (distributed workforce) or from a centralized physical location. True if remote, False if on-site.']
  language_support varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of languages the team can support using ISO 639-1 two-letter codes (e.g., en, es, fr, de, zh, ja). Enables language-based case routing.']
  max_concurrent_cases varchar [note: 'Maximum number of cases the team can handle concurrently based on capacity and workload models. Used for case routing throttling and queue management.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the team record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or context about the team (e.g., special operating procedures, temporary capacity changes, pilot program details).']
  nps_target varchar [note: 'Target Net Promoter Score (NPS) for the team, ranging from -100 to +100. Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend based on team interactions.']
  operates_holidays boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the team operates on public holidays. True if holiday coverage is provided, False if holidays are non-operational.']
  operates_weekends boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the team operates on weekends (Saturday and Sunday). True if weekend coverage is provided, False if weekends are non-operational.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Daily end time of the team\'s operating hours in HH:mm format (24-hour clock, team\'s local timezone). Used for case routing and availability scheduling.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Daily start time of the team\'s operating hours in HH:mm format (24-hour clock, team\'s local timezone). Used for case routing and availability scheduling.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the team, used for internal escalations and coordination. Organizational contact data, classified as confidential.']
  physical_location varchar [note: 'Physical office or site location where the team is based (e.g., New York HQ, Manila Contact Center, Remote). Used for workforce planning and facility management.']
  primary_function varchar [note: 'Brief description of the team\'s primary business function and scope of responsibility (e.g., Order inquiries and tracking, Returns and refunds processing, Account and billing support, Technical troubleshooting).']
  quality_score_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target quality assurance score for the team, expressed as a percentage (0-100). Represents the expected standard for case handling quality, monitored through QA audits.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'The SLA tier this team is assigned to deliver: standard (baseline service levels), priority (faster response for high-value segments), premium (enhanced service commitments), vip (white-glove service for top customers). Drives case routing and performance targets.']
  specialization_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specialization keywords or skill tags that describe the team\'s expertise (e.g., rma, fraud_detection, vip_service, technical_support, billing_disputes). Used for intelligent case routing based on issue type.']
  target_first_response_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for the team to provide the first response to a customer inquiry, as defined by the team\'s SLA tier. Key performance indicator (KPI) for responsiveness.']
  target_resolution_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target time in hours for the team to resolve a case, as defined by the team\'s SLA tier. Key performance indicator (KPI) for case closure efficiency.']
  team_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the team for operational systems and reporting (e.g., T1-FRONT, T2-ESC, RMA-SPEC).']
  team_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the customer service team (e.g., Tier-1 Frontline Support, Returns Specialist Team, VIP Customer Care).']
  team_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the team: active (currently operational and accepting cases), inactive (temporarily not operational), suspended (on hold pending review), dissolved (permanently closed).']
  team_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the team based on its role in the service organization: tier_1_frontline (first contact resolution), tier_2_escalation (complex issue handling), specialist (domain-specific expertise such as RMA or fraud), back_office (non-customer-facing support), quality_assurance (monitoring and coaching), training (onboarding and skill development).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the team was dissolved or permanently closed. Null for active teams. Used for team lifecycle tracking and historical analysis.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the team\'s operating location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Tokyo). Used to calculate local operating hours and SLA deadlines.']

  Note: 'Master record defining customer service teams and organizational groupings within the support operation. Captures team name, team type (tier-1 frontline, tier-2 escalation, specialist, back-office), supported contact channels, operating hours, geographic coverage, team lead reference, SLA tier assignment, and capacity targets. Enables hierarchical case routing, team-level SLA tracking, and workforce planning for the service organization.'
}

Table service.sla_policy {
  sla_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SLA policy record. Primary key.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: SLA policies in e-commerce are channel-specific (social media: 1-hour response; email: 24-hour). Linking sla_policy to marketing.channel enables proper policy lookup by channel and channel-level SLA c']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: SLA policies carry breach_penalty_amount and breach_penalty_currency. Linking to cost_center enables allocation of SLA breach penalty costs to the responsible cost center, supporting operational cost ']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: sla_policy.customer_segment is a denormalized text field. SLA policies are defined per customer segment (VIP, B2B, standard). Proper FK to segment enables automated SLA assignment during case creation']
  superseded_by_policy_sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer SLA policy that replaces this one. Nullable for current active policies. Creates version chain for policy evolution tracking.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the SLA policy. Policies must be approved before becoming active. Supports governance and change control processes.']
  approved_by_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved this SLA policy. Typically a senior operations leader or executive. Nullable for unapproved policies.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA policy was approved. Nullable for unapproved policies. Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  auto_escalation_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automatic escalation is enabled when escalation_threshold_hours is reached. True enables automated escalation workflow, False requires manual escalation.']
  breach_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of penalty or credit applied when SLA is breached. Applicable when breach_penalty_type is financial or credit. Stored in base currency.']
  breach_penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the breach penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  breach_penalty_type varchar [note: 'Type of penalty or action triggered when SLA is breached. Options include no penalty, financial penalty, customer credit, automatic escalation, or notification to management.']
  business_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of business days when SLA is active (e.g., \'Monday,Tuesday,Wednesday,Thursday,Friday\'). Used when time_calculation_method is business_hours to exclude weekends or specific days.']
  business_hours_end varchar [note: 'End time of business hours in HH:MM format (24-hour clock) when time_calculation_method is business_hours. Used to calculate SLA compliance during operational hours only.']
  business_hours_start varchar [note: 'Start time of business hours in HH:MM format (24-hour clock) when time_calculation_method is business_hours. Used to calculate SLA compliance during operational hours only.']
  case_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of cases this SLA policy applies to. Used in conjunction with case_type to form the SLA matrix (e.g., High priority order inquiries have different SLA targets than Low priority inquiries).']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Type of customer service case this SLA policy applies to (e.g., \'Order Inquiry\', \'Product Return\', \'Technical Issue\', \'Billing Dispute\', \'Account Management\'). Defines the scope of cases governed by this policy.']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SLA policy is driven by regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., GDPR data subject request response times, financial services complaint handling). True indicates regulatory mandate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA policy record was first created in the system. Part of standard audit trail.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this SLA policy expires or is superseded by a new version. Nullable for currently active policies with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this SLA policy becomes active and begins governing case handling. Part of the effective date range for policy versioning.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric escalation tier this SLA policy triggers (e.g., 1 = supervisor, 2 = manager, 3 = director). Defines the organizational level to which cases are escalated when thresholds are breached.']
  escalation_threshold_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time threshold in hours that triggers automatic escalation if the case is not resolved. Typically set before the resolution target to allow time for escalated handling.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Description of cases or conditions excluded from this SLA policy (e.g., \'Excludes cases opened during system maintenance windows\', \'Does not apply to third-party vendor issues\'). Defines policy boundaries.']
  first_response_target_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target time in hours for the first response to a customer case under this SLA policy. Represents the maximum time allowed before initial acknowledgment or response to the customer.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market this SLA policy applies to (e.g., \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\'). Supports region-specific service level commitments.']
  is_default_policy boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default SLA policy applied when no specific policy matches a case. Only one policy per case_type should be marked as default.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA policy record was last updated. Part of standard audit trail for change tracking.']
  notification_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automated notifications are sent when SLA thresholds are approaching or breached. True enables notification workflow.']
  notification_threshold_percent varchar [note: 'Percentage of SLA target time elapsed that triggers a warning notification (e.g., 80 means notify when 80% of resolution_target_hours has passed). Used for proactive SLA management.']
  owner_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business user or manager responsible for defining and maintaining this SLA policy. Typically a customer service director or operations manager.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the SLA policy used in operational systems and reporting.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the SLA policy (e.g., \'Premium Support SLA\', \'Standard Response Policy\').']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA policy indicating the service area it governs (customer support, technical support, returns processing, escalation handling, VIP service, standard service).']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory framework or law that mandates this SLA policy (e.g., \'GDPR\', \'CCPA\', \'FTC Consumer Protection\', \'PCI DSS\'). Populated when compliance_required is True.']
  resolution_target_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target time in hours for full resolution of a customer case under this SLA policy. Represents the maximum time allowed to completely resolve and close the case.']
  sla_policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the SLA policy including its purpose, scope, and any special conditions or exceptions. Provides context for policy application and interpretation.']
  sla_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA policy indicating whether it is actively enforced, inactive, in draft state, archived, or temporarily suspended.']
  time_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate SLA time targets. Business hours counts only operational hours (e.g., 9am-5pm weekdays), while calendar hours counts all hours including nights and weekends.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this SLA policy. Incremented when policy terms are updated. Supports policy change tracking and historical analysis.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining SLA (Service Level Agreement) policies governing response and resolution time targets for customer support cases. Captures SLA policy name, applicable case type and priority matrix, first response time target (hours), resolution time target (hours), escalation trigger thresholds, business hours vs. calendar hours flag, breach penalty rules, and effective date range. Provides the authoritative SLA configuration used to evaluate case compliance and drive automated escalations.'
}

Table service.escalation {
  escalation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the escalation event. Primary key.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Channel-level escalation rate reporting is a core e-commerce service metric; escalations may occur on a different channel than the originating case. `channel` plain string is a denormalization of mark']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Escalations in B2B e-commerce are initiated by or directed to a specific contact. Contact-level escalation history drives SLA accountability reporting, executive escalation routing, and contact satisf']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.dispute. Business justification: Marketplace disputes that exceed standard resolution thresholds trigger service escalations. Escalation management and SLA compliance reporting require direct traceability from the escalation record t']
  escalation_escalating_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the support agent who initiated or triggered the escalation.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_shipment. Business justification: Escalations triggered by shipment failures (SLA breach, lost package, repeated delivery failures) require direct reference to the shipment for carrier chargeback filing and root-cause analysis. Escala']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.header. Business justification: Escalations triggered by order fulfillment failures (late delivery, wrong item, fraud) require direct order linkage for SLA breach root-cause analysis, seller performance scorecards, and customer comp']
  receiving_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the support agent who received and accepted the escalated case.']
  receiving_team_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the support team or queue to which the case was escalated.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: escalation.customer_segment is a denormalized text field. Proper FK to segment enables segment-level escalation rate reporting, SLA breach analysis by customer tier, and escalation routing rules by se']
  seller_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to seller.seller_profile. Business justification: Marketplace escalations are frequently triggered by seller behavior (late shipment, item not as described, policy violations). Seller performance management, scorecard updates, and violation workflows']
  sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.sla_policy. Business justification: escalation tracks sla_breach_flag and sla_target_resolution_minutes but has no FK to the governing SLA policy. Adding sla_policy_id → service.sla_policy.sla_policy_id directly links each escalation ev']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating support case that was escalated.']
  compensation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of compensation issued to the customer as part of escalation resolution.']
  compensation_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether customer compensation (refund, credit, discount, gift) was issued as part of escalation resolution (True) or not (False).']
  compensation_type varchar [note: 'The type of compensation provided to the customer (e.g., refund, account credit, discount code, gift card, free shipping, product replacement).']
  cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual cost associated with handling the escalation, including labor, compensation, and operational overhead.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the escalation record was first created in the system, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post-resolution customer satisfaction score for the escalated case, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.']
  escalation_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the escalation event, formatted as ESC-NNNNNNNN.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the escalation event within the support workflow.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the escalation event occurred, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  escalation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the escalation mechanism: functional (to specialist team), hierarchical (to supervisor/manager), automatic (system-triggered), or manual (agent-initiated).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments provided by the escalating agent explaining the context, urgency, or special handling requirements for the escalation.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score collected after escalation resolution, measuring customer loyalty on a scale of 0-10.']
  priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to the escalation, indicating urgency and required response time.']
  reason varchar [note: 'The trigger or reason that caused the case to be escalated. Common reasons include SLA (Service Level Agreement) breach, customer demand, case complexity, VIP customer status, regulatory concern, or repeat issue.']
  resolution_at_escalated_tier_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the case was successfully resolved at the escalated tier (True) or required further escalation (False).']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating how the escalated case was resolved (e.g., refund issued, replacement shipped, technical fix applied).']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the escalated case was resolved, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'The identified root cause category for the issue that led to escalation, used for continuous improvement and pattern analysis.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the escalation was triggered by an SLA breach (True) or not (False).']
  sla_target_resolution_minutes varchar [note: 'The target resolution time in minutes as defined by the applicable SLA for the escalated case.']
  source_tier varchar [note: 'The support tier or level from which the case was escalated.']
  target_tier varchar [note: 'The support tier or level to which the case was escalated.']
  time_to_resolution_minutes varchar [note: 'The elapsed time in minutes from escalation timestamp to resolution timestamp, used for performance analysis and SLA compliance tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the escalation record was last modified, formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking case escalation events within the support workflow. Captures the originating case reference, escalation trigger reason (SLA breach, customer demand, complexity, VIP customer), escalation tier (tier-1 to tier-2, tier-2 to specialist, agent to supervisor), escalating agent, receiving agent or team, escalation timestamp, resolution at escalated tier flag, and time-to-resolution post-escalation. Supports escalation pattern analysis and service quality improvement.'
}

Table service.feedback_response {
  feedback_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for feedback_response']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Post-purchase NPS/CSAT surveys are frequently triggered by marketing campaigns in e-commerce. Linking feedback_response to the triggering campaign enables campaign-level satisfaction measurement, a cr']
  catalog_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.catalog_item. Business justification: Post-purchase NPS and CSAT surveys are triggered by specific product purchases. Linking feedback to catalog_item enables product quality scoring dashboards, PDP improvement decisions, and brand-level ']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.channel. Business justification: Post-purchase surveys are delivered via specific channels; channel-level survey response rate and NPS analysis are standard e-commerce CX metrics. `delivery_channel` plain string is a denormalization ']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who provided the feedback response. Links to the unified customer profile in the Customer Data Platform.']
  fulfillment_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_shipment. Business justification: Post-delivery CSAT/NPS surveys are triggered by shipment delivery events. Linking feedback_response to fulfillment_shipment enables delivery experience analytics — correlating satisfaction scores with']
  header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order associated with this feedback, if the survey was triggered by an order-related service interaction or post-delivery survey.']
  loyalty_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.loyalty_enrollment. Business justification: feedback_response.incentive_offered_flag indicates loyalty point incentives for survey completion. Direct FK to loyalty_enrollment enables automated point crediting for survey participation — a standa']
  marketplace_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketplace.marketplace_transaction. Business justification: Post-purchase feedback surveys are triggered by marketplace transactions. Seller performance dashboards, marketplace quality scoring, and FTC compliance reporting all require linking feedback response']
  primary_feedback_agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent who handled the case being evaluated. Used for agent performance tracking and coaching.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: feedback_response.customer_segment is a denormalized text field. Proper FK to segment enables segment-level NPS/CSAT reporting, regulatory consent-aware survey targeting, and CX performance benchmarki']
  support_case_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service case or support ticket that triggered this feedback survey. Links the feedback to the specific service interaction being evaluated.']
  case_category varchar [note: 'The category of the service case that triggered this feedback (e.g., order_inquiry, return_request, product_defect, delivery_issue, account_management, technical_support). Used to analyze satisfaction by case type and identify problem areas.']
  case_resolution_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total time in hours from case creation to case closure for the service case being evaluated. Captured at the time of survey to enable correlation analysis between resolution speed and customer satisfaction.']
  channel_of_service varchar [note: 'The channel through which the customer received service for the case being evaluated. Used to analyze satisfaction by service channel and optimize channel strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phone|email|chat|social_media|in-app|self-service|store — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  classification_category varchar [note: 'The categorical classification derived from the numeric score. For NPS: promoter (9-10), passive (7-8), detractor (0-6). For CSAT: satisfied (4-5), neutral (3), dissatisfied (1-2). For CES: low_effort (6-7), high_effort (1-3). Used for segmentation and aggregate metrics calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promoter|passive|detractor|satisfied|neutral|dissatisfied|low_effort|high_effort — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contact_reason varchar [note: 'The specific reason the customer contacted support, as classified by the service team. More granular than case_category, capturing the detailed nature of the inquiry or issue (e.g., damaged_item, late_delivery, wrong_item_shipped, refund_status, promo_code_issue).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this feedback response record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and tracking data freshness.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the service case was escalated to a supervisor or specialist team. Escalated cases typically have different satisfaction patterns and require special analysis.']
  external_survey_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this survey response in the external survey platform or CRM system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and to link back to the source system for detailed survey metadata.']
  first_contact_resolution_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer\'s issue was resolved on the first contact without requiring follow-up interactions. Key performance indicator (KPI) for service efficiency and a strong predictor of customer satisfaction.']
  follow_up_action_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this feedback response requires follow-up action from the service team. Typically set to true for detractor responses, low CSAT scores, or when verbatim feedback indicates unresolved issues. Triggers service recovery workflows.']
  follow_up_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the required follow-up action has been completed. Used to track closure of service recovery loops and ensure no customer feedback is left unaddressed.']
